```text
1	//! Wire protocol between `chess-net-server` and clients.
2	//!
3	//! Frames are JSON text frames, one message per frame. Tagged enums via
4	//! `#[serde(tag = "type")]` keep the schema obvious in `wscat`/`jq`.
5	//!
6	//! All payload types come straight from `chess-core` and already derive
7	//! `Serialize/Deserialize`, including the proptest-validated no-leak
8	//! projection in `PlayerView` (hidden banqi pieces stay opaque on the wire).
9	
10	use chess_core::moves::Move;
11	use chess_core::piece::Side;
12	use chess_core::rules::{RuleSet, Variant};
13	use chess_core::view::PlayerView;
14	use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
15	
16	/// Bumped whenever the wire schema changes incompatibly.
17	///
18	/// v1 → v2: added `ServerMsg::Rooms` and `ClientMsg::ListRooms` for the
19	/// multi-room lobby. Old (v1) clients still work against a v2 server in
20	/// the default room "main" — the new message variants are additive.
21	///
22	/// v2 → v3: added `ServerMsg::{Spectating, ChatHistory, Chat}` and
23	/// `ClientMsg::Chat` for read-only spectators + in-room chat. v2 clients
24	/// keep working as players (they never request spectator role; the new
25	/// chat variants are additive). `RoomSummary` gains a `spectators` field
26	/// with `#[serde(default)]` so v2 lobby clients deserializing a v3 server
27	/// snapshot ignore the new column.
28	///
29	/// v3 → v4: added `PlayerView.in_check: bool` so clients can render a
30	/// "將軍 / CHECK" warning without having to recompute it from the board.
31	/// Wire-compatible — `#[serde(default)]` on the new field means a v3
32	/// client deserializing a v4 message reads `in_check = false` and a v4
33	/// client deserializing a v3 message gets the same default. The bump is
34	/// for documentation, not enforcement; the handshake still uses lenient
35	/// equality. See ADR-0007.
36	///
37	/// v4 → v5: added `hints_allowed: bool` to `RoomSummary`, `Hello`, and
38	/// `Spectating` so clients can decide whether to mount the AI debug /
39	/// hint panel for a given room. Set at room creation time from the
40	/// first joiner's `?hints=1` query parameter, frozen for the room's
41	/// lifetime (same pattern as `password`). Closes the previous
42	/// client-only-`?debug=1` cheat hole — net-mode clients now refuse to
43	/// mount the panel unless the server says hints are allowed in this
44	/// room. Wire-compatible: `#[serde(default)]` on every new field means
45	/// v4 clients deserializing v5 messages see `hints_allowed = false`,
46	/// and v5 clients deserializing v4 messages also see `false`. Local
47	/// (offline / GitHub Pages) `?debug=1` and `?hints=1` are unaffected
48	/// — they bypass the server entirely.
49	pub const PROTOCOL_VERSION: u16 = 5;
50	
51	/// Server → client.
52	#[derive(Clone, Debug, Serialize, Deserialize)]
53	#[serde(tag = "type")]
54	pub enum ServerMsg {
55	    /// Sent once per client right after side assignment. Carries the variant
56	    /// (so the client knows what to render) plus the initial `PlayerView`.
57	    /// `hints_allowed` (v5+) reflects whether the room was created with
58	    /// `?hints=1`; clients consult this to decide whether to mount the
59	    /// AI debug / hint panel.
60	    Hello {
61	        protocol: u16,
62	        observer: Side,
63	        rules: RuleSet,
64	        view: PlayerView,
65	        #[serde(default)]
66	        hints_allowed: bool,
67	    },
68	    /// Sent to every seated client after each committed move.
69	    Update { view: PlayerView },
70	    /// Illegal move, room full, malformed message, opponent disconnect, etc.
71	    /// Non-fatal by default — clients should display and keep the connection.
72	    Error { message: String },
73	    /// Pushed on the lobby socket only — initial snapshot on connect, then
74	    /// again whenever any room's state changes (seat insertion / removal /
75	    /// game finish / GC). Game sockets never receive this variant.
76	    Rooms { rooms: Vec<RoomSummary> },
77	    /// Spectator-side counterpart to `Hello` — no `observer` because
78	    /// spectators are not seated. Carries the same `rules` + initial `view`
79	    /// (projected from `Side::RED`'s perspective so banqi hidden tiles stay
80	    /// hidden). Sent immediately after a `?role=spectator` connection
81	    /// upgrades successfully. `hints_allowed` (v5+) — see [`Hello`].
82	    Spectating {
83	        protocol: u16,
84	        rules: RuleSet,
85	        view: PlayerView,
86	        #[serde(default)]
87	        hints_allowed: bool,
88	    },
89	    /// Pushed once right after `Hello` / `Spectating` with the room's chat
90	    /// ring buffer (≤50 lines). Empty `lines` for a fresh room.
91	    ChatHistory { lines: Vec<ChatLine> },
92	    /// Pushed live to every recipient (seats + spectators) on each new chat
93	    /// line. `from` is currently always a player's `Side`; system messages
94	    /// (player joined / left) are deferred — see
95	    /// `backlog/chess-net-system-messages.md`.
96	    Chat { line: ChatLine },
97	}
98	
99	/// Client → server.
100	#[derive(Clone, Debug, Serialize, Deserialize)]
101	#[serde(tag = "type")]
102	pub enum ClientMsg {
103	    /// Submit a move. For `Move::Reveal`, send `revealed: None` — the server
104	    /// fills in the piece identity locally; the wire form stays `None`.
105	    Move { mv: Move },
106	    /// Resign — ends the game with `WinReason::Resignation` for the opponent.
107	    Resign,
108	    /// Request a rematch. Only valid after the current game has ended
109	    /// (Won / Drawn). Server resets the game when both sides have requested
110	    /// and re-sends `Hello` to each. Same observer assignment, same rules,
111	    /// fresh `GameState`.
112	    Rematch,
113	    /// Lobby-only: forces a fresh `Rooms` snapshot to the requester. The
114	    /// server pushes `Rooms` automatically on state changes; this is the
115	    /// manual-refresh button (`r` in the TUI lobby).
116	    ListRooms,
117	    /// Send a chat line. Server enforces players-only (spectators get
118	    /// `Error{"spectators cannot chat"}`), trims to ≤256 chars after stripping
119	    /// control chars, stamps `ts_ms` server-side, and broadcasts the result
120	    /// to every seat + spectator as `ServerMsg::Chat`.
121	    Chat { text: String },
122	}
123	
124	/// One chat line as it appears on the wire and in the per-room ring buffer.
125	/// `ts_ms` is unix milliseconds set server-side so clients don't have to
126	/// agree on a clock.
127	#[derive(Clone, Debug, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq, Eq)]
128	pub struct ChatLine {
129	    pub from: Side,
130	    pub text: String,
131	    pub ts_ms: u64,
132	}
133	
134	/// One row in the lobby browser. Mirrors what's interesting to a player
135	/// deciding whether to join: variant for setting expectations, seat count
136	/// for "is this room joinable?", `has_password` for the lock icon, and
137	/// `status` so finished rooms stand out from active games.
138	#[derive(Clone, Debug, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq, Eq)]
139	pub struct RoomSummary {
140	    pub id: String,
141	    pub variant: String,
142	    pub seats: u8,
143	    /// Watching-only clients connected via `?role=spectator`. v2 lobby
144	    /// snapshots omit this field entirely; the `serde(default)` keeps v2
145	    /// clients deserializing a v3 `RoomSummary` cleanly (they just see 0).
146	    #[serde(default)]
147	    pub spectators: u16,
148	    pub has_password: bool,
149	    pub status: RoomStatus,
150	    /// Whether this room was created with `?hints=1` — i.e., AI hints
151	    /// (the in-game debug panel + PV overlay) are sanctioned by the
152	    /// room and visible to BOTH players + spectators. Frozen for the
153	    /// room's lifetime. v4 lobby snapshots omit this field; the
154	    /// `serde(default)` keeps v4 clients reading v5 summaries cleanly
155	    /// (treating the column as `false`).
156	    #[serde(default)]
157	    pub hints_allowed: bool,
158	}
159	
160	#[derive(Copy, Clone, Debug, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq, Eq)]
161	#[serde(rename_all = "lowercase")]
162	pub enum RoomStatus {
163	    /// Game has not begun (≤1 seat occupied or ongoing with both seats).
164	    Lobby,
165	    /// Both seats occupied and the game is `Ongoing`.
166	    Playing,
167	    /// `GameStatus::Won` or `GameStatus::Drawn`. The room still exists but
168	    /// requires a rematch (or for both clients to disconnect → GC) to be
169	    /// reusable.
170	    Finished,
171	}
172	
173	/// Stable variant string for `RoomSummary.variant`. Uses kebab-case so the
174	/// JSON looks the same as the chess-tui `--style ascii` / `--style cjk`
175	/// arguments and the `--house` token list.
176	pub fn variant_label(rules: &RuleSet) -> &'static str {
177	    match rules.variant {
178	        Variant::Xiangqi => {
179	            if rules.xiangqi_allow_self_check {
180	                "xiangqi"
181	            } else {
182	                "xiangqi-strict"
183	            }
184	        }
185	        Variant::Banqi => "banqi",
186	        Variant::ThreeKingdomBanqi => "three-kingdom",
187	    }
188	}
189
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
1	//! Multi-room ws server. Routes:
2	//!
3	//!   GET /             → upgrade into default room "main" (back-compat)
4	//!   GET /ws           → upgrade into default room "main" (back-compat)
5	//!   GET /ws/:room_id  → upgrade into named room (auto-created on first join)
6	//!   GET /lobby        → subscribe to live `Rooms` pushes
7	//!   GET /rooms        → JSON snapshot for `curl`/debug
8	//!
9	//! Optional `?password=<secret>` query param on the game-room routes locks
10	//! the room to the first joiner's secret. Wrong password gets
11	//! `Error{"bad password"}` + close.
12	//!
13	//! Optional `?role=spectator` upgrades the connection as a read-only
14	//! spectator instead of a seat. Spectators receive `Spectating` (instead of
15	//! `Hello`) plus `ChatHistory`, then live `Update` and `Chat` pushes. They
16	//! cannot move, resign, request rematch, or send chat. Player connections
17	//! arrive without `?role=spectator` — back-compat means v2 clients keep
18	//! getting "room full" if they're the third joiner.
19	//!
20	//! Empty rooms are GC'd when the last seat disconnects **and** the last
21	//! spectator leaves, except `main` which is permanent so v1 clients always
22	//! have a stable default to land in.
23	//!
24	//! Reconnect / mixed-variant rooms / time controls / TLS still deferred —
25	//! see `TODO.md`.
26	//!
27	//! ## Refactor history
28	//!
29	//! As of 2026-05-11 (Phase 1 of `backlog/webrtc-lan-pairing.md`) all room
30	//! state lives in [`crate::room::Room`] — a transport-agnostic state
31	//! machine that compiles for `wasm32-unknown-unknown`. This file is now
32	//! a thin axum/tokio glue that owns:
33	//!
34	//! 1. The per-room `Arc<Mutex<RoomEntry>>` table (room + peer routing map).
35	//! 2. The lobby-summary projection + push fan-out.
36	//! 3. `?password=`/`?role=`/`?hints=` URL parsing.
37	//! 4. The `--static-dir` static-file serving.
38	//!
39	//! Per-message work is just: deserialize → `room.apply(peer, msg)` →
40	//! fan out the returned `Vec<Outbound>` via the per-room routing table.
41	
42	// === module skeleton — section bodies filled in via edits below ===
43	
44	// SECTION: imports
45	use std::collections::HashMap;
46	use std::net::SocketAddr;
47	use std::sync::atomic::{AtomicU64, Ordering};
48	use std::sync::Arc;
49	
50	use anyhow::{Context, Result};
51	use axum::extract::ws::{Message, WebSocket, WebSocketUpgrade};
52	use axum::extract::{Path, Query, State};
53	#[cfg(feature = "static-serve")]
54	use axum::http::{header, HeaderValue};
55	#[cfg(feature = "static-serve")]
56	use axum::middleware::Next;
57	use axum::response::IntoResponse;
58	use axum::routing::get;
59	use axum::{Json, Router};
60	use chess_core::rules::RuleSet;
61	use futures_util::stream::StreamExt;
62	use futures_util::SinkExt;
63	use serde::Deserialize;
64	use tokio::sync::{mpsc, Mutex};
65	
66	use crate::protocol::{ClientMsg, RoomSummary, ServerMsg};
67	use crate::room::{
68	    parse_hints_param, valid_password, valid_room_id, JoinError, Outbound, PeerId, Room,
69	    DEFAULT_MAX_SPECTATORS, DEFAULT_ROOM, MAX_PASSWORD_LEN, MAX_ROOM_ID_LEN,
70	};
71	// SECTION: cache constants (static-serve)
72	#[cfg(feature = "static-serve")]
73	const IMMUTABLE_ASSET_CACHE: &str = "public, max-age=31536000, immutable";
74	#[cfg(feature = "static-serve")]
75	const HTML_CACHE: &str = "no-cache";
76	// SECTION: app state types
77	
78	/// Per-connection outbound channel. The handler hands one of these to the
79	/// per-room peer map; the `Room::apply` fan-out delivers `ServerMsg`s into
80	/// it; a per-connection write task drains it onto the WebSocket sink.
81	type PeerSender = mpsc::UnboundedSender<ServerMsg>;
82	
83	/// One row in the per-room peer routing table.
84	struct RoomEntry {
85	    room: Room,
86	    peers: HashMap<PeerId, PeerSender>,
87	}
88	
89	impl RoomEntry {
90	    fn new(room: Room) -> Self {
91	        Self { room, peers: HashMap::new() }
92	    }
93	
94	    /// Deliver an `Outbound` list to the matching peer senders. Drops any
95	    /// outbound whose target peer has already disconnected — its sender
96	    /// is gone from the map.
97	    fn fanout(&self, out: Vec<Outbound>) {
98	        for ob in out {
99	            if let Some(tx) = self.peers.get(&ob.peer) {
100	                let _ = tx.send(ob.msg);
101	            }
102	        }
103	    }
104	}
105	
106	type RoomMap = Arc<Mutex<HashMap<String, Arc<Mutex<RoomEntry>>>>>;
107	type SummaryMap = Arc<Mutex<HashMap<String, RoomSummary>>>;
108	type LobbySubs = Arc<Mutex<Vec<mpsc::UnboundedSender<ServerMsg>>>>;
109	
110	/// Server-wide state shared across every connection. Lock acquisition order
111	/// when multiple maps are touched: `rooms` → `summaries` → `lobby_subs`.
112	/// Per-room inner locks (`Arc<Mutex<RoomEntry>>`) live separately and are
113	/// never held across an outer-map lock acquisition.
114	pub struct AppState {
115	    rooms: RoomMap,
116	    /// Lobby-visible projection of every live room. Updated by
117	    /// `refresh_summary` / `drop_summary` after every room mutation, so
118	    /// `notify_lobby` can build a `Rooms` push without touching any inner
119	    /// `Room` lock.
120	    summaries: SummaryMap,
121	    /// Outbound senders for every connected lobby socket.
122	    lobby_subs: LobbySubs,
123	    /// Variant for every auto-created room. Mixed-variant servers are a
124	    /// separate TODO (`chess-net: mixed-variant rooms`).
125	    default_rules: RuleSet,
126	    /// Per-room cap on `?role=spectator` connections. The 17th spectator
127	    /// (at the default cap of 16) gets `Error{"room watch capacity reached"}`.
128	    max_spectators: usize,
129	    /// Monotonic counter for `PeerId` allocation. Only the value's
130	    /// uniqueness within a single room matters; using one global counter
131	    /// keeps the implementation trivial.
132	    next_peer_id: AtomicU64,
133	}
134	
135	impl AppState {
136	    fn new(default_rules: RuleSet, max_spectators: usize) -> Arc<Self> {
137	        Arc::new(Self {
138	            rooms: Arc::new(Mutex::new(HashMap::new())),
139	            summaries: Arc::new(Mutex::new(HashMap::new())),
140	            lobby_subs: Arc::new(Mutex::new(Vec::new())),
141	            default_rules,
142	            max_spectators,
143	            next_peer_id: AtomicU64::new(1),
144	        })
145	    }
146	
147	    fn alloc_peer(&self) -> PeerId {
148	        PeerId(self.next_peer_id.fetch_add(1, Ordering::Relaxed))
149	    }
150	}
151	// SECTION: query types
152	
153	#[derive(Debug, Deserialize)]
154	struct AuthQuery {
155	    #[serde(default)]
156	    password: Option<String>,
157	    /// Opt-in spectator request. `Some("spectator")` upgrades the connection
158	    /// as a read-only watcher. v2 clients never set this so back-compat
159	    /// behaviour is preserved (third joiner still gets "room full").
160	    #[serde(default)]
161	    role: Option<String>,
162	    /// Opt-in AI hint sanction. `Some("1")` / `Some("true")` from the
163	    /// **first joiner** sets `Room.hints_allowed = true` for the room's
164	    /// lifetime; subsequent joiners' values are ignored.
165	    #[serde(default)]
166	    hints: Option<String>,
167	}
168	
169	#[derive(Copy, Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Eq)]
170	enum JoinRole {
171	    Player,
172	    Spectator,
173	}
174	
175	fn parse_role(raw: Option<&str>) -> JoinRole {
176	    match raw {
177	        Some(s) if s.eq_ignore_ascii_case("spectator") => JoinRole::Spectator,
178	        _ => JoinRole::Player,
179	    }
180	}
181	// SECTION: serve options + run/serve entry points
182	
183	/// Server options. `rules` is required; `static_dir` is opt-in and only
184	/// honored when the `static-serve` feature is enabled.
185	#[derive(Clone, Debug)]
186	pub struct ServeOpts {
187	    pub rules: RuleSet,
188	    pub static_dir: Option<std::path::PathBuf>,
189	    /// Per-room spectator cap. Defaults to [`DEFAULT_MAX_SPECTATORS`] when
190	    /// constructed via [`ServeOpts::new`].
191	    pub max_spectators: usize,
192	}
193	
194	impl ServeOpts {
195	    pub fn new(rules: RuleSet) -> Self {
196	        Self { rules, static_dir: None, max_spectators: DEFAULT_MAX_SPECTATORS }
197	    }
198	
199	    pub fn with_static_dir(mut self, dir: Option<std::path::PathBuf>) -> Self {
200	        self.static_dir = dir;
201	        self
202	    }
203	
204	    pub fn with_max_spectators(mut self, cap: usize) -> Self {
205	        self.max_spectators = cap;
206	        self
207	    }
208	}
209	
210	/// Bind `addr` and serve until error / SIGINT (caller's choice — we don't
211	/// install signal handling here). For tests that need an ephemeral port,
212	/// use [`serve`] directly with a pre-bound listener.
213	pub async fn run(addr: SocketAddr, rules: RuleSet) -> Result<()> {
214	    run_with(addr, ServeOpts::new(rules)).await
215	}
216	
217	/// Like [`run`] but with the full options struct (e.g. `--static-dir`).
218	pub async fn run_with(addr: SocketAddr, opts: ServeOpts) -> Result<()> {
219	    let listener =
220	        tokio::net::TcpListener::bind(addr).await.with_context(|| format!("bind {addr}"))?;
221	    eprintln!("[server] listening on ws://{}", listener.local_addr()?);
222	    if opts.static_dir.is_some() {
223	        eprintln!("[server] serving static dir at /");
224	    }
225	    serve_with(listener, opts).await
226	}
227	
228	/// Serve on a pre-bound listener. The listener owns the port; use
229	/// `listener.local_addr()` before passing it in if you need to know it
230	/// (e.g. ephemeral ports in tests).
231	pub async fn serve(listener: tokio::net::TcpListener, rules: RuleSet) -> Result<()> {
232	    serve_with(listener, ServeOpts::new(rules)).await
233	}
234	
235	/// Like [`serve`] but with the full options struct.
236	pub async fn serve_with(listener: tokio::net::TcpListener, opts: ServeOpts) -> Result<()> {
237	    let app_state = AppState::new(opts.rules, opts.max_spectators);
238	    let mut app = Router::new()
239	        .route("/ws", get(upgrade_default))
240	        .route("/ws/:room_id", get(upgrade_room))
241	        .route("/lobby", get(upgrade_lobby))
242	        .route("/rooms", get(rooms_snapshot_json));
243	
244	    #[cfg(feature = "static-serve")]
245	    {
246	        if let Some(dir) = opts.static_dir.as_ref() {
247	            // SPA fallback — `/local/xiangqi`, `/play/foo`, etc. all
248	            // serve `index.html` so client-side routing takes over. The
249	            // explicit `/ws*`, `/lobby`, `/rooms` routes above still match
250	            // first because axum tries routes before fallback.
251	            //
252	            // NOTE: enabling --static-dir means `GET /` serves index.html.
253	            // Old `chess-tui --connect ws://host` (which hits `/`) must
254	            // switch to `--connect ws://host/ws`.
255	            use tower::ServiceBuilder;
256	            use tower_http::compression::CompressionLayer;
257	            use tower_http::services::{ServeDir, ServeFile};
258	            let index = dir.join("index.html");
259	            let serve_dir = ServeDir::new(dir).fallback(ServeFile::new(index));
260	            let static_service = ServiceBuilder::new()
261	                .layer(CompressionLayer::new().no_deflate().no_zstd())
262	                .service(serve_dir);
263	            app = app
264	                .fallback_service(static_service)
265	                .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn(static_cache_headers));
266	        } else {
267	            app = app.route("/", get(upgrade_default));
268	        }
269	    }
270	    #[cfg(not(feature = "static-serve"))]
271	    {
272	        let _ = opts.static_dir;
273	        app = app.route("/", get(upgrade_default));
274	    }
275	
276	    let app = app.with_state(app_state);
277	    axum::serve(listener, app).await.context("axum::serve")?;
278	    Ok(())
279	}
280	// SECTION: static-serve cache headers
281	
282	#[cfg(feature = "static-serve")]
283	async fn static_cache_headers(req: axum::extract::Request, next: Next) -> axum::response::Response {
284	    let path = req.uri().path().to_string();
285	    let mut res = next.run(req).await;
286	    if let Some(value) =
287	        cache_control_for_static_response(&path, res.headers().get(header::CONTENT_TYPE))
288	    {
289	        res.headers_mut().insert(header::CACHE_CONTROL, HeaderValue::from_static(value));
290	    }
291	    res
292	}
293	
294	#[cfg(feature = "static-serve")]
295	fn cache_control_for_static_response(
296	    path: &str,
297	    content_type: Option<&HeaderValue>,
298	) -> Option<&'static str> {
299	    if path == "/rooms" || path == "/lobby" || path.starts_with("/ws") {
300	        return None;
301	    }
302	    let content_type = content_type.and_then(|value| value.to_str().ok()).unwrap_or_default();
303	    if content_type.starts_with("text/html") {
304	        return Some(HTML_CACHE);
305	    }
306	    if is_hashed_asset_path(path) {
307	        return Some(IMMUTABLE_ASSET_CACHE);
308	    }
309	    Some(HTML_CACHE)
310	}
311	
312	#[cfg(feature = "static-serve")]
313	fn is_hashed_asset_path(path: &str) -> bool {
314	    let file_name = path.rsplit('/').next().unwrap_or_default();
315	    (file_name.ends_with(".css") || file_name.ends_with(".js") || file_name.ends_with(".wasm"))
316	        && file_name.contains('-')
317	}
318	// SECTION: room socket upgrade
319	
320	async fn upgrade_default(
321	    State(app): State<Arc<AppState>>,
322	    Query(q): Query<AuthQuery>,
323	    ws: WebSocketUpgrade,
324	) -> impl IntoResponse {
325	    let role = parse_role(q.role.as_deref());
326	    let hints = parse_hints_param(q.hints.as_deref());
327	    ws.on_upgrade(move |socket| {
328	        handle_room_socket(socket, app, DEFAULT_ROOM.to_string(), q.password, role, hints)
329	    })
330	}
331	
332	async fn upgrade_room(
333	    State(app): State<Arc<AppState>>,
334	    Path(room_id): Path<String>,
335	    Query(q): Query<AuthQuery>,
336	    ws: WebSocketUpgrade,
337	) -> impl IntoResponse {
338	    let room_id_clone = room_id.clone();
339	    let role = parse_role(q.role.as_deref());
340	    let hints = parse_hints_param(q.hints.as_deref());
341	    ws.on_upgrade(move |socket| {
342	        handle_room_socket(socket, app, room_id_clone, q.password, role, hints)
343	    })
344	}
345	
346	async fn upgrade_lobby(
347	    State(app): State<Arc<AppState>>,
348	    ws: WebSocketUpgrade,
349	) -> impl IntoResponse {
350	    ws.on_upgrade(move |socket| handle_lobby_socket(socket, app))
351	}
352	// SECTION: room socket handler
353	
354	async fn handle_room_socket(
355	    socket: WebSocket,
356	    app: Arc<AppState>,
357	    room_id: String,
358	    password_param: Option<String>,
359	    role: JoinRole,
360	    hints_param: bool,
361	) {
362	    let (mut sender, mut receiver) = socket.split();
363	    let (out_tx, mut out_rx) = mpsc::unbounded_channel::<ServerMsg>();
364	
365	    // === Pre-flight validation ===
366	    if !valid_room_id(&room_id) {
367	        let _ = send_close_with(
368	            &mut sender,
369	            ServerMsg::Error {
370	                message: format!(
371	                    "invalid room id (allowed: a-z A-Z 0-9 _ -, max {MAX_ROOM_ID_LEN} chars)"
372	                ),
373	            },
374	        )
375	        .await;
376	        return;
377	    }
378	    if let Some(pw) = password_param.as_ref() {
379	        if !valid_password(pw) {
380	            let _ = send_close_with(
381	                &mut sender,
382	                ServerMsg::Error {
383	                    message: format!(
384	                        "invalid password (max {MAX_PASSWORD_LEN} chars, no control chars)"
385	                    ),
386	                },
387	            )
388	            .await;
389	            return;
390	        }
391	    }
392	
393	    // === Get-or-create the room ===
394	    //
395	    // Spectators don't auto-create — joining as a spectator into a
396	    // non-existent room would be a confusing UX (you'd see an empty board
397	    // with no players). Players still create on demand.
398	    let room_entry = {
399	        let mut rooms = app.rooms.lock().await;
400	        if role == JoinRole::Spectator && !rooms.contains_key(&room_id) {
401	            drop(rooms);
402	            let _ = send_close_with(
403	                &mut sender,
404	                ServerMsg::Error { message: "no such room to spectate".into() },
405	            )
406	            .await;
407	            return;
408	        }
409	        rooms
410	            .entry(room_id.clone())
411	            .or_insert_with(|| {
412	                Arc::new(Mutex::new(RoomEntry::new(Room::new(
413	                    app.default_rules.clone(),
414	                    password_param.clone(),
415	                    hints_param,
416	                ))))
417	            })
418	            .clone()
419	    };
420	
421	    // === Password gate ===
422	    //
423	    // Locked rooms are locked for everyone (seats and spectators).
424	    {
425	        let entry = room_entry.lock().await;
426	        if entry.room.password() != password_param.as_deref() {
427	            drop(entry);
428	            let _ =
429	                send_close_with(&mut sender, ServerMsg::Error { message: "bad password".into() })
430	                    .await;
431	            return;
432	        }
433	    }
434	
435	    // === Seat the peer ===
436	    let peer = app.alloc_peer();
437	    let summary_after_join = {
438	        let mut entry = room_entry.lock().await;
439	        // Insert sender FIRST so any outbound the room emits during join
440	        // (Hello / ChatHistory) is deliverable.
441	        entry.peers.insert(peer, out_tx.clone());
442	        // The two role branches return different shapes — `join_player`
443	        // also reports the assigned `Side` for the eprintln. Handle each
444	        // branch separately and reduce both into `Result<Vec<Outbound>, JoinError>`.
445	        let join_result: Result<Vec<Outbound>, JoinError> = match role {
446	            JoinRole::Player => match entry.room.join_player(peer) {
447	                Ok((side, out)) => {
448	                    eprintln!(
449	                        "[server][{}] seated {:?} ({}/2) as peer {}",
450	                        room_id,
451	                        side,
452	                        entry.room.seat_count(),
453	                        peer.0
454	                    );
455	                    Ok(out)
456	                }
457	                Err(e) => Err(e),
458	            },
459	            JoinRole::Spectator => {
460	                let cap = app.max_spectators;
461	                match entry.room.join_spectator(peer, cap) {
462	                    Ok(out) => {
463	                        eprintln!(
464	                            "[server][{}] spectator joined as peer {} ({}/{})",
465	                            room_id,
466	                            peer.0,
467	                            entry.room.spectator_count(),
468	                            cap
469	                        );
470	                        Ok(out)
471	                    }
472	                    Err(e) => Err(e),
473	                }
474	            }
475	        };
476	        match join_result {
477	            Ok(outbound) => {
478	                entry.fanout(outbound);
479	                Some(entry.room.summary(&room_id))
480	            }
481	            Err(err) => {
482	                // Roll back the peer insert; never seated.
483	                entry.peers.remove(&peer);
484	                let msg = match err {
485	                    JoinError::RoomFull => "room full",
486	                    JoinError::SpectatorCapReached => "room watch capacity reached",
487	                };
488	                drop(entry);
489	                let _ =
490	                    send_close_with(&mut sender, ServerMsg::Error { message: msg.into() }).await;
491	                return;
492	            }
493	        }
494	    };
495	    if let Some(summary) = summary_after_join {
496	        refresh_summary(&app, &room_id, summary).await;
497	    }
498	
499	    // === Write task: drain mpsc → ws sink ===
500	    //
501	    // Exits naturally when all senders drop.
502	    let write_task = tokio::spawn(async move {
503	        while let Some(msg) = out_rx.recv().await {
504	            let json = match serde_json::to_string(&msg) {
505	                Ok(s) => s,
506	                Err(e) => {
507	                    eprintln!("[server][warn] serialize ServerMsg: {e}");
508	                    continue;
509	                }
510	            };
511	            if sender.send(Message::Text(json)).await.is_err() {
512	                break;
513	            }
514	        }
515	        let _ = sender.close().await;
516	    });
517	
518	    // === Read loop ===
519	    while let Some(frame) = receiver.next().await {
520	        let frame = match frame {
521	            Ok(f) => f,
522	            Err(e) => {
523	                eprintln!("[server][warn][{}] ws read error (peer {}): {e}", room_id, peer.0);
524	                break;
525	            }
526	        };
527	        let text = match frame {
528	            Message::Text(t) => t,
529	            Message::Close(_) => break,
530	            _ => continue,
531	        };
532	        let msg: ClientMsg = match serde_json::from_str(&text) {
533	            Ok(m) => m,
534	            Err(e) => {
535	                let _ =
536	                    out_tx.send(ServerMsg::Error { message: format!("malformed message: {e}") });
537	                continue;
538	            }
539	        };
540	        let entry_arc = match get_room(&app, &room_id).await {
541	            Some(r) => r,
542	            None => break,
543	        };
544	        let summary_after = {
545	            let mut entry = entry_arc.lock().await;
546	            let outbound = entry.room.apply(peer, msg);
547	            entry.fanout(outbound);
548	            entry.room.summary(&room_id)
549	        };
550	        refresh_summary(&app, &room_id, summary_after).await;
551	    }
552	
553	    // === Disconnect cleanup ===
554	    eprintln!("[server][{}] peer {} disconnected", room_id, peer.0);
555	    if let Some(entry_arc) = get_room(&app, &room_id).await {
556	        let (summary_after, room_now_empty) = {
557	            let mut entry = entry_arc.lock().await;
558	            let outbound = entry.room.leave(peer);
559	            entry.fanout(outbound);
560	            entry.peers.remove(&peer);
561	            (entry.room.summary(&room_id), entry.room.is_empty())
562	        };
563	        if room_now_empty && room_id != DEFAULT_ROOM {
564	            let mut rooms = app.rooms.lock().await;
565	            rooms.remove(&room_id);
566	            drop(rooms);
567	            drop_summary(&app, &room_id).await;
568	        } else {
569	            refresh_summary(&app, &room_id, summary_after).await;
570	        }
571	    }
572	
573	    drop(out_tx);
574	    let _ = write_task.await;
575	}
576	// SECTION: lobby socket handler
577	
578	async fn handle_lobby_socket(socket: WebSocket, app: Arc<AppState>) {
579	    let (mut sender, mut receiver) = socket.split();
580	    let (out_tx, mut out_rx) = mpsc::unbounded_channel::<ServerMsg>();
581	
582	    // Snapshot first, then subscribe. Doing it in this order guarantees the
583	    // subscriber doesn't miss a push that lands between snapshot and
584	    // subscribe — the worst case is they get the same state twice, which
585	    // the client treats as idempotent.
586	    let snapshot = current_summary_list(&app).await;
587	    if out_tx.send(ServerMsg::Rooms { rooms: snapshot }).is_err() {
588	        return;
589	    }
590	
591	    {
592	        let mut subs = app.lobby_subs.lock().await;
593	        subs.push(out_tx.clone());
594	    }
595	
596	    let write_task = tokio::spawn(async move {
597	        while let Some(msg) = out_rx.recv().await {
598	            let json = match serde_json::to_string(&msg) {
599	                Ok(s) => s,
600	                Err(e) => {
601	                    eprintln!("[server][warn] serialize lobby ServerMsg: {e}");
602	                    continue;
603	                }
604	            };
605	            if sender.send(Message::Text(json)).await.is_err() {
606	                break;
607	            }
608	        }
609	        let _ = sender.close().await;
610	    });
611	
612	    // Lobby read loop: only `ListRooms` is meaningful. Everything else gets
613	    // a polite Error (the client may have been confused about which socket
614	    // it has open).
615	    while let Some(frame) = receiver.next().await {
616	        let frame = match frame {
617	            Ok(f) => f,
618	            Err(_) => break,
619	        };
620	        let text = match frame {
621	            Message::Text(t) => t,
622	            Message::Close(_) => break,
623	            _ => continue,
624	        };
625	        match serde_json::from_str::<ClientMsg>(&text) {
626	            Ok(ClientMsg::ListRooms) => {
627	                let snap = current_summary_list(&app).await;
628	                let _ = out_tx.send(ServerMsg::Rooms { rooms: snap });
629	            }
630	            Ok(_) => {
631	                let _ = out_tx.send(ServerMsg::Error {
632	                    message: "lobby socket only accepts ListRooms; join /ws/<id> to play".into(),
633	                });
634	            }
635	            Err(e) => {
636	                let _ =
637	                    out_tx.send(ServerMsg::Error { message: format!("malformed message: {e}") });
638	            }
639	        }
640	    }
641	
642	    // Cleanup: remove this sub from the broadcast list.
643	    {
644	        let mut subs = app.lobby_subs.lock().await;
645	        subs.retain(|tx| !tx.same_channel(&out_tx));
646	    }
647	    drop(out_tx);
648	    let _ = write_task.await;
649	}
650	// SECTION: lobby summary helpers
651	
652	async fn get_room(app: &AppState, room_id: &str) -> Option<Arc<Mutex<RoomEntry>>> {
653	    let rooms = app.rooms.lock().await;
654	    rooms.get(room_id).cloned()
655	}
656	
657	async fn current_summary_list(app: &AppState) -> Vec<RoomSummary> {
658	    let summaries = app.summaries.lock().await;
659	    let mut rooms: Vec<RoomSummary> = summaries.values().cloned().collect();
660	    rooms.sort_by(|a, b| a.id.cmp(&b.id));
661	    rooms
662	}
663	
664	async fn refresh_summary(app: &AppState, room_id: &str, summary: RoomSummary) {
665	    {
666	        let mut summaries = app.summaries.lock().await;
667	        summaries.insert(room_id.to_string(), summary);
668	    }
669	    notify_lobby(app).await;
670	}
671	
672	async fn drop_summary(app: &AppState, room_id: &str) {
673	    {
674	        let mut summaries = app.summaries.lock().await;
675	        summaries.remove(room_id);
676	    }
677	    notify_lobby(app).await;
678	}
679	
680	async fn notify_lobby(app: &AppState) {
681	    let snapshot = current_summary_list(app).await;
682	    let msg = ServerMsg::Rooms { rooms: snapshot };
683	    let subs: Vec<_> = {
684	        let s = app.lobby_subs.lock().await;
685	        s.clone()
686	    };
687	    for tx in subs {
688	        let _ = tx.send(msg.clone());
689	    }
690	}
691	// SECTION: rooms snapshot json
692	
693	async fn rooms_snapshot_json(State(app): State<Arc<AppState>>) -> Json<Vec<RoomSummary>> {
694	    let summaries = app.summaries.lock().await;
695	    let mut rooms: Vec<RoomSummary> = summaries.values().cloned().collect();
696	    rooms.sort_by(|a, b| a.id.cmp(&b.id));
697	    Json(rooms)
698	}
699	// SECTION: send_close helper
700	
701	async fn send_close_with(
702	    sender: &mut futures_util::stream::SplitSink<WebSocket, Message>,
703	    msg: ServerMsg,
704	) -> Result<(), axum::Error> {
705	    let json = serde_json::to_string(&msg).unwrap_or_default();
706	    sender.send(Message::Text(json)).await?;
707	    sender.close().await
708	}
709
```

```text
1	//! After the first flip locks `side_assignment`, banqi move-gen must emit
2	//! moves for the piece-color the seat actually controls — not the seat name.
3	
4	use chess_core::moves::Move;
5	use chess_core::piece::Side;
6	use chess_core::rules::{HouseRules, RuleSet};
7	use chess_core::state::GameState;
8	
9	/// Find a banqi seed where RED's first reveal happens to expose a Black
10	/// piece. We deterministically scan seeds; with 32 cells / 2 colors the
11	/// first available reveal is Black with high frequency.
12	fn seed_where_first_reveal_is_black() -> u64 {
13	    for seed in 0u64..256 {
14	        let mut state = GameState::new(RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(HouseRules::empty(), seed));
15	        // Pick the FIRST face-down piece by iteration order and check its
16	        // hidden color (we peek at the board rather than calling make_move
17	        // so we can pick a deterministic seed without mutating state).
18	        let first_sq = state
19	            .board
20	            .squares()
21	            .find(|sq| state.board.get(*sq).map(|p| !p.revealed).unwrap_or(false));
22	        let Some(sq) = first_sq else { continue };
23	        let pos = state.board.get(sq).unwrap();
24	        if pos.piece.side == Side::BLACK {
25	            // Validate that we can reveal this square as RED's first move.
26	            let m = Move::Reveal { at: sq, revealed: None };
27	            if state.make_move(&m).is_ok() {
28	                return seed;
29	            }
30	        }
31	    }
32	    panic!("no seed found where RED's first reveal is Black");
33	}
34	
35	#[test]
36	fn red_seat_revealing_black_plays_black_color() {
37	    let seed = seed_where_first_reveal_is_black();
38	    let mut state = GameState::new(RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(HouseRules::empty(), seed));
39	    assert_eq!(state.side_to_move, Side::RED, "banqi always starts with RED seat");
40	
41	    // Locate the first face-down square (matches the seed-search above).
42	    let first_sq = state
43	        .board
44	        .squares()
45	        .find(|sq| state.board.get(*sq).map(|p| !p.revealed).unwrap_or(false))
46	        .unwrap();
47	    let revealed_color = state.board.get(first_sq).unwrap().piece.side;
48	    assert_eq!(revealed_color, Side::BLACK, "seed selected for first-flip-Black");
49	
50	    // RED reveals a Black piece — RED-seat now plays Black.
51	    state.make_move(&Move::Reveal { at: first_sq, revealed: None }).unwrap();
52	
53	    let mapping = state
54	        .side_assignment
55	        .as_ref()
56	        .expect("side_assignment must lock after first flip")
57	        .mapping
58	        .clone();
59	    assert_eq!(mapping[Side::RED.0 as usize], Side::BLACK, "RED-seat plays Black");
60	    assert_eq!(mapping[Side::BLACK.0 as usize], Side::RED, "BLACK-seat plays Red");
61	
62	    // Now turn advances to BLACK-seat (who plays Red color). `current_color()`
63	    // must reflect Red, not Black.
64	    assert_eq!(state.side_to_move, Side::BLACK, "turn advances to BLACK seat");
65	    assert_eq!(state.current_color(), Side::RED, "BLACK-seat plays Red color after first flip");
66	
67	    // Move-gen must not offer moves for Black pieces; only Red pieces +
68	    // remaining reveals. (The seat is BLACK, but the color it plays is RED.)
69	    let moves = state.legal_moves();
70	    let any_non_reveal_for_black = moves.iter().any(|m| {
71	        if let Move::Reveal { .. } = m {
72	            return false;
73	        }
74	        // Look up the piece at the move origin and confirm it's RED-colored
75	        // (i.e. the seat's controlled color).
76	        let origin = m.origin_square();
77	        match state.board.get(origin) {
78	            Some(p) if p.revealed => p.piece.side == Side::RED,
79	            _ => false,
80	        }
81	    });
82	    let any_non_reveal_for_red = moves.iter().any(|m| {
83	        if let Move::Reveal { .. } = m {
84	            return false;
85	        }
86	        let origin = m.origin_square();
87	        match state.board.get(origin) {
88	            Some(p) if p.revealed => p.piece.side == Side::BLACK,
89	            _ => false,
90	        }
91	    });
92	    // BLACK-seat plays RED color → only Red-piece moves should appear.
93	    // (At this point only one piece is revealed — the Black one we flipped.
94	    // BLACK can't move it. So only Reveal moves are legal. The presence/absence
95	    // checks below validate the *direction* of the filter even when no movable
96	    // piece exists yet.)
97	    assert!(
98	        !any_non_reveal_for_red,
99	        "BLACK-seat (playing Red) must not see moves on Black-colored pieces"
100	    );
101	    let _ = any_non_reveal_for_black; // either path is fine — no Red is revealed yet
102	}
103	
104	#[test]
105	fn before_first_flip_current_color_falls_back_to_seat() {
106	    let state = GameState::new(RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(HouseRules::empty(), 0));
107	    assert!(state.side_assignment.is_none());
108	    assert_eq!(state.current_color(), state.side_to_move);
109	}
```

```text
1	//! Transport-agnostic room state machine.
2	//!
3	//! Extracted from `server.rs` so the same room logic can be driven by any
4	//! transport — the existing axum WebSocket server, a future WebRTC
5	//! DataChannel host running inside a PWA, an in-process test harness, etc.
6	//! See `backlog/webrtc-lan-pairing.md` Phase 1 for the motivating refactor.
7	//!
8	//! The key abstraction is [`PeerId`] — an opaque identifier the transport
9	//! assigns to each connection. [`Room::apply`] consumes a `(PeerId,
10	//! ClientMsg)` pair and returns a `Vec<Outbound>` describing exactly which
11	//! peers should receive which `ServerMsg`s. The transport layer owns the
12	//! mapping `PeerId -> outbound channel` and fans out accordingly.
13	//!
14	//! No `tokio` / `axum` / `futures` dependency — this module compiles for
15	//! `wasm32-unknown-unknown` so the chess-web PWA can host a room locally
16	//! over WebRTC DataChannels.
17	
18	// === module skeleton — section bodies filled in via edits below ===
19	
20	// SECTION: imports
21	use std::collections::VecDeque;
22	#[cfg(not(target_arch = "wasm32"))]
23	use std::time::{SystemTime, UNIX_EPOCH};
24	
25	use chess_core::moves::Move;
26	use chess_core::piece::Side;
27	use chess_core::rules::RuleSet;
28	use chess_core::state::{GameState, GameStatus, WinReason};
29	use chess_core::view::PlayerView;
30	
31	use crate::protocol::{
32	    variant_label, ChatLine, ClientMsg, RoomStatus, RoomSummary, ServerMsg, PROTOCOL_VERSION,
33	};
34	// SECTION: constants
35	
36	/// Names a server's persistent default room. Kept as a `pub const` so the
37	/// transport layer can decide whether GC applies (the v1 default room is
38	/// never GC'd; user-created rooms are).
39	pub const DEFAULT_ROOM: &str = "main";
40	
41	/// Hard cap on `?password=` length to keep server log noise bounded.
42	pub const MAX_PASSWORD_LEN: usize = 64;
43	
44	/// Hard cap on `:room_id` length; mirrors what the URL extractor accepts.
45	pub const MAX_ROOM_ID_LEN: usize = 32;
46	
47	/// Trim chat lines server-side so a noisy client cannot fill the ring.
48	pub const MAX_CHAT_LEN: usize = 256;
49	
50	/// Per-room chat ring buffer capacity. Sent verbatim to every new joiner
51	/// via `ChatHistory`.
52	pub const CHAT_HISTORY_CAP: usize = 50;
53	
54	/// Default per-room cap on `?role=spectator` connections. The transport
55	/// can pick a different value (see `ServeOpts::with_max_spectators`); the
56	/// `Room` itself takes the cap as an explicit argument so it has no
57	/// hidden global state.
58	pub const DEFAULT_MAX_SPECTATORS: usize = 16;
59	// SECTION: peer + outbound types
60	
61	/// Opaque per-connection identifier. The `Room` does not allocate these —
62	/// the transport layer assigns one per accepted connection (e.g. a monotonic
63	/// counter on the axum server, or a random u64 on the WebRTC host) and is
64	/// responsible for keeping the `PeerId -> outbound channel` mapping.
65	///
66	/// The room only needs `Eq` + `Copy`, never inspects the inner value, and
67	/// makes no assumptions about uniqueness across rooms — only within a single
68	/// room's lifetime.
69	#[derive(Copy, Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Hash, Ord, PartialOrd)]
70	pub struct PeerId(pub u64);
71	
72	/// Role this peer occupies inside the room.
73	#[derive(Copy, Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq)]
74	pub enum SeatRole {
75	    Player(Side),
76	    Spectator,
77	}
78	
79	/// One outbound message the transport must deliver to a specific peer.
80	///
81	/// We intentionally pre-project per-peer payloads inside the `Room` (e.g.
82	/// each seated player gets their own [`PlayerView`] in `Update`) rather
83	/// than expose a fan-out enum. This keeps the transport glue trivial — it
84	/// just iterates the returned vec and `send`s each `msg` to `peer` — at
85	/// the cost of cloning chat lines / non-projected messages once per
86	/// recipient. Acceptable: rooms cap at 2 seats + a handful of spectators.
87	///
88	/// `PartialEq` is for tests only — `ServerMsg` doesn't itself implement
89	/// `Eq` (some payloads carry `f32` win-rates) so `Outbound` cannot derive
90	/// `Eq`. Tests use the field-by-field check via `assert!(matches!(..))`
91	/// patterns.
92	#[derive(Clone, Debug)]
93	pub struct Outbound {
94	    pub peer: PeerId,
95	    pub msg: ServerMsg,
96	}
97	
98	/// Result of attempting to seat a peer in a room.
99	#[derive(Copy, Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq)]
100	pub enum JoinError {
101	    /// Both seats already taken.
102	    RoomFull,
103	    /// Spectator slots full (parameter to [`Room::join_spectator`]).
104	    SpectatorCapReached,
105	}
106	// SECTION: room struct
107	
108	/// Authoritative per-room state plus its peer roster.
109	///
110	/// Replaces the pre-refactor `server::RoomState`, with two changes:
111	///
112	/// 1. Senders are out — peers are tracked by [`PeerId`] only. The transport
113	///    keeps the `PeerId -> Sender` map separately and consumes the
114	///    [`Outbound`] vec returned from `apply` / `join_*` / `leave`.
115	/// 2. Logging side-effects (`eprintln!`) moved to the transport layer
116	///    so this module compiles for `wasm32-unknown-unknown` without dragging
117	///    in stderr semantics.
118	///
119	/// Locking remains the transport's responsibility (the axum server wraps
120	/// each `Room` in `Arc<Mutex<...>>`, the WebRTC host can use a `RefCell`
121	/// because it's single-threaded). The `Room` API is `&mut self` for any
122	/// state-changing operation.
123	pub struct Room {
124	    state: GameState,
125	    /// (side, peer-id). Empty until first connect; capped at 2.
126	    seats: Vec<(Side, PeerId)>,
127	    /// Read-only watchers connected via `?role=spectator`. Cap is enforced
128	    /// by [`Room::join_spectator`]'s `max` argument, not stored on the
129	    /// room itself.
130	    spectators: Vec<PeerId>,
131	    /// Sides that have requested a rematch since the last reset. Cleared
132	    /// every time the game resets; mutual consent triggers reset.
133	    rematch: Vec<Side>,
134	    /// Friend-list lock. `None` = open. Set by the first joiner from the
135	    /// `?password=` query string (or equivalent in non-WS transports) and
136	    /// never mutated afterwards. **Plain text is intentional** — this is
137	    /// a "type the secret to enter the game with the right friends"
138	    /// mechanism, not a security boundary.
139	    password: Option<String>,
140	    /// AI-hint sanction. `false` = clients must NOT mount the AI debug /
141	    /// hint panel against this room. Set by the first joiner and frozen
142	    /// for the room's lifetime (same pattern as `password`). Closes the
143	    /// previous client-only `?debug=1` cheat hole.
144	    hints_allowed: bool,
145	    /// In-memory ring buffer of recent chat lines (cap [`CHAT_HISTORY_CAP`]).
146	    /// Sent verbatim to every new joiner via `ChatHistory`.
147	    chat: VecDeque<ChatLine>,
148	}
149	// SECTION: room construction + simple accessors
150	
151	impl Room {
152	    /// Create a fresh room. `password` and `hints_allowed` are set once and
153	    /// frozen — the [`Room::join_player`] / [`Room::join_spectator`]
154	    /// callers do their own password-equality check before insertion (the

156	    pub fn new(rules: RuleSet, password: Option<String>, hints_allowed: bool) -> Self {
157	        Self {
158	            state: GameState::new(rules),
159	            seats: Vec::with_capacity(2),
160	            spectators: Vec::new(),
161	            rematch: Vec::with_capacity(2),
162	            password,
163	            hints_allowed,
164	            chat: VecDeque::with_capacity(CHAT_HISTORY_CAP),
165	        }
166	    }
167	
168	    pub fn password(&self) -> Option<&str> {
169	        self.password.as_deref()
170	    }
171	
172	    pub fn hints_allowed(&self) -> bool {
173	        self.hints_allowed
174	    }
175	
176	    pub fn rules(&self) -> &RuleSet {
177	        &self.state.rules
178	    }
179	
180	    pub fn seat_count(&self) -> usize {
181	        self.seats.len()
182	    }
183	
184	    pub fn spectator_count(&self) -> usize {
185	        self.spectators.len()
186	    }
187	
188	    /// `true` when the room has no seated players AND no spectators. The
189	    /// transport uses this to GC user-created rooms (the v1 default room
190	    /// `"main"` is exempt).
191	    pub fn is_empty(&self) -> bool {
192	        self.seats.is_empty() && self.spectators.is_empty()
193	    }
```

```text
209	    pub fn summary(&self, id: &str) -> RoomSummary {
210	        let status = match self.state.status {
211	            GameStatus::Won { .. } | GameStatus::Drawn { .. } => RoomStatus::Finished,
212	            GameStatus::Ongoing => {
213	                if self.seats.len() >= 2 {
214	                    RoomStatus::Playing
215	                } else {
216	                    RoomStatus::Lobby
217	                }
218	            }
219	        };
220	        RoomSummary {
221	            id: id.to_string(),
222	            variant: variant_label(&self.state.rules).to_string(),
223	            seats: self.seats.len() as u8,
224	            spectators: self.spectators.len() as u16,
225	            has_password: self.password.is_some(),
226	            status,
227	            hints_allowed: self.hints_allowed,
228	        }
229	    }
230	
231	    fn chat_history(&self) -> Vec<ChatLine> {
232	        self.chat.iter().cloned().collect()
233	    }
234	
235	    /// What role is this peer in? `None` = unknown peer (e.g. already
236	    /// disconnected, or never joined this room).
237	    fn role_of(&self, peer: PeerId) -> Option<SeatRole> {
238	        if let Some((side, _)) = self.seats.iter().find(|(_, p)| *p == peer) {
239	            return Some(SeatRole::Player(*side));
240	        }
241	        if self.spectators.contains(&peer) {
242	            return Some(SeatRole::Spectator);
243	        }
244	        None
245	    }
246	}
247	// SECTION: join_player
248	
249	impl Room {
250	    /// Seat a peer as a player. Returns the assigned [`Side`] plus the
251	    /// initial outbound list (Hello + ChatHistory) the transport should
252	    /// deliver to this peer immediately.
253	    ///
254	    /// Errors with [`JoinError::RoomFull`] when both seats are taken; the
255	    /// transport is expected to send `Error{"room full"}` (or transport
256	    /// equivalent) and close the connection.
257	    pub fn join_player(&mut self, peer: PeerId) -> Result<(Side, Vec<Outbound>), JoinError> {
258	        let side = self.next_seat().ok_or(JoinError::RoomFull)?;
259	        let view = PlayerView::project(&self.state, side);
260	        let out = vec![
261	            Outbound {
262	                peer,
263	                msg: ServerMsg::Hello {
264	                    protocol: PROTOCOL_VERSION,
265	                    observer: side,
266	                    rules: self.state.rules.clone(),
267	                    view,
268	                    hints_allowed: self.hints_allowed,
269	                },
270	            },
271	            Outbound { peer, msg: ServerMsg::ChatHistory { lines: self.chat_history() } },
272	        ];
273	        self.seats.push((side, peer));
274	        Ok((side, out))
275	    }
276	}
277	// SECTION: join_spectator
278	
279	impl Room {
280	    /// Seat a peer as a read-only spectator. `max` is the cap the transport
281	    /// wants to enforce (typically [`DEFAULT_MAX_SPECTATORS`]); the room
282	    /// stores no global cap of its own. Returns the initial outbound list
283	    /// (Spectating + ChatHistory).
284	    ///
285	    /// Spectators see the board from RED's POV — `PlayerView::project` is
286	    /// leak-safe so banqi hidden tiles stay opaque. Three-kingdom would
287	    /// need a neutral projection later (out of scope).
288	    pub fn join_spectator(&mut self, peer: PeerId, max: usize) -> Result<Vec<Outbound>, JoinError> {
289	        if self.spectators.len() >= max {
290	            return Err(JoinError::SpectatorCapReached);
291	        }
292	        let view = PlayerView::project(&self.state, Side::RED);
293	        let out = vec![
294	            Outbound {
295	                peer,
296	                msg: ServerMsg::Spectating {
297	                    protocol: PROTOCOL_VERSION,
298	                    rules: self.state.rules.clone(),
299	                    view,
300	                    hints_allowed: self.hints_allowed,
301	                },
302	            },
303	            Outbound { peer, msg: ServerMsg::ChatHistory { lines: self.chat_history() } },
304	        ];
305	        self.spectators.push(peer);
306	        Ok(out)
307	    }
308	}
309	// SECTION: leave
310	
311	impl Room {
312	    /// Remove a peer (player or spectator). Returns outbound messages the
313	    /// transport should fire — for example, mid-game opponent-disconnect
314	    /// notifications to surviving seats. The peer's own outbound channel
315	    /// is never targeted (the connection is closing).
316	    ///
317	    /// No-op when `peer` isn't recognised (already left, or never joined).
318	    pub fn leave(&mut self, peer: PeerId) -> Vec<Outbound> {
319	        let role = match self.role_of(peer) {
320	            Some(r) => r,
321	            None => return Vec::new(),
322	        };
323	        let mut out = Vec::new();
324	        match role {
325	            SeatRole::Player(side) => {
326	                self.seats.retain(|(_, p)| *p != peer);
327	                self.rematch.retain(|s| *s != side);
328	                if matches!(self.state.status, GameStatus::Ongoing) && !self.seats.is_empty() {
329	                    for (_, surviving) in &self.seats {
330	                        out.push(Outbound {
331	                            peer: *surviving,
332	                            msg: ServerMsg::Error { message: "opponent disconnected".into() },
333	                        });
334	                    }
335	                }
336	            }
337	            SeatRole::Spectator => {
338	                self.spectators.retain(|p| *p != peer);
339	            }
340	        }
341	        out
342	    }
343	}
344	// SECTION: apply (entry point)
345	
346	impl Room {
347	    /// Process an incoming `ClientMsg` from `peer`. Returns the outbound
348	    /// fan-out the transport should deliver. Unknown peers (not seated,
349	    /// not spectating) get a single `Error` reply addressed to themselves
350	    /// — the transport is free to log + close, or just discard.
351	    ///
352	    /// All seat / spectator gating (e.g. "spectators cannot move") happens
353	    /// inside this function; the transport does not need to inspect the
354	    /// message kind.
355	    pub fn apply(&mut self, peer: PeerId, msg: ClientMsg) -> Vec<Outbound> {
356	        let role = match self.role_of(peer) {
357	            Some(r) => r,
358	            None => {
359	                return vec![Outbound {
360	                    peer,
361	                    msg: ServerMsg::Error { message: "unknown peer".into() },
362	                }];
363	            }
364	        };
365	        let seat = match role {
366	            SeatRole::Player(s) => Some(s),
367	            SeatRole::Spectator => None,
368	        };
369	        let mut out = Vec::new();
370	        match msg {
371	            ClientMsg::Move { mv } => match seat {
372	                Some(s) => {
373	                    if !matches!(self.state.status, GameStatus::Ongoing) {
374	                        out.push(Outbound {
375	                            peer,
376	                            msg: ServerMsg::Error {
377	                                message: "game is over — press 'n' to request a rematch".into(),
378	                            },
379	                        });
380	                    } else {
381	                        self.process_move(s, mv, peer, &mut out);
382	                    }
383	                }
384	                None => out.push(Outbound {
385	                    peer,
386	                    msg: ServerMsg::Error { message: "spectators cannot move".into() },
387	                }),
388	            },
389	            ClientMsg::Resign => match seat {
390	                Some(s) => {
391	                    if !matches!(self.state.status, GameStatus::Ongoing) {
392	                        out.push(Outbound {
393	                            peer,
394	                            msg: ServerMsg::Error { message: "game is over".into() },
395	                        });
396	                    } else {
397	                        let winner = if s == Side::RED { Side::BLACK } else { Side::RED };
398	                        self.state.status =
399	                            GameStatus::Won { winner, reason: WinReason::Resignation };
400	                        self.broadcast_update(&mut out);
401	                    }
402	                }
403	                None => out.push(Outbound {
404	                    peer,
405	                    msg: ServerMsg::Error { message: "spectators cannot resign".into() },
406	                }),
407	            },
408	            ClientMsg::Rematch => match seat {
409	                Some(s) => self.process_rematch(s, peer, &mut out),
410	                None => out.push(Outbound {
411	                    peer,
412	                    msg: ServerMsg::Error { message: "spectators cannot request a rematch".into() },
413	                }),
414	            },
415	            ClientMsg::Chat { text } => match seat {
416	                Some(s) => self.process_chat(s, text, peer, &mut out),
417	                None => out.push(Outbound {
418	                    peer,
419	                    msg: ServerMsg::Error { message: "spectators cannot chat".into() },
420	                }),
421	            },
422	            ClientMsg::ListRooms => out.push(Outbound {
423	                peer,
424	                msg: ServerMsg::Error {
425	                    message: "ListRooms is a lobby-only message; subscribe via /lobby".into(),
426	                },
427	            }),
428	        }
429	        out
430	    }
431	}
432	// SECTION: process_move
433	
434	impl Room {
435	    fn process_move(&mut self, seat: Side, mv: Move, from: PeerId, out: &mut Vec<Outbound>) {
436	        if self.state.side_to_move != seat {
437	            out.push(Outbound {
438	                peer: from,
439	                msg: ServerMsg::Error { message: "not your turn".into() },
440	            });
441	            return;
442	        }
443	        match self.state.make_move(&mv) {
444	            Ok(()) => {
445	                self.state.refresh_status();
446	                self.broadcast_update(out);
447	            }
448	            Err(e) => {
449	                out.push(Outbound {
450	                    peer: from,
451	                    msg: ServerMsg::Error { message: format!("illegal move: {e}") },
452	                });
453	            }
454	        }
455	    }
456	}
457	// SECTION: process_resign — inlined into `apply` (3-line state mutation +
458	// broadcast_update); kept here only as a section marker for the section list
459	// at the top of the file.
460	
461	// SECTION: process_rematch
462	
463	impl Room {
464	    fn process_rematch(&mut self, seat: Side, from: PeerId, out: &mut Vec<Outbound>) {
465	        if matches!(self.state.status, GameStatus::Ongoing) {
466	            out.push(Outbound {
467	                peer: from,
468	                msg: ServerMsg::Error {
469	                    message: "Game still in progress — finish or resign first.".into(),
470	                },
471	            });
472	            return;
473	        }
474	        if self.seats.len() < 2 {
475	            out.push(Outbound {
476	                peer: from,
477	                msg: ServerMsg::Error {
478	                    message: "No opponent connected — can't start a rematch.".into(),
479	                },
480	            });
481	            return;
482	        }
483	        if !self.rematch.contains(&seat) {
484	            self.rematch.push(seat);
485	        }
486	        let want_seats: Vec<Side> = self.seats.iter().map(|(s, _)| *s).collect();
487	        let all_ready = want_seats.iter().all(|s| self.rematch.contains(s));
488	        if all_ready {
489	            let rules = self.state.rules.clone();
490	            self.state = GameState::new(rules);
491	            self.rematch.clear();
492	            // Re-Hello every seated player from their own POV.
493	            for (side, peer) in &self.seats {
494	                let view = PlayerView::project(&self.state, *side);
495	                out.push(Outbound {
496	                    peer: *peer,
497	                    msg: ServerMsg::Hello {
498	                        protocol: PROTOCOL_VERSION,
499	                        observer: *side,
```

```text
1	//! Pieces and sides.
2	
3	use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
4	
5	/// Player side. Two-player games use 0 and 1; 三國暗棋 uses 0, 1, 2.
6	///
7	/// Wraps a small int rather than a fixed enum so 3-player isn't a special case.
8	#[derive(Copy, Clone, Eq, PartialEq, Hash, Serialize, Deserialize, Debug)]
9	#[repr(transparent)]
10	pub struct Side(pub u8);
11	
12	impl Side {
13	    pub const RED: Side = Side(0);
14	    pub const BLACK: Side = Side(1);
15	    /// Third faction in 三國暗棋. Maps to 蜀/吳/魏 externally as the variant chooses.
16	    pub const GREEN: Side = Side(2);
17	
18	    #[inline]
19	    pub const fn raw(self) -> u8 {
20	        self.0
21	    }
22	
23	    /// 2-player only: the opposing side.
24	    #[inline]
25	    pub const fn opposite(self) -> Side {
26	        Side(self.0 ^ 1)
27	    }
28	}
29	
30	#[derive(Copy, Clone, Eq, PartialEq, Hash, Serialize, Deserialize, Debug)]
31	pub enum PieceKind {
32	    /// 將 / 帥
33	    General,
34	    /// 士 / 仕
35	    Advisor,
36	    /// 象 / 相
37	    Elephant,
38	    /// 車 / 俥
39	    Chariot,
40	    /// 馬 / 傌
41	    Horse,
42	    /// 炮 / 砲
43	    Cannon,
44	    /// 卒 / 兵
45	    Soldier,
46	}
47	
48	impl PieceKind {
49	    /// Banqi capture rank. Higher beats lower (with the cannon and
```

```text
Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding. Let me compile my report.

---

## Banqi Client-Side Rendering and Gating Logic — Final Report

### A. Chess-TUI (`clients/chess-tui/src/`)

**"You play Red/Black" rendering and perspective:**
- **Sidebar status:** `ui.rs` line 1882–1893. The turn label uses `observer_side` (from `NetView.role`) to determine "Your turn:" vs "Opponent:" vs "To move:". The color shown is `view.current_color` (line 1876, 1890), which post-first-flip in banqi reflects the piece color the seat plays, not the seat name.
- **TUI orientation:** `orient.rs` (duplication of chess-web's). Red observer sees rank 0 at bottom; Black observer sees it flipped. Banqi transposes to 4×8 display layout via `project_cell` (line 37: for banqi, swaps rank/file).
- **Local vs Net distinction:** 
  - Local (pass-and-play): `GameView` has `observer: Side` (set on line 704, defaults to RED for new games). No `NetRole` — the TUI just shows "Current player (Red/Black) to move" without a seat-assignment prefix.
  - Net mode: `NetView` stores `role: Option<NetRole>` (NetRole::Player(Side) or Spectator). Line 1877–1880 gates the label generation — only players with `role == Some(NetRole::Player(_))` see "Your turn:" vs "Opponent:".

**Side-to-move gating in Net mode:**
- `dispatch_net` (line 2133+): Before applying a move, line 2256 checks `view.legal_moves.iter().any(|m| m.origin_square() == sq)` to validate legality. Spectators are blocked at line 2137–2146 (no `NetRole::Player` means no dispatch on moves).
- **Reveal move in Net mode** (line 2198): Sends `Move::Reveal { at: sq, revealed: None }` only when the square is in `legal_moves` (which the server filters by `side_to_move` and banqi seat color).

**Local pass-and-play banqi tile flips:**
- `dispatch_game` line 1882–1883: Creates `Move::Reveal { at: sq, revealed: None }` when the user presses 'f' at a hidden tile. **No side-based restriction** — the engine's move-gen handles gating. After the first flip locks `side_assignment`, the engine only emits reveals for the current seat's controlled color (confirmed by test `/crates/chess-core/tests/banqi_first_flip_color.rs` line 67–100).

**Is first move required to be Reveal by Red?**
- Test `banqi_first_flip_color.rs` line 39: "banqi always starts with RED seat". No `banqi_seed` or rule config changes this. The server-side engine always initializes `side_to_move = Side::RED` (core/src/setup.rs line 29, 95).
- **Current behavior:** Red must move/flip first. There is no enforcement of "first move must be Reveal" — Red *could* theoretically move a piece if pieces were already revealed, but that's not the case on banqi's initial setup. The engine only exposes legal reveals (all tiles are hidden on move 0).

### B. Chess-Web (`clients/chess-web/src/`)

**"You play X" rendering and ClientRole:**
- **Sidebar status:** `pages/play.rs` lines 608–614. `role_label` is a closure matching `ClientRole::Player(Side::RED)` → "You play Red 紅", `Player(Side::BLACK)` → "You play Black 黑", `Spectator` → "Spectator — read-only", `None` → "Awaiting seat assignment".
- **Turn label:** `turn_label` (lines 629–637) shows `current_color` to move, but **does NOT** indicate whose turn based on role. That's left to the player to infer from comparing their assigned `role` with the displayed `current_color`.
- **ClientRole enum:** `state.rs` lines 16–38. Three variants: `Player(Side)`, `Spectator`. Methods: `is_player()`, `is_spectator()`, `observer()` (spectators default to RED POV).

**Click-gating in Net mode:**
- **Spectators blocked:** `pages/play.rs` line 461–462: if `is_spectator`, return immediately.
- **Not-your-turn blocked:** Lines 464–467: if `v.observer != v.side_to_move` (seat is not to move), return.
- **Reveal click:** Lines 504–508. Hidden tiles call `find_move(&v, sq, sq)` to locate a `Move::Reveal { at }` in `legal_moves`. If found, send immediately; engine has already filtered by color.

**Local mode (pass-and-play):**
- **Observer fixed to RED:** Line 271–273. In vs-AI, observer is `ai_side.opposite()` (player sees from their own perspective); in pure pass-and-play, `observer = Side::RED` (fixed board orientation).
- **Click gating:** Lines 398–470. `player_can_act` signal (line 399, controls whether UI is responsive) is always true in local (no socket/role check). Reveal at hidden tile (line 460–467): `find_move(&v, sq, sq)` for `Move::Reveal { at }`. Legal moves are server-side filtered (the local `GameState` enforces piece-color rules just like networked).

### C. First-Flip Handling — Current Behavior

- **Red always forced to flip first:** Game initializes with `side_to_move = Side::RED` (immutable in both clients). Banqi rules allow any party to flip *any* hidden tile on their turn; the first flip's revealed color determines the seat-color mapping.
- **Second player cannot flip first:** The engine's move-gen enforces turn order. Even if `side_to_move` advanced to BLACK immediately (which it doesn't), only BLACK-seat's legal moves are in `legal_moves` — limiting reveals to pieces of BLACK's assigned color.
- **No "first move must be Reveal" rule:** The engine allows any legal move (in practice, only reveals exist until one tile is flipped). There is no explicit validation blocking non-reveal moves at ply 0.

### D. Banner / Status Text for Banqi

**Check badge rendering:**
- **TUI:** `ui.rs` line 1898: `if show_check_banner && matches!(view.shape, BoardShape::Xiangqi9x10) && view.in_check`. Banqi never shows the badge because the condition restricts to Xiangqi9x10 and `view.in_check` is always false for banqi (no general).
- **Web:** `components/sidebar.rs` line 108–124 and `pages/play.rs` line 671–685. Same logic: the check badge appears only when `v.in_check` is true. Banqi's `PlayerView` has `in_check = false` (server always returns false per protocol for non-xiangqi variants).

**Status text for banqi:**
- **TUI sidebar:** Lines 1876–1893 build the turn line showing `view.current_color` (Red/Black/Green seat name after side_assignment locks). No variant-specific copy; same display as xiangqi.
- **Web sidebar:** Lines 630–635 show `current_color` (Red/Black/Green). No banqi-specific messaging.

---

### Key Call Sites to Update for Graceful Thompson Revelation Rule

For your "any player can flip first" feature, you'll need to gate on move 0 only:

1. **Server-side engine** (`crates/chess-core`): Modify move-gen to allow both `side_to_move == RED` and `side_to_move == BLACK` to emit `Move::Reveal` on ply 0 (before `side_assignment` locks). Currently only RED's reveals are legal.
2. **TUI & web clients**: No changes required — they already gate on `legal_moves`, which the server populates. Once the server allows BLACK's reveals at ply 0, the UI will automatically permit them.
3. **Status text**: Consider adding a message for banqi's pre-flip state (e.g., "Awaiting first reveal — either side may flip"). Line 613 (web) or equivalent in TUI could emit that until ply > 0.
```

```text
542	        &mut self,
543	        from_side: Side,

545	        from: PeerId,
546	        out: &mut Vec<Outbound>,
547	    ) {
548	        let cleaned: String = raw
549	            .chars()
550	            .filter(|c| !c.is_control() || *c == '\n' || *c == '\t')
551	            .map(|c| if c == '\n' || c == '\t' { ' ' } else { c })
552	            .collect();
553	        let trimmed = cleaned.trim();
554	        if trimmed.is_empty() {
555	            out.push(Outbound {
556	                peer: from,
557	                msg: ServerMsg::Error { message: "chat message is empty".into() },
558	            });
559	            return;
560	        }

562	        text.shrink_to_fit();
563	        let line = ChatLine { from: from_side, text, ts_ms: now_ms() };
564	        if self.chat.len() >= CHAT_HISTORY_CAP {
565	            self.chat.pop_front();
566	        }
567	        self.chat.push_back(line.clone());

570	        // same payload — `Update` uses `broadcast_update` instead because
571	        // seats need per-side projections.

573	        for (_, peer) in &self.seats {
574	            out.push(Outbound { peer: *peer, msg: msg.clone() });

577	            out.push(Outbound { peer: *peer, msg: msg.clone() });

582	/// Unix milliseconds source. Native uses `SystemTime`; wasm32 uses
583	/// `js_sys::Date::now()` because `SystemTime::now()` panics on wasm
584	/// with "time not implemented on this platform" — discovered when
585	/// `host_room.rs` started running `Room` in-browser for LAN play and
586	/// the first chat message panicked the entire fanout (silently
587	/// dropping the chat broadcast). Returns `0` only on the impossible
588	/// `SystemTimeError` (CI clock before the unix epoch).
589	fn now_ms() -> u64 {
590	    #[cfg(not(target_arch = "wasm32"))]
591	    {
592	        SystemTime::now().duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH).map(|d| d.as_millis() as u64).unwrap_or(0)
593	    }
594	    #[cfg(target_arch = "wasm32")]
595	    {
596	        // `Date::now()` returns f64 millis since unix epoch. Cast to
597	        // u64 — within the next ~285,000,000 years this won't overflow.
598	        js_sys::Date::now() as u64
599	    }

604	    /// Push one `Update` per recipient: each seated player gets a
```

Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive view of the protocol and room logic. Let me create a summary report.

## Report: Chess-Net Seat/Side Assignment for Banqi First-Flip Rule Change

### A. Protocol (`crates/chess-net/src/protocol.rs`, lines 49–88)

**Protocol version**: `PROTOCOL_VERSION = 5` (line 49)

**`ServerMsg::Hello` (lines 60–67)**:
```rust
Hello {
    protocol: u16,
    observer: Side,              // ← ASSIGNED SIDE (RED or BLACK)
    rules: RuleSet,
    view: PlayerView,
    #[serde(default)]
    hints_allowed: bool,
}
```
The field is named `observer` (not `your_side`), type `Side` (not `Option<Side>`). This is the pre-assigned seat before any piece reveal.

**`ServerMsg::Spectating` (lines 82–88)**:
```rust
Spectating {
    protocol: u16,
    rules: RuleSet,
    view: PlayerView,
    #[serde(default)]
    hints_allowed: bool,
}
```
No `observer` field—spectators are unseated. Board is **always projected from `Side::RED`** (line 79 comment, line 292 in room.rs).

**`ClientMsg` enum (lines 100–122)**: Only `Move`, `Resign`, `Rematch`, `Chat`, and `ListRooms`—no "declare role" message. Side is baked at join time.

### B. Server/Room (`crates/chess-net/src/room.rs`)

**Seat assignment logic** (lines 198–206):
```rust
pub fn next_seat(&self) -> Option<Side> {
    match self.seats.len() {
        0 => Some(Side::RED),              // First joiner → RED
        1 => {
            let taken = self.seats[0].0;
            Some(if taken == Side::RED { Side::BLACK } else { Side::RED })
        }
        _ => None,                          // Room full
    }
}
```
Deterministic: first joiner always RED, second always BLACK. Third joiner gets `JoinError::RoomFull`.

**`join_player` (lines 257–275)**: Calls `next_seat()` to assign side, then emits `ServerMsg::Hello { observer: side }` immediately.

**`RoomState` / `Room` struct (lines 123–148)**:
```rust
pub struct Room {
    state: GameState,                  // Core board + game status
    seats: Vec<(Side, PeerId)>,        // (assigned side, peer id)
    spectators: Vec<PeerId>,
    rematch: Vec<Side>,
    password: Option<String>,
    hints_allowed: bool,
    chat: VecDeque<ChatLine>,
}
```

### C. Move Validation & Game-Flow

**Move guard** (lines 435–442):
```rust
fn process_move(&mut self, seat: Side, mv: Move, from: PeerId, out: &mut Vec<Outbound>) {
    if self.state.side_to_move != seat {
        out.push(Outbound {
            peer: from,
            msg: ServerMsg::Error { message: "not your turn".into() },
        });
        return;
    }
    // ... make_move() and broadcast_update()
}
```
Guard checks that `move_from_side` (the `seat: Side` parameter) matches `state.side_to_move`. This is enforced **after** the seat role check in `apply()` (line 356–368).

**Broadcast after move** (lines 603–619):
```rust
fn broadcast_update(&self, out: &mut Vec<Outbound>) {
    for (side, peer) in &self.seats {
        let view = PlayerView::project(&self.state, *side);  // Each player sees their POV
        out.push(Outbound { peer: *peer, msg: ServerMsg::Update { view } });
    }
    if !self.spectators.is_empty() {
        let view = PlayerView::project(&self.state, Side::RED);  // All spectators see RED POV
        for peer in &self.spectators {
            out.push(Outbound { peer: *peer, msg: ServerMsg::Update { view: view.clone() } });
        }
    }
}
```
Each seated player gets a **per-side projection**; all spectators share a **RED-only projection**.

### D. Spectator Path

**Join spectator** (lines 288–307): Always projects from `Side::RED` (line 292). No side assignment—`Spectating` message carries no `observer` field.

---

### Impact for First-Flip Rule

For banqi where color is determined on first piece reveal:

1. **Protocol change needed**: `observer: Side` → `observer: Option<Side>` (or new enum `role: PlayerRole { Unassigned | Assigned(Side) }`). Wire-compatible with `#[serde(default)]` if you default `Option<Side>` to `None`.

2. **Room state**: Keep seat assignment deterministic (RED first, BLACK second) for routing, but **delay broadcasting `Hello`** until the first `Reveal` move. On reveal, assign colors (side_to_move alternates; first revealer gets the non-moving color, or vice versa per rule). Emit retroactive `Hello` with assigned `observer`.

3. **Move validation**: `process_move` must allow **either seat** to issue `Move::Reveal` when `self.state.side_to_move` is unassigned or defer turn-check until post-reveal.

4. **Spectator projection**: Always RED—no change needed (spectators see the banqi reveal sequence from one POV).

5. **Rematch**: Re-Hello after reset must preserve seat→side mapping (same as today) unless you want to re-flip for each rematch.

**Line references**:
- Protocol: `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-05-06-chinese-chess/crates/chess-net/src/protocol.rs:49, 60–67, 82–88`
- Seat logic: `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-05-06-chinese-chess/crates/chess-net/src/room.rs:198–206, 257–275`
- Move guard: `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-05-06-chinese-chess/crates/chess-net/src/room.rs:435–442`
- Broadcast: `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-05-06-chinese-chess/crates/chess-net/src/room.rs:603–619`
