```text
46	// SECTION: ICE diag rendering helpers
47	
48	fn ice_label(s: RtcIceConnectionState) -> &'static str {
49	    match s {
50	        RtcIceConnectionState::New => "new",
51	        RtcIceConnectionState::Checking => "checking",
52	        RtcIceConnectionState::Connected => "connected",
53	        RtcIceConnectionState::Completed => "completed",
54	        RtcIceConnectionState::Failed => "failed",
55	        RtcIceConnectionState::Disconnected => "disconnected",
56	        RtcIceConnectionState::Closed => "closed",
57	        _ => "?",
58	    }
59	}
60	
61	fn conn_label(s: RtcPeerConnectionState) -> &'static str {
62	    match s {
63	        RtcPeerConnectionState::New => "new",
64	        RtcPeerConnectionState::Connecting => "connecting",
65	        RtcPeerConnectionState::Connected => "connected",
66	        RtcPeerConnectionState::Disconnected => "disconnected",
67	        RtcPeerConnectionState::Failed => "failed",
68	        RtcPeerConnectionState::Closed => "closed",
69	        _ => "?",
70	    }
71	}
72	
73	fn gather_label(s: RtcIceGatheringState) -> &'static str {
74	    match s {
75	        RtcIceGatheringState::New => "new",
76	        RtcIceGatheringState::Gathering => "gathering",
77	        RtcIceGatheringState::Complete => "complete",
78	        _ => "?",
79	    }
80	}
81	
82	/// Format an [`IceDiag`] snapshot as a single line for the inline badge.
83	fn diag_line(d: &IceDiag) -> String {
84	    format!(
85	        "ICE: {} · connection: {} · gathering: {} · candidates: {}",
86	        ice_label(d.ice),
87	        conn_label(d.conn),
88	        gather_label(d.gather),
89	        d.candidates,
90	    )
91	}
92	
93	/// Hint string appended to the 10-s DC-open timeout error, varied by
94	/// what we can infer from the host's own SDP candidates. Surfacing
95	/// "your VPN is hijacking the LAN" instead of the generic mDNS hint
96	/// when the host candidate IP betrays a VPN tunnel saves the user
97	/// from blaming the wrong layer.
98	fn timeout_hint_for(sdp: &str) -> &'static str {
99	    match classify(&parse_candidate_addrs(sdp)) {
100	        NetDiag::VpnTunnel => {
101	            "VPN tunnel detected (host candidate is in 198.18.0.0/15). \
102	             DISABLE the VPN on both devices and retry — VPNs route LAN \
103	             traffic through the tunnel and replace your real LAN IP \
104	             with a fake address. If the VPN must stay on, configure \
105	             split-tunnel to bypass 192.168.0.0/16, 10.0.0.0/8, \
106	             172.16.0.0/12, 172.20.10.0/28 (iOS hotspot), and \
107	             224.0.0.0/4 (multicast / mDNS)."
108	        }
109	        NetDiag::Cgnat => {
110	            "CGNAT detected (host candidate is in 100.64.0.0/10). \
111	             Your ISP / carrier is using Carrier-Grade NAT, which often \
112	             blocks direct P2P even with STUN. Try an iPhone/Android \
113	             personal hotspot instead."
114	        }
115	        NetDiag::Plain => {
116	            "Common fixes: switch both devices to an iPhone/Android \
117	             personal hotspot (this network's WebRTC mDNS resolution \
118	             may be failing), or enable \"Use STUN\"."
119	        }
120	    }
121	}
122	// SECTION: LanHostPage component
123	
124	#[component]
125	pub fn LanHostPage() -> impl IntoView {
126	    // ── Page state ────────────────────────────────────────────
127	    // We progress through:
128	    //   Idle → Generating → AwaitingAnswer → AcceptingAnswer →
129	    //   WaitingForChannel → Playing
130	    // Each transition mutates the corresponding signals; the view
131	    // renders accordingly.
132	    let (status, set_status) = create_signal::<HostStatus>(HostStatus::Idle);
133	    let (use_stun, set_use_stun) = create_signal::<bool>(false);
134	    let (offer_blob, set_offer_blob) = create_signal::<String>(String::new());
135	    let (answer_input, set_answer_input) = create_signal::<String>(String::new());
136	    let (error_msg, set_error_msg) = create_signal::<Option<String>>(None);
137	
138	    // ── Variant + rules form state ─────────────────────────────
139	    // Mirrors picker.rs::BanqiCard: one bool signal per HouseRules
140	    // bitflag (rather than one signal holding the whole bitset) so
141	    // each checkbox can bind directly to `prop:checked` without a
142	    // closure dance. `apply_preset` flips all five at once.
143	    //
144	    // Initial values come from the URL query string (`?variant=banqi
145	    // &house=chain,rush&seed=42`) via the same `parse_local_rules`
146	    // parser the picker uses. Lets a future picker "Host on LAN"
147	    // button deep-link a fully-configured /lan/host without a fresh
148	    // parser; today, hand-typed URLs are the only consumer.
149	    let query = use_query_map();
150	    let initial_variant = query.with_untracked(|q| {
151	        q.get("variant").and_then(|s| parse_variant_slug(s)).unwrap_or(Variant::Xiangqi)
152	    });
153	    // Three-kingdom is 3-player; not a valid LAN choice — clamp to xiangqi.
154	    let initial_variant = match initial_variant {
155	        Variant::ThreeKingdomBanqi => Variant::Xiangqi,
156	        v => v,
157	    };
158	    let initial_params: LocalRulesParams =
159	        query.with_untracked(|q| parse_local_rules(|k| q.get(k).cloned()));
160	
161	    let variant = create_rw_signal(initial_variant);
162	    let strict = create_rw_signal(initial_params.strict);
163	    let chain = create_rw_signal(initial_params.house.contains(HouseRules::CHAIN_CAPTURE));
164	    let dark = create_rw_signal(initial_params.house.contains(HouseRules::DARK_CAPTURE));
165	    let dark_trade =
166	        create_rw_signal(initial_params.house.contains(HouseRules::DARK_CAPTURE_TRADE));
167	    let rush = create_rw_signal(initial_params.house.contains(HouseRules::CHARIOT_RUSH));
168	    let horse = create_rw_signal(initial_params.house.contains(HouseRules::HORSE_DIAGONAL));
169	    let seed_text =
170	        create_rw_signal(initial_params.seed.map(|s| s.to_string()).unwrap_or_default());
171	
172	    // Snapshot of the RuleSet that was passed to HostRoom::new — set
173	    // once on successful `on_accept`. Drives the post-Idle "Playing:
174	    // …" status line that replaces the form once pairing starts.
175	    let (chosen_rules, set_chosen_rules) = create_signal::<Option<RuleSet>>(None);
176	
177	    let apply_preset = move |preset: HouseRules| {
178	        chain.set(preset.contains(HouseRules::CHAIN_CAPTURE));
179	        dark.set(preset.contains(HouseRules::DARK_CAPTURE));
180	        dark_trade.set(preset.contains(HouseRules::DARK_CAPTURE_TRADE));
181	        rush.set(preset.contains(HouseRules::CHARIOT_RUSH));
182	        horse.set(preset.contains(HouseRules::HORSE_DIAGONAL));
183	    };
184	
185	    let build_params = move || -> LocalRulesParams {
186	        let mut house = HouseRules::empty();
187	        if chain.get_untracked() {
188	            house.insert(HouseRules::CHAIN_CAPTURE);
189	        }
190	        if dark.get_untracked() {
191	            house.insert(HouseRules::DARK_CAPTURE);
192	        }
193	        if dark_trade.get_untracked() {
194	            house.insert(HouseRules::DARK_CAPTURE_TRADE);
195	        }
196	        if rush.get_untracked() {
197	            house.insert(HouseRules::CHARIOT_RUSH);
198	        }
199	        if horse.get_untracked() {
200	            house.insert(HouseRules::HORSE_DIAGONAL);
201	        }
202	        let seed = seed_text.with_untracked(|s| s.trim().parse::<u64>().ok());
203	        LocalRulesParams { strict: strict.get_untracked(), house, seed, ..Default::default() }
204	    };
205	
206	    // QR scanner modal state. `cam_available` is set asynchronously
207	    // on mount; default false to keep the Scan-camera button hidden
208	    // until detection completes (no flash-of-button-then-disappear
209	    // for cameraless devices).
210	    let (scanner_open, set_scanner_open) = create_signal::<bool>(false);
211	    let (cam_available, set_cam_available) = create_signal::<bool>(false);
212	    spawn_local(async move {
213	        if has_camera().await {
214	            set_cam_available.set(true);
215	        }
216	    });
217	
218	    // Keep the in-flight HostHandshake alive in the page; closures
219	    // and accept_answer need a stable reference.
220	    let handshake: Rc<RefCell<Option<HostHandshake>>> = Rc::new(RefCell::new(None));
221	    // The HostRoom + its Session aren't constructed until the
222	    // DataChannel actually opens; on `state == Open` we instantiate
223	    // both and flip status to `Playing`.
224	    let host_room: Rc<RefCell<Option<Rc<HostRoom>>>> = Rc::new(RefCell::new(None));
225	    let (play_session, set_play_session) = create_signal::<Option<Session>>(None);
226	    // Holder for the handshake's live ICE diag signal so the inline
227	    // status badge can render it reactively. None before Open room
228	    // succeeds; Some thereafter.
229	    let (ice_diag_holder, set_ice_diag_holder) = create_signal::<Option<ReadSignal<IceDiag>>>(None);
230	
231	    // Re-snapshot the offer SDP whenever the diag signal fires (every
232	    // `oniceicecandidate` / connection / gathering state change). Catches
233	    // late-arriving srflx candidates that wouldn't be in the half-
234	    // gathered SDP captured at connect_as_host return time. See
235	    // HostHandshake::current_offer for the rationale.
236	    {
237	        let handshake = handshake.clone();
238	        create_effect(move |_| {
239	            if let Some(sig) = ice_diag_holder.get() {
240	                let _ = sig.get(); // subscribe to inner changes
241	                if let Some(hh) = handshake.borrow().as_ref() {
242	                    set_offer_blob.set(hh.current_offer().0);
243	                }
244	            }
245	        });
246	    }
247	
248	    // ── Open room (generate offer) ────────────────────────────
249	    let handshake_for_open = handshake.clone();
250	    let on_open: Callback<()> = Callback::new(move |_: ()| {
251	        if !matches!(status.get_untracked(), HostStatus::Idle) {
252	            return;
253	        }
254	        // Commit the form selection to `chosen_rules` BEFORE status
255	        // leaves Idle (which hides the form). Without this, the form
256	        // disappears immediately but the "Playing: …" summary line
257	        // doesn't appear until on_accept fires — leaving a window
258	        // with no visible record of the chosen rules. The actual
259	        // `HostRoom::new(…)` call still happens inside `on_accept`
260	        // and reads `chosen_rules.get_untracked()` to avoid drift.
261	        let rules = build_rule_set(variant.get_untracked(), &build_params());
262	        set_chosen_rules.set(Some(rules));
263	        set_error_msg.set(None);
264	        set_status.set(HostStatus::Generating);
265	        let cfg = WebRtcConfig {
266	            ice_mode: if use_stun.get_untracked() { IceMode::WithStun } else { IceMode::LanOnly },
267	        };
268	        let handshake_slot = handshake_for_open.clone();
269	        spawn_local(async move {
270	            match connect_as_host(cfg).await {
271	                Ok(hh) => {
272	                    set_offer_blob.set(hh.offer.0.clone());
273	                    set_ice_diag_holder.set(Some(hh.ice_diag));
274	                    *handshake_slot.borrow_mut() = Some(hh);
275	                    set_status.set(HostStatus::AwaitingAnswer);
276	                }
277	                Err(e) => {
278	                    set_error_msg.set(Some(format!("connect_as_host failed: {e:?}")));
279	                    set_status.set(HostStatus::Idle);
280	                }
281	            }
282	        });
283	    });
284	
285	    // ── Accept answer ──────────────────────────────────────────
286	    let handshake_for_accept = handshake.clone();
287	    let host_room_slot = host_room.clone();
288	    let on_accept: Callback<()> = Callback::new(move |_: ()| {
289	        if !matches!(status.get_untracked(), HostStatus::AwaitingAnswer) {
290	            return;
291	        }
292	        let blob = answer_input.get_untracked();
293	        if blob.trim().is_empty() {
294	            set_error_msg.set(Some("paste the joiner's answer SDP first".into()));
295	            return;
296	        }
297	        // Rules were committed in `on_open` (before the form hid).
298	        // Recover them here for the actual room construction; this
299	        // can't be None unless the open flow short-circuited, in
300	        // which case we're not in AwaitingAnswer either.
301	        let rules = match chosen_rules.get_untracked() {
302	            Some(r) => r,
303	            None => {
304	                set_error_msg.set(Some("rules not configured — reopen the page".into()));
305	                set_status.set(HostStatus::Idle);
306	                return;
307	            }
308	        };
309	        set_error_msg.set(None);
310	        set_status.set(HostStatus::AcceptingAnswer);
311	        let handshake_slot = handshake_for_accept.clone();
312	        let host_room_slot = host_room_slot.clone();
313	        spawn_local(async move {
314	            let hh = match handshake_slot.borrow_mut().take() {
315	                Some(hh) => hh,
316	                None => {
317	                    set_error_msg.set(Some("no handshake in flight".into()));
318	                    set_status.set(HostStatus::Idle);
319	                    return;
320	                }
321	            };
322	            if let Err(e) = hh.accept_answer(AnswerBlob(blob)).await {
323	                set_error_msg.set(Some(format!("accept_answer failed: {e:?}")));
324	                set_status.set(HostStatus::Idle);
325	                return;
326	            }
327	            // CRITICAL: `accept_answer` resolves as soon as
328	            // `setRemoteDescription` returns — BEFORE the SCTP
329	            // handshake completes. If we call `attach_remote_player_dc`
330	            // immediately, the `dc.send_with_str(...)` for Hello +
331	            // ChatHistory silently fails (DC ready_state is still
332	            // "connecting"). Wait for actual DC open first.
333	            let dc = match hh.dc.borrow().clone() {
334	                Some(d) => d,
335	                None => {
336	                    set_error_msg.set(Some("DataChannel slot is empty".into()));
337	                    set_status.set(HostStatus::Idle);
338	                    return;
339	                }
340	            };
341	            let ice_diag = hh.ice_diag;
342	            if !wait_for_dc_open(&dc, 10_000).await {
343	                let d = ice_diag.get_untracked();
344	                let sdp = hh.pc.local_description().map(|desc| desc.sdp()).unwrap_or_default();
345	                set_error_msg.set(Some(format!(
346	                    "DataChannel did not open within 10 s — {}. {}",
347	                    diag_line(&d),
348	                    timeout_hint_for(&sdp),
349	                )));
350	                set_status.set(HostStatus::Idle);
351	                return;
352	            }
353	            let (room, session) = HostRoom::new(rules, None, /* hints */ false);
354	            if let Err(e) = room.attach_remote_player_dc(dc) {
355	                set_error_msg.set(Some(format!("attach joiner failed: {e:?}")));
356	                set_status.set(HostStatus::Idle);
357	                return;
358	            }
359	            *host_room_slot.borrow_mut() = Some(room);
360	            set_play_session.set(Some(session));
361	            set_status.set(HostStatus::Playing);
362	        });
363	    });
364	
365	    // ── Reset (full page reload) ──────────────────────────────
366	    let on_reset: Callback<()> = Callback::new(move |_: ()| {
367	        if let Some(win) = web_sys::window() {
368	            let _ = win.location().reload();
369	        }
370	    });
371	
372	    view! {
373	        <div class="lan-page" style="max-width:720px;margin:1.5rem auto;padding:1rem">
374	            <Show
375	                when=move || matches!(status.get(), HostStatus::Playing)
376	                fallback=move || view! {
377	                    <a href=app_href("/") rel="external" class="back-link">"← Back to picker"</a>
378	                    <h1>"LAN host (WebRTC)"</h1>
379	                    <Show when=move || matches!(status.get(), HostStatus::Idle)>
380	                        <fieldset class="card-fieldset" style="margin-bottom:0.5rem">
381	                            <legend>"Game"</legend>
382	                            <label class="radio-row">
383	                                <span style="margin-right:0.5rem;min-width:5rem">"Variant"</span>
384	                                <select
385	                                    style="flex:1"
386	                                    on:change=move |ev| {
387	                                        let s = event_target_value(&ev);
388	                                        if let Some(v) = parse_variant_slug(&s) {
389	                                            variant.set(v);
390	                                        }
391	                                    }
392	                                >
393	                                    <option value="xiangqi"
394	                                        prop:selected=move || variant.get() == Variant::Xiangqi>
395	                                        "Xiangqi 象棋"
396	                                    </option>
397	                                    <option value="banqi"
398	                                        prop:selected=move || variant.get() == Variant::Banqi>
399	                                        "Banqi 暗棋"
400	                                    </option>
401	                                </select>
402	                            </label>
403	                            <p class="hint">
404	                                "Three-Kingdom 三國暗棋 is 3-player and unsupported on the \
405	                                 2-peer LAN channel — pick another variant, or use online lobby."
406	                            </p>
407	                        </fieldset>
408	
409	                        <Show when=move || variant.get() == Variant::Xiangqi>
410	                            <fieldset class="card-fieldset" style="margin-bottom:0.5rem">
411	                                <legend>"Xiangqi rules"</legend>
412	                                <label class="check-row">
413	                                    <input type="checkbox"
414	                                        prop:checked=move || strict.get()
415	                                        on:change=move |ev| strict.set(event_target_checked(&ev))/>
416	                                    <span>
417	                                        "Strict — leaving your general capturable is illegal \
418	                                         (standard tournament rule). OFF = casual; the game \
419	                                         only ends when the general is actually captured."
420	                                    </span>
421	                                </label>
422	                            </fieldset>
423	                        </Show>
424	
425	                        <Show when=move || variant.get() == Variant::Banqi>
426	                            <fieldset class="card-fieldset" style="margin-bottom:0.5rem">
427	                                <legend>"Banqi preset"</legend>
428	                                <div class="preset-row">
429	                                    <button class="btn btn-ghost" type="button"
430	                                        on:click=move |_| apply_preset(PRESET_PURIST)>"Purist"</button>
431	                                    <button class="btn btn-ghost" type="button"
432	                                        on:click=move |_| apply_preset(PRESET_TAIWAN)>"Taiwan"</button>
433	                                    <button class="btn btn-ghost" type="button"
434	                                        on:click=move |_| apply_preset(PRESET_AGGRESSIVE)>"Aggressive"</button>
435	                                </div>
436	                            </fieldset>
437	                            <fieldset class="card-fieldset" style="margin-bottom:0.5rem">
438	                                <legend>"Banqi house rules"</legend>
439	                                <label class="check-row">
440	                                    <input type="checkbox"
441	                                        prop:checked=move || chain.get()
442	                                        on:change=move |ev| chain.set(event_target_checked(&ev))/>
443	                                    <span>"連吃 — chain captures along a line"</span>
444	                                </label>
445	                                <label class="check-row">
446	                                    <input type="checkbox"
447	                                        prop:checked=move || dark.get()
448	                                        on:change=move |ev| dark.set(event_target_checked(&ev))/>
449	                                    <span>"暗吃 — atomic reveal+capture; on rank-fail your piece stays put (probe)"</span>
450	                                </label>
451	                                <label class="check-row">
452	                                    <input type="checkbox"
453	                                        prop:checked=move || dark_trade.get()
454	                                        on:change=move |ev| dark_trade.set(event_target_checked(&ev))/>
455	                                    <span>"暗吃·搏命 — on rank-fail your attacker dies (implies 暗吃)"</span>
456	                                </label>
457	                                <label class="check-row">
458	                                    <input type="checkbox"
459	                                        prop:checked=move || rush.get()
460	                                        on:change=move |ev| rush.set(event_target_checked(&ev))/>
461	                                    <span>"車衝 — chariot rays the full board; with a gap, captures any piece"</span>
462	                                </label>
463	                                <label class="check-row">
464	                                    <input type="checkbox"
465	                                        prop:checked=move || horse.get()
466	                                        on:change=move |ev| horse.set(event_target_checked(&ev))/>
467	                                    <span>"馬斜 — horse adds diagonal one-step moves; diagonal captures any piece"</span>
468	                                </label>
469	                                <p class="hint">
470	                                    "炮快移 is accepted by the engine but not yet wired into move-gen "
471	                                    "(see "<code>"TODO.md"</code>")."
472	                                </p>
473	                            </fieldset>
474	                            <fieldset class="card-fieldset" style="margin-bottom:0.5rem">
475	                                <legend>"Seed (optional)"</legend>
476	                                <input
477	                                    type="text"
478	                                    inputmode="numeric"
479	                                    placeholder="leave blank for random"
480	                                    class="text-input"
481	                                    prop:value=move || seed_text.get()
482	                                    on:input=move |ev| seed_text.set(event_target_value(&ev))
483	                                />
484	                                <p class="hint">
485	                                    "Same seed = same shuffle on both devices. Use it for puzzle \
486	                                     replays or to make the layout reproducible."
487	                                </p>
488	                            </fieldset>
489	                        </Show>
490	                    </Show>
491	
492	                    <Show when=move || chosen_rules.with(|r| r.is_some())>
493	                        <p style="margin:0.5rem 0;font-size:14px">
494	                            "Playing: "
495	                            <b>{move || chosen_rules.get()
496	                                .as_ref()
497	                                .map(describe_rules)
498	                                .unwrap_or_default()}</b>
499	                        </p>
500	                    </Show>
501	                    <p class="muted">
502	                        "iOS hint: do not switch apps after tapping Open room. iOS Safari pauses \
503	                         WebRTC when the page is backgrounded. If you must AirDrop the offer, \
504	                         keep this Safari tab in the foreground (split-view works)."
505	                    </p>
506	                    <p class="muted">
507	                        "VPN hint: disable any VPN (Cloudflare WARP, NordVPN, etc.) on BOTH \
508	                         devices before pairing. VPNs replace the real LAN IP with a tunnel \

657	#[component]
658	pub fn LanJoinPage() -> impl IntoView {
659	    let (status, set_status) = create_signal::<JoinStatus>(JoinStatus::Idle);
660	    let (use_stun, set_use_stun) = create_signal::<bool>(false);
661	    let (offer_input, set_offer_input) = create_signal::<String>(String::new());
662	    let (answer_blob, set_answer_blob) = create_signal::<String>(String::new());
663	    let (error_msg, set_error_msg) = create_signal::<Option<String>>(None);
664	
665	    // QR scanner modal state. See LanHostPage for the same pattern.
666	    let (scanner_open, set_scanner_open) = create_signal::<bool>(false);
667	    let (cam_available, set_cam_available) = create_signal::<bool>(false);
668	    spawn_local(async move {
669	        if has_camera().await {
670	            set_cam_available.set(true);
671	        }
672	    });
673	
674	    // Joiner side: store the JoinerHandshake so we can pull its
675	    // session out once the DC opens.
676	    let handshake: Rc<RefCell<Option<JoinerHandshake>>> = Rc::new(RefCell::new(None));
677	    let (play_session, set_play_session) = create_signal::<Option<Session>>(None);
678	    // Diag-signal holder mirroring the host page. Populated once
679	    // `connect_as_joiner` resolves; drives the inline ICE-state badge
680	    // until the joiner's `handshake` is dropped (at which point the
681	    // PC's event handlers stop firing).
682	    let (ice_diag_holder, set_ice_diag_holder) = create_signal::<Option<ReadSignal<IceDiag>>>(None);
683	
684	    // Re-snapshot the answer SDP on every diag fire (candidates,
685	    // connection state, gathering). Mirrors the host page; see
686	    // JoinerHandshake::current_answer.
687	    {
688	        let handshake = handshake.clone();
689	        create_effect(move |_| {
690	            if let Some(sig) = ice_diag_holder.get() {
691	                let _ = sig.get();
692	                if let Some(jh) = handshake.borrow().as_ref() {
693	                    set_answer_blob.set(jh.current_answer().0);
694	                }
695	            }
696	        });
697	    }
698	
699	    // Holder pattern: spawn_local runs without a Leptos owner context,
700	    // so a `create_effect` inside it would be GC'd immediately (no
701	    // subscriptions retained — `state.set(Open)` would fire but no
702	    // effect would react). Solution: define the holder signals and
703	    // the effect at component scope (properly owned), and have
704	    // spawn_local just `set` the holders. The component-scope effect
705	    // re-runs when the holders change AND when the inner state
706	    // signal changes.
707	    let (joiner_state_holder, set_joiner_state_holder) =
708	        create_signal::<Option<ReadSignal<ConnState>>>(None);
709	    let session_holder: Rc<RefCell<Option<Session>>> = Rc::new(RefCell::new(None));
710	
711	    {
712	        let session_holder = session_holder.clone();
713	        create_effect(move |_| {
714	            // Read both signals to register subscriptions:
715	            //   * joiner_state_holder fires once when handshake completes.
716	            //   * the inner state signal fires when DC opens.
717	            if let Some(state_sig) = joiner_state_holder.get() {
718	                if state_sig.get() == ConnState::Open {
719	                    if let Some(session) = session_holder.borrow().clone() {
720	                        set_play_session.set(Some(session));
721	                        set_status.set(JoinStatus::Playing);
722	                    }
723	                }
724	            }
725	        });
726	    }
727	
728	    let handshake_for_gen = handshake.clone();
729	    let session_holder_for_gen = session_holder.clone();
730	    let on_generate: Callback<()> = Callback::new(move |_: ()| {
731	        if !matches!(status.get_untracked(), JoinStatus::Idle) {
732	            return;
733	        }
734	        let blob = offer_input.get_untracked();
735	        if blob.trim().is_empty() {
736	            set_error_msg.set(Some("paste the host's offer SDP first".into()));
737	            return;
738	        }
739	        set_error_msg.set(None);
740	        set_status.set(JoinStatus::Generating);
741	        let cfg = WebRtcConfig {
742	            ice_mode: if use_stun.get_untracked() { IceMode::WithStun } else { IceMode::LanOnly },
743	        };
744	        let handshake_slot = handshake_for_gen.clone();
745	        let session_holder = session_holder_for_gen.clone();
746	        spawn_local(async move {
747	            match connect_as_joiner(cfg, OfferBlob(blob)).await {
748	                Ok(jh) => {
749	                    set_answer_blob.set(jh.answer.0.clone());
750	                    let state = jh.session.state;
751	                    set_ice_diag_holder.set(Some(jh.ice_diag));
752	                    *session_holder.borrow_mut() = Some(jh.session.clone());
753	                    *handshake_slot.borrow_mut() = Some(jh);
754	                    set_status.set(JoinStatus::WaitingForOpen);
755	                    // Triggers the component-scope effect to subscribe
756	                    // to the state signal. Once DC opens, that effect
757	                    // flips status → Playing.
758	                    set_joiner_state_holder.set(Some(state));
759	                }
760	                Err(e) => {
761	                    set_error_msg.set(Some(format!("connect_as_joiner failed: {e:?}")));
762	                    set_status.set(JoinStatus::Idle);
763	                }
764	            }
765	        });
766	    });
767	
768	    let on_reset: Callback<()> = Callback::new(move |_: ()| {
769	        if let Some(win) = web_sys::window() {
770	            let _ = win.location().reload();
771	        }
772	    });

774	    view! {

776	            <Show

790	                         172.16.0.0/12 (private LANs), 172.20.10.0/28 (iOS hotspot), and \
791	                         224.0.0.0/4 (multicast / mDNS)."

808	                                        .and_then(|t| t.dyn_into::<web_sys::HtmlInputElement>().ok())
809	                                        .map(|el| el.checked())

811	                                    set_use_stun.set(v);
812	                                }
813	                            />

815	                        </label>

821	                                .map(|sig| diag_line(&sig.get()))

933	/// so any code path that DID happen to use it for URL building
```

```text
1	//! Host-side room authority for LAN play.
2	//!
3	//! Phase 4 of `backlog/webrtc-lan-pairing.md`. Wraps the
4	//! `chess_net::room::Room` state machine with a multi-peer routing
5	//! table:
6	//!
7	//!   - Host runs the `Room` in-process (no chess-net server involved).
8	//!   - Host is itself one of the seated players (`PeerSink::Local`):
9	//!     its `ClientMsg`s flow through `Room::apply` exactly the same
10	//!     way a remote peer's would, and it receives projected
11	//!     `ServerMsg`s via a Leptos signal that the play page reads.
12	//!   - Remote peers (joiner + spectators) connect via WebRTC. Each
13	//!     `RtcDataChannel` is a `PeerSink::Remote`; incoming JSON-decoded
14	//!     `ClientMsg`s route through the same `Room::apply` path.
15	//!
16	//! The host page consumes the same `Session { handle, incoming, state }`
17	//! shape that `transport::ws::connect` returns, so the existing
18	//! `pages/play.rs` works unchanged once Phase 5 wires up the
19	//! `/lan/host` route to provide the host's `Session` via Leptos
20	//! context.
21	//!
22	//! ## Lifetime + reference-cycle notes
23	//!
24	//! Closures wired to `RtcDataChannel.onmessage` capture `Weak<HostRoom>`,
25	//! not `Rc<HostRoom>`, to avoid a reference cycle between the room
26	//! and the per-peer keepalive vector. When the page drops its
27	//! `Rc<HostRoom>`, all `Weak::upgrade` calls inside the closures
28	//! return `None` and become silent no-ops — the next time the
29	//! browser GCs the channel, the closure goes too.
30	//!
31	//! ## Dead-code allowance
32	//!
33	//! Phase 5 (`pages/lan.rs`) consumes `HostRoom::new` +
34	//! `attach_remote_player_dc`. Spectator support, the
35	//! `add_remote_*` (non-DC) variants used by tests, and the
36	//! `room_id` / `summary` accessors are kept for Phase 5 polish.
37	#![allow(dead_code)]
38	
39	// === module skeleton — section bodies filled in via edits below ===
40	
41	// SECTION: imports
42	use std::cell::{Cell, RefCell};
43	use std::collections::HashMap;
44	use std::rc::Rc;
45	
46	use chess_core::rules::RuleSet;
47	use chess_net::protocol::{ClientMsg, ServerMsg};
48	use chess_net::room::{JoinError, Outbound, PeerId, Room};
49	use leptos::*;
50	
51	use crate::transport::{ConnState, Incoming, Session, Transport};
52	
53	#[cfg(target_arch = "wasm32")]
54	#[allow(unused_imports)]
55	use {
56	    std::rc::Weak,
57	    wasm_bindgen::closure::Closure,
58	    wasm_bindgen::{JsCast, JsValue},
59	    web_sys::{MessageEvent, RtcDataChannel},
60	};
61	// SECTION: PeerSink
62	
63	/// Where to deliver `ServerMsg`s for a given peer.
64	///
65	/// `Local` is the host's own play page — `ServerMsg`s become entries
66	/// in a Leptos-tracked `Incoming` queue (a `VecDeque` + tick signal,
67	/// see `transport::Incoming`) that the page reads via
68	/// `Session.incoming` (and drains via `Incoming::drain`). The queue
69	/// design (rather than a latched single-value signal) is critical:
70	/// a fresh `Room::join_player` synchronously emits `Hello` +
71	/// `ChatHistory`, which on a latched signal would silently overwrite
72	/// each other in a single Leptos batch. The queue + monotonic tick
73	/// guarantees both messages survive AND that any post-mount push
74	/// (e.g. the host's own move's `Update` echo back through the local
75	/// sink) is observed — see `transport::Incoming` doc for the race
76	/// analysis.
77	///
78	/// `Remote` is a peer connected over WebRTC — `ServerMsg`s are
79	/// JSON-serialised and written into a `RtcDataChannel`. The
80	/// non-wasm32 build (workspace `cargo check`) substitutes a
81	/// `RemoteMock` variant for unit tests since `web_sys` types
82	/// aren't reachable on native targets.
83	pub enum PeerSink {
84	    Local(Incoming),
85	    #[cfg(target_arch = "wasm32")]
86	    Remote(RtcDataChannel),
87	    /// Test-only sink that records every delivered message into a
88	    /// shared `Vec`. Native + wasm32 both compile this so unit
89	    /// tests can run on either target.
90	    Mock(Rc<RefCell<Vec<ServerMsg>>>),
91	}
92	
93	impl PeerSink {
94	    fn deliver(&self, msg: ServerMsg) {
95	        match self {
96	            PeerSink::Local(inc) => inc.push(msg),
97	            #[cfg(target_arch = "wasm32")]
98	            PeerSink::Remote(dc) => {
99	                if let Ok(text) = serde_json::to_string(&msg) {
100	                    let _ = dc.send_with_str(&text);
101	                }
102	            }
103	            PeerSink::Mock(buf) => buf.borrow_mut().push(msg),
104	        }
105	    }
106	}
107	// SECTION: HostRoom struct
108	
109	/// Host-side authoritative room. Owns a `chess_net::room::Room`
110	/// (the same state machine the chess-net server runs) plus a routing
111	/// table mapping each peer's [`PeerId`] to a [`PeerSink`].
112	///
113	/// All mutable state is in `RefCell`s (single-threaded WASM context).
114	/// The struct is consumed via `Rc<HostRoom>` because:
115	///   1. The host's `Transport` impl needs to hold a strong ref so
116	///      `Transport::send` can route the host's own `ClientMsg`s
117	///      through `Room::apply`.
118	///   2. Per-peer DataChannel `onmessage` closures need a `Weak`
119	///      ref (NOT strong — see the cycle note in the module doc).
120	pub struct HostRoom {
121	    room: RefCell<Room>,
122	    sinks: RefCell<HashMap<PeerId, PeerSink>>,
123	    /// Monotonic peer-id allocator. The host gets `PeerId(1)`; the
124	    /// first remote peer is `PeerId(2)`, etc.
125	    next_peer: Cell<u64>,
126	    /// `PeerId` for the host's own play UI. Always `PeerId(1)` from
127	    /// `new()`; surfaced as a getter for clarity at call sites.
128	    self_peer: PeerId,
129	    /// Stable label used in `RoomSummary` (the host page shows it
130	    /// in the lobby-style indicator). Defaults to "lan".
131	    room_id: String,
132	}
133	// SECTION: HostRoom::new + Session bridge
134	
135	impl HostRoom {
136	    /// Construct a fresh host room and seat the host as the first
137	    /// player. Returns:
138	    ///
139	    ///   * `Rc<HostRoom>` — handle the host page holds for adding
140	    ///     remote peers as they pair (`add_remote_player`,
141	    ///     `add_remote_spectator`).
142	    ///   * `Session` — drop into the play page exactly where
143	    ///     `transport::ws::connect`'s return value would go. The
144	    ///     session's `incoming` signal already has the initial
145	    ///     `Hello` + `ChatHistory` queued (the host always seats as
146	    ///     RED, so the page sees `observer: Side::RED` immediately).
147	    ///
148	    /// Panics only on the impossible case where `Room::join_player`
149	    /// fails for the very first joiner (the room is fresh — both
150	    /// seats are open, no password mismatch is possible because the
151	    /// password was set on the same line).
152	    pub fn new(
153	        rules: RuleSet,
154	        password: Option<String>,
155	        hints_allowed: bool,
156	    ) -> (Rc<Self>, Session) {
157	        let mut room = Room::new(rules, password, hints_allowed);
158	        let self_peer = PeerId(1);
159	        let incoming = Incoming::new();
160	        let (state, _set_state) = create_signal(ConnState::Open);
161	
162	        // Insert host's sink BEFORE join_player so the Hello +
163	        // ChatHistory the room emits queue into the host's own
164	        // incoming Incoming — same VecDeque, processed in arrival
165	        // order by the play page's draining effect (which reads
166	        // every pending message via Incoming::drain on each tick).
167	        let mut sinks: HashMap<PeerId, PeerSink> = HashMap::new();
168	        sinks.insert(self_peer, PeerSink::Local(incoming.clone()));
169	
170	        let (_side, outbound) =
171	            room.join_player(self_peer).expect("fresh room always seats the first joiner");
172	        for ob in outbound {
173	            if let Some(sink) = sinks.get(&ob.peer) {
174	                sink.deliver(ob.msg);
175	            }
176	        }
177	
178	        let host = Rc::new(Self {
179	            room: RefCell::new(room),
180	            sinks: RefCell::new(sinks),
181	            next_peer: Cell::new(2),
182	            self_peer,
183	            room_id: "lan".into(),
184	        });
185	
186	        let handle: Rc<dyn Transport> =
187	            Rc::new(HostSelfTransport { host: host.clone(), self_peer });
188	        let session = Session { handle, incoming, state };
189	        (host, session)
190	    }
191	
192	    /// `PeerId` assigned to the host's own play UI. Stable across
193	    /// the room's lifetime.
194	    pub fn self_peer(&self) -> PeerId {
195	        self.self_peer
196	    }
197	
198	    /// Allocate the next `PeerId` for a newly-arriving remote peer.
199	    fn alloc_peer(&self) -> PeerId {
200	        let id = self.next_peer.get();
201	        self.next_peer.set(id + 1);
202	        PeerId(id)
203	    }
204	}
205	// SECTION: HostSelfTransport (impl Transport for the host's own page)
206	
207	/// `Transport` impl that routes the host's own `ClientMsg`s through
208	/// the in-process `Room::apply` instead of a network socket.
209	struct HostSelfTransport {
210	    host: Rc<HostRoom>,
211	    self_peer: PeerId,
212	}
213	
214	impl Transport for HostSelfTransport {
215	    fn send(&self, msg: ClientMsg) -> bool {
216	        self.host.handle_self_send(self.self_peer, msg);
217	        true
218	    }
219	}
220	// SECTION: add_remote_player / add_remote_spectator
221	
222	impl HostRoom {
223	    /// Insert a remote peer with a player-side seat.
224	    ///
225	    /// `sink` should normally be `PeerSink::Remote(dc)` for production
226	    /// (the page just got a `RtcDataChannel` from
227	    /// `HostHandshake::accept_answer` succeeding); tests pass
228	    /// `PeerSink::Mock(...)` instead.
229	    ///
230	    /// Wires the DataChannel's `onmessage` to route incoming
231	    /// `ClientMsg`s through `Room::apply` (production only — the
232	    /// `dc_for_handlers` arg passes the channel through to the
233	    /// handler installer; tests skip it). On error (room full),
234	    /// the sink is removed and the error returned.
235	    pub fn add_remote_player(self: &Rc<Self>, sink: PeerSink) -> Result<PeerId, JoinError> {
236	        let peer = self.alloc_peer();
237	        self.sinks.borrow_mut().insert(peer, sink);
238	        let outbound = match self.room.borrow_mut().join_player(peer) {
239	            Ok((_side, ob)) => ob,
240	            Err(e) => {
241	                self.sinks.borrow_mut().remove(&peer);
242	                return Err(e);
243	            }
244	        };
245	        self.fanout(outbound);
246	        Ok(peer)
247	    }
248	
249	    /// Insert a remote peer as a read-only spectator. `max` is the
250	    /// per-room spectator cap (the v1 plan says 4).
251	    pub fn add_remote_spectator(
252	        self: &Rc<Self>,
253	        sink: PeerSink,
254	        max: usize,
255	    ) -> Result<PeerId, JoinError> {
256	        let peer = self.alloc_peer();
257	        self.sinks.borrow_mut().insert(peer, sink);
258	        let outbound = match self.room.borrow_mut().join_spectator(peer, max) {
259	            Ok(ob) => ob,
260	            Err(e) => {
261	                self.sinks.borrow_mut().remove(&peer);
262	                return Err(e);
263	            }
264	        };
265	        self.fanout(outbound);
266	        Ok(peer)
267	    }
268	
269	    /// Wasm-only convenience: take a freshly-opened `RtcDataChannel`
270	    /// from a completed `HostHandshake`, install onmessage routing
271	    /// to `Room::apply`, and seat the peer as a player.
272	    #[cfg(target_arch = "wasm32")]
273	    pub fn attach_remote_player_dc(
274	        self: &Rc<Self>,
275	        dc: RtcDataChannel,
276	    ) -> Result<PeerId, JoinError> {
277	        let peer = self.add_remote_player(PeerSink::Remote(dc.clone()))?;
278	        install_remote_dc_handlers(self, peer, &dc);
279	        Ok(peer)
280	    }
281	
282	    #[cfg(target_arch = "wasm32")]
283	    pub fn attach_remote_spectator_dc(
284	        self: &Rc<Self>,
285	        dc: RtcDataChannel,
286	        max: usize,
287	    ) -> Result<PeerId, JoinError> {
288	        let peer = self.add_remote_spectator(PeerSink::Remote(dc.clone()), max)?;
289	        install_remote_dc_handlers(self, peer, &dc);
290	        Ok(peer)
291	    }
292	}
293	// SECTION: handle_self_send / handle_remote_msg / fanout
294	
295	impl HostRoom {
296	    /// Route the host's own `ClientMsg` (from the play page's
297	    /// `Transport::send`) through the room.
298	    fn handle_self_send(&self, peer: PeerId, msg: ClientMsg) {
299	        let outbound = self.room.borrow_mut().apply(peer, msg);
300	        self.fanout(outbound);
301	    }
302	
303	    /// Route a remote peer's incoming `ClientMsg` through the room.
304	    /// Called from the per-DataChannel `onmessage` closure.
305	    fn handle_remote_msg(&self, peer: PeerId, msg: ClientMsg) {
306	        let outbound = self.room.borrow_mut().apply(peer, msg);
307	        self.fanout(outbound);
308	    }
309	
310	    /// Deliver each `Outbound` to its target peer's sink. Outbounds
311	    /// for unknown peers (e.g. peer disconnected mid-broadcast) are
312	    /// silently dropped.
313	    fn fanout(&self, outbound: Vec<Outbound>) {
314	        let sinks = self.sinks.borrow();
315	        for ob in outbound {
316	            if let Some(sink) = sinks.get(&ob.peer) {
317	                sink.deliver(ob.msg);
318	            }
319	        }
320	    }
321	
322	    /// Stable label used in `RoomSummary` (the host page may show
323	    /// the room state in a sidebar widget).
324	    pub fn room_id(&self) -> &str {
325	        &self.room_id
326	    }
327	
328	    /// Snapshot of the underlying room's [`chess_net::RoomSummary`].
329	    /// Useful for the host page's sidebar / future lobby UI.
330	    pub fn summary(&self) -> chess_net::RoomSummary {
331	        self.room.borrow().summary(&self.room_id)
332	    }
333	}
334	// SECTION: peer disconnect handling
335	
336	impl HostRoom {
337	    /// Remove a peer from the routing table + room. Returns the
338	    /// outbound list `Room::leave` produces (e.g. mid-game
339	    /// "opponent disconnected" notice to the surviving seat). The
340	    /// caller is responsible for invoking this from a DataChannel
341	    /// `onclose` event — production wires it via
342	    /// `install_remote_dc_handlers`.
343	    pub fn drop_peer(&self, peer: PeerId) {
344	        let outbound = self.room.borrow_mut().leave(peer);
345	        self.fanout(outbound);
346	        self.sinks.borrow_mut().remove(&peer);
347	    }
348	}
349	// SECTION: install_remote_dc_handlers (Weak<HostRoom> closures)
350	
351	/// Wire a remote peer's `RtcDataChannel.onmessage` to route incoming
352	/// `ClientMsg` JSON through `Room::apply`, and `onclose` to drop the
353	/// peer.
354	///
355	/// Closures capture `Weak<HostRoom>` (NOT `Rc`) so dropping the
356	/// page's strong references actually frees the room — without
357	/// `Weak`, the closures-in-keepalive-vec → `Rc<HostRoom>` cycle
358	/// would leak forever. Once the room drops, `Weak::upgrade` returns
359	/// `None` and any in-flight callback is a silent no-op.
360	///
361	/// Closures are `cb.forget()`-leaked: the only way to cancel them
362	/// would be `dc.set_onmessage(None)`, but the room dropping already
363	/// makes them harmless. For Phase 4 this is acceptable; Phase 5+
364	/// could add explicit teardown if memory pressure ever shows up.
365	#[cfg(target_arch = "wasm32")]
366	fn install_remote_dc_handlers(host: &Rc<HostRoom>, peer: PeerId, dc: &RtcDataChannel) {
367	    {
368	        let weak = Rc::downgrade(host);
369	        let cb = Closure::wrap(Box::new(move |ev: JsValue| {
370	            let ev: MessageEvent = ev.unchecked_into();
371	            let text = match ev.data().as_string() {
372	                Some(t) => t,
373	                None => return,
374	            };
375	            let msg: ClientMsg = match serde_json::from_str(&text) {
376	                Ok(m) => m,
377	                Err(_) => return,
378	            };
379	            if let Some(host) = weak.upgrade() {
380	                host.handle_remote_msg(peer, msg);
381	            }
382	        }) as Box<dyn FnMut(JsValue)>);
383	        dc.set_onmessage(Some(cb.as_ref().unchecked_ref()));
384	        cb.forget();
385	    }
386	    {
387	        let weak = Rc::downgrade(host);
388	        let cb = Closure::wrap(Box::new(move |_ev: JsValue| {
389	            if let Some(host) = weak.upgrade() {
390	                host.drop_peer(peer);
391	            }
392	        }) as Box<dyn FnMut(JsValue)>);
393	        dc.set_onclose(Some(cb.as_ref().unchecked_ref()));
394	        cb.forget();
395	    }
396	}
397	// SECTION: tests
398	//
399	// HostRoom is wasm32-only because of the Leptos `WriteSignal` /
400	// `ReadSignal` dependency on the host's `Session` bridge. Native
401	// `cargo test --workspace` therefore cannot exercise this module
402	// directly; the meaningful test coverage lives elsewhere:
403	//
404	// * `chess-net::room::tests` (in the workspace) has 17 unit tests
405	//   covering every `Room::apply` branch (move / resign / chat /
406	//   rematch / leave / spectator gates / ...). HostRoom is a thin
407	//   routing wrapper on top of that; the moment a `ClientMsg`
408	//   reaches `Room::apply`, behaviour is identical to the chess-net
409	//   server. So the room-side correctness is well-covered.
410	//
411	// * The Phase 5 `/lan/host` and `/lan/join` pages exercise the
412	//   wasm-specific glue (DataChannel routing, Local-sink Hello
413	//   delivery, peer disconnect notifications) end-to-end on real
414	//   devices.
415	//
416	// If we ever need native unit coverage of HostRoom itself, the
417	// cleanest split is to move the pure-routing `handle_remote_msg` /
418	// `fanout` / `drop_peer` methods + `PeerSink::Mock` into a
419	// `host_room/router.rs` submodule that has no Leptos dependency,
420	// leaving the `Session` bridge in a wasm32-only `host_room/
421	// session.rs`. Deferred until a routing-bug-without-an-obvious-fix
422	// shows up.
423
```

```text
158	fn XiangqiCard() -> impl IntoView {
159	    let strict = create_rw_signal(false);
160	    let mode = create_rw_signal(PlayMode::Pvp);
161	    let player_red = create_rw_signal(true);
162	    // Difficulty signal stores the selected preset (Easy/Normal/Hard).
163	    // When `is_custom` is true, this signal is ignored for depth
164	    // resolution but still feeds the randomness fallback (since
165	    // `Difficulty::default_randomness()` provides per-level defaults
166	    // and there's no neutral "custom" randomness option). We pin the
167	    // underlying value to `Hard` while in Custom mode so the fallback
168	    // is `Randomness::SUBTLE` — power-user appropriate. Users wanting
169	    // a different mix override via the Variation fieldset.
170	    let difficulty = create_rw_signal(Difficulty::Normal);
171	    // Custom is rendered as a 4th radio in the Difficulty fieldset
172	    // — click reveals the Search-depth + Node-budget inputs below
173	    // (they're conditionally mounted via <Show when=is_custom>).
174	    let is_custom = create_rw_signal(false);
175	    let ai_strategy = create_rw_signal(Strategy::default());
176	    // None = "use difficulty default"; Some(_) = explicit override.
177	    // Encoded into the URL via `&variation=`.
178	    let ai_variation = create_rw_signal::<Option<Randomness>>(None);
179	    // Empty = use difficulty default depth; numeric = override (clamped
180	    // 1..=MAX_AI_DEPTH on parse). ONLY emitted to the URL when
181	    // is_custom is true — preset difficulties always defer to
182	    // `Difficulty::default_depth()`.
183	    let ai_depth_text = create_rw_signal(String::new());
184	    // Empty = use the engine's auto-scaled budget (v5:
185	    // node_budget_for_depth(target); v1-v4: flat NODE_BUDGET).
186	    // Numeric = explicit override clamped to MAX_AI_NODE_BUDGET. Only
187	    // emitted when is_custom is true.
188	    let ai_budget_text = create_rw_signal(String::new());
189	    // Two independent advanced toggles — see the corresponding fields
190	    // on `LocalRulesParams` for the semantic split:
191	    //   - ai_show_hints → URL `hints=1` → in-game 🧠 toggle button,
192	    //     default hidden, runs analyze from the human's POV when
193	    //     opened (player-friendly).
194	    //   - ai_show_debug → URL `debug=1` → sticky always-on panel
195	    //     showing the AI's POV after each AI move (power-user).
196	    // Both can be enabled together; `pages/local.rs` mounts a single
197	    // `<DebugPanel>` and lets either source feed it.
198	    let ai_show_hints = create_rw_signal(false);
199	    let ai_show_debug = create_rw_signal(false);
200	    // Win-rate display: separate URL flag (`?evalbar=1`). Independent
201	    // of hints/debug — works in both vs-Computer and Pass-and-play
202	    // (samples come from the same hint pump that powers `?hints=1`,
203	    // which is unconditionally enabled when either flag is set).
204	    let ai_show_evalbar = create_rw_signal(false);
205	    // Pass-and-play only: rotate Black piece glyphs 180° so the player
206	    // sitting on the opposite side of the device reads their own pieces
207	    // upright. Coordinate system unchanged.
208	    let mirror_black = create_rw_signal(false);
209	    let href = move || {
210	        let ai_side = if player_red.get() { Side::BLACK } else { Side::RED };
211	        // Custom mode: parse depth + budget; preset modes: leave both None
212	        // so the engine defers to Difficulty::default_depth() and the
213	        // engine-internal node-budget policy.
214	        let (ai_depth, ai_node_budget, effective_difficulty) = if is_custom.get() {
215	            let depth = ai_depth_text
216	                .with(|s| s.trim().parse::<u8>().ok().map(|d| d.clamp(1, MAX_AI_DEPTH)));
217	            let budget = ai_budget_text
218	                .with(|s| s.trim().parse::<u32>().ok().map(|b| b.clamp(1, MAX_AI_NODE_BUDGET)));
219	            // Custom rides on top of `Difficulty::Hard` so the
220	            // randomness fallback (when Variation = Default) lands on
221	            // `Randomness::SUBTLE` — the power-user-appropriate
222	            // default. See the comment on `difficulty` above.
223	            (depth, budget, Difficulty::Hard)
224	        } else {
225	            (None, None, difficulty.get())
226	        };
227	        let params = LocalRulesParams {
228	            strict: strict.get(),
229	            mode: mode.get(),
230	            ai_side,
231	            ai_difficulty: effective_difficulty,
232	            ai_strategy: ai_strategy.get(),
233	            ai_variation: ai_variation.get(),
234	            ai_depth,
235	            ai_node_budget,
236	            ai_debug: ai_show_debug.get(),
237	            ai_hints: ai_show_hints.get(),
238	            ai_evalbar: ai_show_evalbar.get(),
239	            mirror: mirror_black.get(),
240	            ..Default::default()
241	        };
242	        app_href(&build_local_href(Variant::Xiangqi, &params))
243	    };
244	    view! {
245	        <div class="variant-card variant-card--form">
246	            <h2>"Xiangqi 象棋"</h2>
247	            <p>"Standard 9×10 Chinese chess."</p>
248	            <fieldset class="card-fieldset">
249	                <legend>"Mode"</legend>
250	                <label class="radio-row">
251	                    <input
252	                        type="radio"
253	                        name="xiangqi-mode"
254	                        prop:checked=move || mode.get() == PlayMode::Pvp
255	                        on:change=move |_| mode.set(PlayMode::Pvp)
256	                    />
257	                    <span>"Pass-and-play — two humans share this device."</span>
258	                </label>
259	                <label class="radio-row">
260	                    <input
261	                        type="radio"
262	                        name="xiangqi-mode"
263	                        prop:checked=move || mode.get() == PlayMode::VsAi
264	                        on:change=move |_| mode.set(PlayMode::VsAi)
265	                    />
266	                    <span>"vs Computer — alpha-beta engine, runs entirely in your browser."</span>
267	                </label>
268	            </fieldset>
269	            <Show when=move || mode.get() == PlayMode::Pvp>
270	                <fieldset class="card-fieldset">
271	                    <legend>"Seat layout"</legend>
272	                    <label class="check-row">
273	                        <input
274	                            type="checkbox"
275	                            prop:checked=move || mirror_black.get()
276	                            on:change=move |ev| mirror_black.set(event_target_checked(&ev))
277	                        />
278	                        <span>"鏡像黑方 — Mirror Black's pieces 180° for a player sitting opposite (phone flat on a table). Captured pieces split to each side's edge."</span>
279	                    </label>
280	                </fieldset>
281	            </Show>
282	            <Show when=move || mode.get() == PlayMode::VsAi>
283	                <fieldset class="card-fieldset">
284	                    <legend>"You play"</legend>
285	                    <label class="radio-row">
286	                        <input
287	                            type="radio"
288	                            name="xiangqi-player-side"
289	                            prop:checked=move || player_red.get()
290	                            on:change=move |_| player_red.set(true)
291	                        />
292	                        <span>"紅 Red — moves first."</span>
293	                    </label>
294	                    <label class="radio-row">
295	                        <input
296	                            type="radio"
297	                            name="xiangqi-player-side"
298	                            prop:checked=move || !player_red.get()
299	                            on:change=move |_| player_red.set(false)
300	                        />
```

```text
1	//! Multi-room ws server. Routes:
2	//!
3	//!   GET /             → upgrade into default room "main" (back-compat)
4	//!   GET /ws           → upgrade into default room "main" (back-compat)
5	//!   GET /ws/:room_id  → upgrade into named room (auto-created on first join)
6	//!   GET /lobby        → subscribe to live `Rooms` pushes
7	//!   GET /rooms        → JSON snapshot for `curl`/debug
8	//!
9	//! Optional `?password=<secret>` query param on the game-room routes locks
10	//! the room to the first joiner's secret. Wrong password gets
11	//! `Error{"bad password"}` + close.
12	//!
13	//! Optional `?role=spectator` upgrades the connection as a read-only
14	//! spectator instead of a seat. Spectators receive `Spectating` (instead of
15	//! `Hello`) plus `ChatHistory`, then live `Update` and `Chat` pushes. They
16	//! cannot move, resign, request rematch, or send chat. Player connections
17	//! arrive without `?role=spectator` — back-compat means v2 clients keep
18	//! getting "room full" if they're the third joiner.
19	//!
20	//! Empty rooms are GC'd when the last seat disconnects **and** the last
21	//! spectator leaves, except `main` which is permanent so v1 clients always
22	//! have a stable default to land in.
23	//!
24	//! Reconnect / mixed-variant rooms / time controls / TLS still deferred —
25	//! see `TODO.md`.
26	//!
27	//! ## Refactor history
28	//!
29	//! As of 2026-05-11 (Phase 1 of `backlog/webrtc-lan-pairing.md`) all room
30	//! state lives in [`crate::room::Room`] — a transport-agnostic state
31	//! machine that compiles for `wasm32-unknown-unknown`. This file is now
32	//! a thin axum/tokio glue that owns:
33	//!
34	//! 1. The per-room `Arc<Mutex<RoomEntry>>` table (room + peer routing map).
35	//! 2. The lobby-summary projection + push fan-out.
36	//! 3. `?password=`/`?role=`/`?hints=` URL parsing.
37	//! 4. The `--static-dir` static-file serving.
38	//!
39	//! Per-message work is just: deserialize → `room.apply(peer, msg)` →
40	//! fan out the returned `Vec<Outbound>` via the per-room routing table.
41	
42	// === module skeleton — section bodies filled in via edits below ===
43	
44	// SECTION: imports
45	use std::collections::HashMap;
46	use std::net::SocketAddr;
47	use std::sync::atomic::{AtomicU64, Ordering};
48	use std::sync::Arc;
49	
50	use anyhow::{Context, Result};
51	use axum::extract::ws::{Message, WebSocket, WebSocketUpgrade};
52	use axum::extract::{Path, Query, State};
53	#[cfg(feature = "static-serve")]
54	use axum::http::{header, HeaderValue};

69	    DEFAULT_MAX_SPECTATORS, DEFAULT_ROOM, MAX_PASSWORD_LEN, MAX_ROOM_ID_LEN,

71	// SECTION: cache constants (static-serve)

73	const IMMUTABLE_ASSET_CACHE: &str = "public, max-age=31536000, immutable";
74	#[cfg(feature = "static-serve")]
75	const HTML_CACHE: &str = "no-cache";
76	// SECTION: app state types

78	/// Per-connection outbound channel. The handler hands one of these to the
79	/// per-room peer map; the `Room::apply` fan-out delivers `ServerMsg`s into
80	/// it; a per-connection write task drains it onto the WebSocket sink.
81	type PeerSender = mpsc::UnboundedSender<ServerMsg>;
82	
83	/// One row in the per-room peer routing table.
84	struct RoomEntry {
85	    room: Room,
86	    peers: HashMap<PeerId, PeerSender>,
87	}
88	
89	impl RoomEntry {
90	    fn new(room: Room) -> Self {
91	        Self { room, peers: HashMap::new() }
92	    }
93	
94	    /// Deliver an `Outbound` list to the matching peer senders. Drops any
95	    /// outbound whose target peer has already disconnected — its sender
96	    /// is gone from the map.
97	    fn fanout(&self, out: Vec<Outbound>) {
98	        for ob in out {
99	            if let Some(tx) = self.peers.get(&ob.peer) {
100	                let _ = tx.send(ob.msg);
```

```text
1	//! WebRTC transport for chess-net protocol.
2	//!
3	//! Production successor to the Phase 0 spike in
4	//! `clients/chess-web/src/spike/rtc.rs`. Same underlying plumbing
5	//! (`web_sys::RtcPeerConnection` + reliable+ordered DataChannel +
6	//! mDNS-only ICE), but exposes a clean API matching `transport::ws`:
7	//!
8	//! * `connect_as_joiner(cfg, offer) -> JoinerHandshake` — the
9	//!   joiner consumes the host's offer SDP and produces an answer SDP.
10	//!   Returns the same [`Session`] shape as `transport::ws::connect`,
11	//!   so `pages/play.rs` and `pages/lobby.rs` work unchanged.
12	//! * `connect_as_host(cfg) -> HostHandshake` — the host generates an
13	//!   offer SDP and waits for the joiner's answer. Exposes the raw
14	//!   PeerConnection + DataChannel handles so Phase 4's
15	//!   `host_room.rs` can wrap them with multi-peer `Room` routing.
16	//!
17	//! Out of scope for Phase 3:
18	//! * QR generation / camera scanning (Phase 5).
19	//! * Multi-peer host (`host_room.rs` — Phase 4).
20	//! * Auto-reconnect / signalling fallback (Approach C — P3 follow-up).
21	//!
22	//! Backlog: `backlog/webrtc-lan-pairing.md`. Pitfalls:
23	//! `pitfalls/webrtc-mdns-lan-ap-isolation.md`.
24	//!
25	//! ## Dead-code allowance
26	//!
27	//! Phase 4 (`host_room.rs`) and Phase 5 (`pages/lan.rs`) consume
28	//! the public surface of this module — `connect_as_*`, `OfferBlob`,
29	//! `AnswerBlob`, `WebRtcConfig`, `IceMode`. Some helpers (the spike's
30	//! `Mock` sink path, future spectator attach, etc.) remain unused;
31	//! kept for completeness pending Phase 5 polish.
32	#![allow(dead_code)]
33

35

38	use std::rc::Rc;

45	use wasm_bindgen_futures::JsFuture;

47	    MessageEvent, RtcConfiguration, RtcDataChannel, RtcDataChannelEvent, RtcDataChannelInit,
48	    RtcDataChannelState, RtcIceConnectionState, RtcIceGatheringState, RtcPeerConnection,
49	    RtcPeerConnectionState, RtcSdpType, RtcSessionDescriptionInit, RtcSignalingState,
50	};
51	
52	use super::{ConnState, Incoming, Session, Transport};
53	// SECTION: public types (IceMode, WebRtcConfig, OfferBlob, AnswerBlob)
54	
55	/// `RtcPeerConnection` ICE configuration.
56	///
57	/// `LanOnly` is the production default — `iceServers: []`, pure mDNS
58	/// `<uuid>.local` host candidates only. No external dependencies.
59	///
60	/// `WithStun` adds a multi-server STUN list for the `srflx` candidates.
61	/// Useful as a fallback when LAN P2P fails (e.g. router blocks WebRTC's
62	/// `<uuid>.local` resolution; see
63	/// `pitfalls/webrtc-mdns-lan-ap-isolation.md`). Server list ordered for
64	/// CN reachability — `stun.miwifi.com` and `stun.qq.com` first, Google
65	/// last (Google STUN is blocked by the GFW from mainland China).
66	#[derive(Copy, Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Eq)]
67	pub enum IceMode {
68	    LanOnly,
69	    WithStun,
70	}
71	
72	/// Knobs for opening a WebRTC session. Defaults to `LanOnly` — the
73	/// "no external dependency" production case.
74	#[derive(Copy, Clone, Debug)]
75	pub struct WebRtcConfig {
76	    pub ice_mode: IceMode,
77	}
78	
79	impl Default for WebRtcConfig {
80	    fn default() -> Self {
81	        Self { ice_mode: IceMode::LanOnly }
82	    }
83	}
84	
85	/// Maximum time we wait for `iceGatheringState == "complete"` before
86	/// giving up and producing SDP with whatever candidates have arrived.
87	///
88	/// Caps the failure mode where one of the configured STUN servers is
89	/// unreachable (e.g. `stun.l.google.com` from mainland China — blocked
90	/// by the GFW), which otherwise hangs the whole `connect_as_*` flow
91	/// indefinitely.
92	///
93	/// 5s is generous: pure-LAN gathering completes in 100-200 ms, healthy
94	/// STUN gathering in <1 s. If we hit the timeout we still produce a
95	/// valid SDP — there will just be fewer candidates than "ideal".
96	const ICE_GATHER_TIMEOUT_MS: i32 = 5000;
97	
98	/// Typed wrapper around the host's offer SDP blob (JSON envelope of
99	/// `{type:"offer", sdp:"..."}`). Prevents accidentally passing an
100	/// answer where an offer is expected.
101	#[derive(Clone, Debug)]
102	pub struct OfferBlob(pub String);
103	
104	/// Typed wrapper around the joiner's answer SDP blob.
105	#[derive(Clone, Debug)]
106	pub struct AnswerBlob(pub String);
107	
108	/// Live diagnostic snapshot of the underlying RTCPeerConnection. Exposed
109	/// to the LAN host/join pages so they can show users WHY pairing is
110	/// stuck (e.g. "ICE: failed, candidates: 1") instead of just timing out
111	/// with no insight. Updated by listeners installed in
112	/// `install_ice_diag_handlers` + the gather-state listener inside
113	/// `wait_for_ice_complete`.
114	///
115	/// The cross-device LAN failure documented in
116	/// `pitfalls/webrtc-mdns-lan-ap-isolation.md` shows up here as
117	/// `ice = Checking` flipping to `Disconnected`/`Failed` while the page
118	/// is staring at the 10 s DC-open wait.
119	#[derive(Copy, Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Eq)]
120	pub struct IceDiag {
121	    pub ice: RtcIceConnectionState,
122	    pub conn: RtcPeerConnectionState,
123	    pub gather: RtcIceGatheringState,
124	    /// Count of non-null `icecandidate` events fired since handler
125	    /// installation. A pure-`.local`-only offer typically lands at 1;
126	    /// healthy STUN adds 1–3 srflx candidates on top.
127	    pub candidates: u32,
128	}
129	
130	impl IceDiag {
131	    pub fn initial(pc: &RtcPeerConnection) -> Self {
132	        Self {
133	            ice: pc.ice_connection_state(),
134	            conn: pc.connection_state(),
135	            gather: pc.ice_gathering_state(),
136	            candidates: 0,
137	        }
138	    }
139	}
140	// SECTION: WebRtcTransport (impl Transport)
141	
142	/// `Transport` impl backed by a single `RtcDataChannel`. Shared by both
143	/// joiner and host sides — the same DataChannel just carries different
144	/// payloads in each direction (joiner sends `ClientMsg`, receives
145	/// `ServerMsg`; host the inverse, but Phase 4 wraps it differently
146	/// rather than reusing this trait directly for the host send path).
147	pub struct WebRtcTransport {
148	    /// `Rc<RefCell<...>>` so the DataChannel reference can be late-bound
149	    /// (joiner side discovers it via `ondatachannel` when the host's
150	    /// channel arrives, after `connect_as_joiner` returns).
151	    dc: Rc<RefCell<Option<RtcDataChannel>>>,
152	}
153	
154	impl Transport for WebRtcTransport {
155	    fn send(&self, msg: ClientMsg) -> bool {
156	        let dc = self.dc.borrow();
157	        let dc = match dc.as_ref() {
158	            Some(dc) => dc,
159	            None => return false,
160	        };
161	        if dc.ready_state() != RtcDataChannelState::Open {
162	            return false;
163	        }
164	        let body = match serde_json::to_string(&msg) {
165	            Ok(s) => s,
166	            Err(_) => return false,
167	        };
168	        dc.send_with_str(&body).is_ok()
169	    }
170	}
171	// SECTION: HostHandshake
172	
173	/// What `connect_as_host` returns.
174	///
175	/// Phase 4 (`host_room.rs`) consumes this: it owns the `Room` state
176	/// machine, holds the `pc` + `dc` references, and routes incoming
177	/// `ClientMsg`s through `Room::apply` to produce per-peer `ServerMsg`
178	/// fan-outs. Phase 3 just exposes the raw handles + the
179	/// offer/accept-answer dance.
180	///
181	/// Note: there's no `Session` here. The host doesn't consume the
182	/// `Transport` trait directly — it operates one level lower because
183	/// it needs to tag incoming bytes by `PeerId` and demultiplex
184	/// outgoing bytes back to specific peers' DataChannels (Phase 4
185	/// adds spectator channels).
186	pub struct HostHandshake {
187	    pub pc: RtcPeerConnection,
188	    /// Slot for the host-side DataChannel. Populated synchronously
189	    /// during `connect_as_host` (the host creates the channel before
190	    /// generating the offer); consumers may `clone()` to drive it.
191	    pub dc: Rc<RefCell<Option<RtcDataChannel>>>,
192	    pub offer: OfferBlob,
193	    /// Reactive view of the joiner's connection state. `Open` once
194	    /// the DataChannel handshake completes; `Closed`/`Error` on
195	    /// failure. Phase 4's `host_room.rs` watches this to know when
196	    /// to emit `Hello` / detect peer disconnect.
197	    pub state: ReadSignal<ConnState>,
198	    /// Live ICE / connection / gathering snapshot. The LAN host page
199	    /// shows this as a status badge and reads it when the DC-open
200	    /// timeout fires so the error message can name the actual stuck
```

```text
350	///
351	/// State signal flips to `Open` when the DataChannel handshake
352	/// completes (on top of `accept_answer` succeeding).
353	pub async fn connect_as_host(cfg: WebRtcConfig) -> Result<HostHandshake, JsValue> {
354	    let pc = new_peer_connection(cfg.ice_mode)?;
355	    let dc_init = {
356	        let init = RtcDataChannelInit::new();
357	        // reliable + ordered — the chess-net wire shape assumes
358	        // in-order delivery.
359	        init.set_ordered(true);
360	        init
361	    };
362	    let dc = pc.create_data_channel_with_data_channel_dict("chess", &dc_init);
363	    let dc_holder: Rc<RefCell<Option<RtcDataChannel>>> = Rc::new(RefCell::new(Some(dc.clone())));
364	    let mut keepalive: Vec<Closure<dyn FnMut(JsValue)>> = Vec::new();
365	
366	    let (state, set_state) = create_signal(ConnState::Connecting);
367	    install_dc_state_handlers(&dc, set_state);
368	    let (ice_diag, set_ice_diag) = create_signal(IceDiag::initial(&pc));
369	    install_ice_diag_handlers(&pc, set_ice_diag, &mut keepalive);
370	
371	    let offer = JsFuture::from(pc.create_offer()).await?;
372	    let offer_desc: RtcSessionDescriptionInit = offer.unchecked_into();
373	    JsFuture::from(pc.set_local_description(&offer_desc)).await?;
374	    wait_for_ice_complete(&pc, set_ice_diag, &mut keepalive).await;
375	
376	    let sdp = pc.local_description().map(|d| d.sdp()).unwrap_or_default();
377	    let offer_blob = OfferBlob(encode_sdp("offer", &sdp));
378	    Ok(HostHandshake {
379	        pc,
380	        dc: dc_holder,
381	        offer: offer_blob,
382	        state,
383	        ice_diag,
384	        _keepalive: keepalive,
385	    })
386	}
387	/// Block until `dc.ready_state() == "open"` OR `timeout_ms` elapses.
388	/// Returns `true` if the channel opened, `false` on timeout.
389	///
390	/// Used by the host page after `accept_answer` resolves: that
391	/// `await` completes as soon as `setRemoteDescription` returns,
392	/// which is BEFORE the SCTP handshake finishes. Calling
393	/// `dc.send_with_str(...)` on a still-`Connecting` DataChannel
394	/// silently fails (browsers return Err but the chess `PeerSink::Remote`
395	/// path discards the result), so the host's `Hello` + `ChatHistory`
396	/// fanout to the joiner would be lost. Awaiting this helper before
397	/// `HostRoom::attach_remote_player_dc` ensures the fanout reaches
398	/// the joiner.
399	///
400	/// Polls every 50 ms via `setTimeout`. Lower-level alternative would
401	/// be a Promise wrapping `dc.set_onopen`, but we already need the
402	/// timeout path for hostile-network failure cases, so polling is
403	/// simpler than two-channel select.
404	pub async fn wait_for_dc_open(dc: &RtcDataChannel, timeout_ms: u32) -> bool {
405	    let start = js_sys::Date::now();
406	    let deadline = start + timeout_ms as f64;
407	    loop {
408	        if dc.ready_state() == RtcDataChannelState::Open {
409	            return true;
410	        }
411	        if js_sys::Date::now() > deadline {
412	            return false;
413	        }
414	        let promise = js_sys::Promise::new(&mut |resolve, _reject| {
415	            if let Some(win) = web_sys::window() {
416	                let _ = win.set_timeout_with_callback_and_timeout_and_arguments_0(&resolve, 50);
417	            }
418	        });
419	        let _ = JsFuture::from(promise).await;
420	    }
421	}
422	// SECTION: SDP envelope encode/decode
423	
424	/// Wrap `(type, sdp)` in a JSON envelope so the textarea contents survive
425	/// copy-paste through clipboard / AirDrop / IM apps that helpfully
426	/// "normalise" line endings (SDP per RFC 4566 demands CRLF; many
427	/// transports strip the CR).
428	///
429	/// Pretty-printed with 2-space indent so the contents eyeball the same
430	/// as raw SDP — useful while debugging.
431	pub fn encode_sdp(kind: &str, sdp: &str) -> String {
432	    format!("{{\n  \"type\": \"{}\",\n  \"sdp\": \"{}\"\n}}", kind, escape_json_string(sdp))
433	}
434	
435	fn escape_json_string(s: &str) -> String {
436	    let mut out = String::with_capacity(s.len() + 8);
437	    for ch in s.chars() {
438	        match ch {
439	            '"' => out.push_str("\\\""),
440	            '\\' => out.push_str("\\\\"),
441	            '\n' => out.push_str("\\n"),
442	            '\r' => out.push_str("\\r"),
443	            '\t' => out.push_str("\\t"),
444	            c if (c as u32) < 0x20 => {
445	                out.push_str(&format!("\\u{:04x}", c as u32));
446	            }
447	            c => out.push(c),
448	        }
449	    }
450	    out
451	}
452	
453	fn decode_sdp_envelope(blob: &str) -> Result<(String, String), JsValue> {
454	    let trimmed = blob.trim();
455	    if !trimmed.starts_with('{') {
456	        return Err(JsValue::from_str(
457	            "SDP must be the JSON envelope produced by the host page (raw-SDP is not accepted)",
458	        ));
459	    }
460	    let parsed = js_sys::JSON::parse(trimmed)
461	        .map_err(|e| JsValue::from_str(&format!("SDP envelope is not valid JSON: {e:?}")))?;
462	    let kind =
463	        Reflect::get(&parsed, &"type".into()).ok().and_then(|v| v.as_string()).unwrap_or_default();
464	    let sdp =
465	        Reflect::get(&parsed, &"sdp".into()).ok().and_then(|v| v.as_string()).unwrap_or_default();
466	    if kind.is_empty() || sdp.is_empty() {
467	        return Err(JsValue::from_str("SDP envelope JSON missing `type` or `sdp` field"));
468	    }
469	    // Browsers accept LF-only SDP in practice but normalise anyway —
470	    // copy-paste through AirDrop / SMS / IM may strip CR.
471	    let sdp = sdp.replace('\r', "").replace('\n', "\r\n");
472	    Ok((kind, sdp))
473	}
474	
475	fn decode_offer(blob: &OfferBlob) -> Result<String, JsValue> {
476	    let (kind, sdp) = decode_sdp_envelope(&blob.0)?;
477	    if kind != "offer" {
478	        return Err(JsValue::from_str(&format!("expected offer envelope, got `{kind}`")));
479	    }
480	    Ok(sdp)
481	}
482	
483	fn decode_answer(blob: &AnswerBlob) -> Result<String, JsValue> {
484	    let (kind, sdp) = decode_sdp_envelope(&blob.0)?;
485	    if kind != "answer" {
486	        return Err(JsValue::from_str(&format!("expected answer envelope, got `{kind}`")));
487	    }
488	    Ok(sdp)
489	}
490	// SECTION: ICE config + ICE-gather wait helper
491	
492	fn new_peer_connection(mode: IceMode) -> Result<RtcPeerConnection, JsValue> {
493	    let cfg = RtcConfiguration::new();
494	    let servers: Array = Array::new();
495	    if mode == IceMode::WithStun {
496	        // Multi-server STUN list, ordered for CN reachability:
497	        //   - stun.miwifi.com — Xiaomi's own STUN; reachable from CN.
498	        //   - stun.qq.com     — Tencent's; reachable from CN.
499	        //   - stun.cloudflare.com — Cloudflare; usually reachable.
```
