```text
1	//! Multi-room ws server. Routes:
2	//!
3	//!   GET /             → upgrade into default room "main" (back-compat)
4	//!   GET /ws           → upgrade into default room "main" (back-compat)
5	//!   GET /ws/:room_id  → upgrade into named room (auto-created on first join)
6	//!   GET /lobby        → subscribe to live `Rooms` pushes
7	//!   GET /rooms        → JSON snapshot for `curl`/debug
8	//!
9	//! Optional `?password=<secret>` query param on the game-room routes locks
10	//! the room to the first joiner's secret. Wrong password gets
11	//! `Error{"bad password"}` + close.
12	//!
13	//! Optional `?role=spectator` upgrades the connection as a read-only
14	//! spectator instead of a seat. Spectators receive `Spectating` (instead of
15	//! `Hello`) plus `ChatHistory`, then live `Update` and `Chat` pushes. They
16	//! cannot move, resign, request rematch, or send chat. Player connections
17	//! arrive without `?role=spectator` — back-compat means v2 clients keep
18	//! getting "room full" if they're the third joiner.
19	//!
20	//! Empty rooms are GC'd when the last seat disconnects **and** the last
21	//! spectator leaves, except `main` which is permanent so v1 clients always
22	//! have a stable default to land in.
23	//!
24	//! Reconnect / mixed-variant rooms / time controls / TLS still deferred —
25	//! see `TODO.md`.
26	//!
27	//! ## Refactor history
28	//!
29	//! As of 2026-05-11 (Phase 1 of `backlog/webrtc-lan-pairing.md`) all room
30	//! state lives in [`crate::room::Room`] — a transport-agnostic state
31	//! machine that compiles for `wasm32-unknown-unknown`. This file is now
32	//! a thin axum/tokio glue that owns:
33	//!
34	//! 1. The per-room `Arc<Mutex<RoomEntry>>` table (room + peer routing map).
35	//! 2. The lobby-summary projection + push fan-out.
36	//! 3. `?password=`/`?role=`/`?hints=` URL parsing.
37	//! 4. The `--static-dir` static-file serving.
38	//!
39	//! Per-message work is just: deserialize → `room.apply(peer, msg)` →
40	//! fan out the returned `Vec<Outbound>` via the per-room routing table.
41	
42	// === module skeleton — section bodies filled in via edits below ===
43	
44	// SECTION: imports
45	use std::collections::HashMap;
46	use std::net::SocketAddr;
47	use std::sync::atomic::{AtomicU64, Ordering};
48	use std::sync::Arc;
49	
50	use anyhow::{Context, Result};
51	use axum::extract::ws::{Message, WebSocket, WebSocketUpgrade};
52	use axum::extract::{Path, Query, State};
53	#[cfg(feature = "static-serve")]
54	use axum::http::{header, HeaderValue};
55	#[cfg(feature = "static-serve")]
56	use axum::middleware::Next;
57	use axum::response::IntoResponse;
58	use axum::routing::get;
59	use axum::{Json, Router};
60	use chess_core::rules::RuleSet;
61	use futures_util::stream::StreamExt;
62	use futures_util::SinkExt;
63	use serde::Deserialize;
64	use tokio::sync::{mpsc, Mutex};
65	
66	use crate::protocol::{ClientMsg, RoomSummary, ServerMsg};
67	use crate::room::{
68	    parse_hints_param, valid_password, valid_room_id, JoinError, Outbound, PeerId, Room,
69	    DEFAULT_MAX_SPECTATORS, DEFAULT_ROOM, MAX_PASSWORD_LEN, MAX_ROOM_ID_LEN,
70	};
71	// SECTION: cache constants (static-serve)
72	#[cfg(feature = "static-serve")]
73	const IMMUTABLE_ASSET_CACHE: &str = "public, max-age=31536000, immutable";
74	#[cfg(feature = "static-serve")]
75	const HTML_CACHE: &str = "no-cache";
76	// SECTION: app state types
77	
78	/// Per-connection outbound channel. The handler hands one of these to the
79	/// per-room peer map; the `Room::apply` fan-out delivers `ServerMsg`s into
80	/// it; a per-connection write task drains it onto the WebSocket sink.
81	type PeerSender = mpsc::UnboundedSender<ServerMsg>;
82	
83	/// One row in the per-room peer routing table.
84	struct RoomEntry {
85	    room: Room,
86	    peers: HashMap<PeerId, PeerSender>,
87	}
88	
89	impl RoomEntry {
90	    fn new(room: Room) -> Self {
91	        Self { room, peers: HashMap::new() }
92	    }
93	
94	    /// Deliver an `Outbound` list to the matching peer senders. Drops any
95	    /// outbound whose target peer has already disconnected — its sender
96	    /// is gone from the map.
97	    fn fanout(&self, out: Vec<Outbound>) {
98	        for ob in out {
99	            if let Some(tx) = self.peers.get(&ob.peer) {
100	                let _ = tx.send(ob.msg);
101	            }
102	        }
103	    }
104	}
105	
106	type RoomMap = Arc<Mutex<HashMap<String, Arc<Mutex<RoomEntry>>>>>;
107	type SummaryMap = Arc<Mutex<HashMap<String, RoomSummary>>>;
108	type LobbySubs = Arc<Mutex<Vec<mpsc::UnboundedSender<ServerMsg>>>>;
109	
110	/// Server-wide state shared across every connection. Lock acquisition order
111	/// when multiple maps are touched: `rooms` → `summaries` → `lobby_subs`.
112	/// Per-room inner locks (`Arc<Mutex<RoomEntry>>`) live separately and are
113	/// never held across an outer-map lock acquisition.
114	pub struct AppState {
115	    rooms: RoomMap,
116	    /// Lobby-visible projection of every live room. Updated by
117	    /// `refresh_summary` / `drop_summary` after every room mutation, so
118	    /// `notify_lobby` can build a `Rooms` push without touching any inner
119	    /// `Room` lock.
120	    summaries: SummaryMap,
121	    /// Outbound senders for every connected lobby socket.
122	    lobby_subs: LobbySubs,
123	    /// Variant for every auto-created room. Mixed-variant servers are a
124	    /// separate TODO (`chess-net: mixed-variant rooms`).
125	    default_rules: RuleSet,
126	    /// Per-room cap on `?role=spectator` connections. The 17th spectator
127	    /// (at the default cap of 16) gets `Error{"room watch capacity reached"}`.
128	    max_spectators: usize,
129	    /// Monotonic counter for `PeerId` allocation. Only the value's
130	    /// uniqueness within a single room matters; using one global counter
131	    /// keeps the implementation trivial.
132	    next_peer_id: AtomicU64,
133	}
134	
135	impl AppState {
136	    fn new(default_rules: RuleSet, max_spectators: usize) -> Arc<Self> {
137	        Arc::new(Self {
138	            rooms: Arc::new(Mutex::new(HashMap::new())),
139	            summaries: Arc::new(Mutex::new(HashMap::new())),
140	            lobby_subs: Arc::new(Mutex::new(Vec::new())),
141	            default_rules,
142	            max_spectators,
143	            next_peer_id: AtomicU64::new(1),
144	        })
145	    }
146	
147	    fn alloc_peer(&self) -> PeerId {
148	        PeerId(self.next_peer_id.fetch_add(1, Ordering::Relaxed))
149	    }
150	}
151	// SECTION: query types
152	
153	#[derive(Debug, Deserialize)]
154	struct AuthQuery {
155	    #[serde(default)]
156	    password: Option<String>,
157	    /// Opt-in spectator request. `Some("spectator")` upgrades the connection
158	    /// as a read-only watcher. v2 clients never set this so back-compat
159	    /// behaviour is preserved (third joiner still gets "room full").
160	    #[serde(default)]
161	    role: Option<String>,
162	    /// Opt-in AI hint sanction. `Some("1")` / `Some("true")` from the
163	    /// **first joiner** sets `Room.hints_allowed = true` for the room's
164	    /// lifetime; subsequent joiners' values are ignored.
165	    #[serde(default)]
166	    hints: Option<String>,
167	}
168	
169	#[derive(Copy, Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Eq)]
170	enum JoinRole {
171	    Player,
172	    Spectator,
173	}
174	
175	fn parse_role(raw: Option<&str>) -> JoinRole {
176	    match raw {
177	        Some(s) if s.eq_ignore_ascii_case("spectator") => JoinRole::Spectator,
178	        _ => JoinRole::Player,
179	    }
180	}

Now I have the full picture. Let me compile my findings into a comprehensive report.

---

## Architecture Summary (Sub-400 words)

### A. `/lan/host` & `/lan/join` Structure

**`clients/chess-web/src/pages/lan.rs`** (938 lines):
- **`LanHostPage`** (lines 125–645): Host initiates pairing. Form state includes:
  - `variant` (xiangqi/banqi radio), rules checkboxes (chain, dark, rush, horse, etc.)
  - `chosen_rules` signal captures the `RuleSet` **before** the offer is sent (line 261)
  - Workflow: Idle → Generating (offer) → AwaitingAnswer → AcceptingAnswer → Playing
  - **Critical: rules are committed at `on_open` (line 261), recovered at `on_accept` (line 301), then passed to `HostRoom::new(rules, None, false)` (line 353)**
  
- **`LanJoinPage`** (lines 658–918): Joiner consumes offer, generates answer. No rule picker — joiner is **passive** in rule selection; they see the host's chosen rules only after connection succeeds (implicitly via game state).

### B. Wire Format for Host→Joiner Config Exchange

**`clients/chess-web/src/transport/webrtc.rs`** (lines 431–488):
- **SDP envelope** is a JSON wrapper, not raw SDP:
  ```rust
  pub fn encode_sdp(kind: &str, sdp: &str) -> String {
      format!("{{\n  \"type\": \"{}\",\n  \"sdp\": \"{}\"\n}}", kind, escape_json_string(sdp))
  }
  ```
  - Type: `"offer"` or `"answer"` (string field)
  - SDP: escaped raw WebRTC Session Description
  - **Status quo: NO variant/rules fields embedded in the offer.** The offer is pure SDP + type.

- **Host→Joiner rule communication path TODAY:**
  1. Host picks rules on `/lan/host`, commits to `chosen_rules`
  2. Host generates offer (SDP only, no rules metadata)
  3. Joiner receives offer SDP, generates answer (also SDP only)
  4. Host accepts answer, calls `HostRoom::new(chosen_rules, ...)` locally
  5. `HostRoom` seats host as RED (side hard-coded in `host_room.rs:171`)
  6. Game state (rules, side) flows **via DataChannel `ServerMsg`** (`Hello` + game updates) after DC opens

- **Implication for banqi "Who flips first?" / xiangqi "I play as Red/Black":**
  - These must **either** go in the offer envelope JSON **or** be communicated via a new DataChannel protocol message before `Hello` is sent.

### C. chess-net Server Rules Handling

**`crates/chess-net/src/server.rs`** (line 125, 413):
- Server stores `default_rules: RuleSet` (CLI arg, baked at startup)
- All rooms use the same rules: `Room::new(app.default_rules.clone(), password, hints)` (line 412–415)
- **Per-room rule customization: NOT supported today.** All chess-net rooms are forced to the CLI's default.
- LAN play (via `HostRoom`) **bypasses this** — host specifies rules directly at `HostRoom::new(rules, ...)`.

### D. Picker UI (Rule Components Reusability)

**`clients/chess-web/src/pages/picker.rs`** (lines 158–473):
- `XiangqiCard` (lines 158–300): Strict checkbox, mode radio (Pvp/VsAi), player-side radio ("You play Red/Black")
- `BanqiCard` (built-in to local, not yet extracted): Preset buttons (Purist/Taiwan/Aggressive), 5 house-rule checkboxes
- **Reusable for `/lan/host`:** Yes. The form pattern (RW signals per checkbox) is already mirrored in `lan.rs:162–170` for banqi. For xiangqi "player side," host needs a new 3-way (Red / Black / Random) picker — `picker.rs` doesn't expose this separately yet.

---

**Key Wire-Format Gap:** The offer/answer envelope has no `rules` or `side` fields. Host→Joiner config exchange is currently **implicit** (decoded from game state via DataChannel after connection). To support explicit host presets, extend `encode_sdp()` to include optional metadata.

**Banqi first-flipper choices on the host picker:**

Options:
- **Either / Host / Joiner (default: Either)** - Three options. "Either" preserves the original-request behaviour (no pre-commitment); "Host"/"Joiner" pre-decide which seat clicks first. Default is fully fluid. (Recommended)
- **Host / Joiner (default: Host)** - Two options only. Always someone is pre-decided to flip first; matches the simpler 'host先手翻 / joiner後手翻' framing. No 'either' option.
- **Either / Host / Joiner (default: Host)** - Three options. Default matches current behaviour (host = first flipper). Users opt INTO 'Either' or 'Joiner' explicitly.

- **Red / Black (default: Red)** - Simple two-option radio; preserves current default.
- **Red / Black / Random (default: Random)** - Random by default — most 'fair' but a surprising default change.

```md
# Banqi defers colour until first flip + host-side game-setup presets

## Context

Today's game-room behaviour is hard-coded:

- **Banqi** — `setup.rs::build_banqi` always seeds `side_to_move = Side::RED` and `side_assignment = None`. The first room-seat (RED) is forced to make the first reveal; only after that flip does `state.side_assignment` lock in who plays which colour (Taiwan rule: flipper plays the colour they reveal, see `state/mod.rs::banqi_side_assignment`).
- **Xiangqi** — the first joiner is always seated as RED (`chess-net/src/room.rs::next_seat` line 198, and `chess-web/src/host_room.rs:171` for the WebRTC variant).
- **chess-net WS server** — rooms inherit a single CLI-baked `RuleSet`. No per-room customisation.
- **chess-web `/lan/host`** — host picks variant + `HouseRules`, but cannot pick which colour they play nor (for banqi) who flips first.

The user wants two related changes:

1. **Banqi defaults to "either seat flips first"** — no colour pre-commitment until a real flip. Pre-assigning colours becomes an opt-in `HouseRules` flag.
2. **Host-side game-setup presets** — when creating a room (either via WebRTC `/lan/host` or chess-net WS), the host can pre-configure:
   - **Banqi first-flipper**: `Either` (default) / `Host` / `Joiner`
   - **Xiangqi host colour**: `Red` (default) / `Black` / `Random`

Both must work on both deployment paths: WebRTC peer-to-peer LAN play and the central chess-net WS server.

## Approach

The work splits into 7 phases. Phases 1–2 are the core "either flips first" behaviour and are independently shippable. Phases 3–5 add the host-preset UI/protocol surface across both deployments. Phases 6–7 are display tweaks and docs.

### Phase 1 — Engine: banqi defers colour until first flip

`crates/chess-core/src/`:

- `rules/house.rs`: add bit `PREASSIGN_COLORS = 1 << 6`. Banqi-only semantics. Default OFF.
- `state/mod.rs`: helper
  \```rust
  pub fn banqi_awaiting_first_flip(&self) -> bool {
      matches!(self.rules.variant, Variant::Banqi)
          && self.side_assignment.is_none()
          && !self.rules.house.contains(HouseRules::PREASSIGN_COLORS)
  }
  \```
  No change to `make_move` — `banqi_side_assignment(flipper, revealed)` already reads `state.side_to_move` as the flipper, so the deployment layer just sets `side_to_move = <clicker's seat>` before calling `make_move` on the first reveal.
- `view.rs::PlayerView`: add `#[serde(default)] pub banqi_awaiting_first_flip: bool` (no `PROTOCOL_VERSION` bump — older payloads default to `false`). In `project`:
  \```rust
  let awaiting = state.banqi_awaiting_first_flip();
  let legal_moves = if observer == state.side_to_move || awaiting {
      sanitize_for_observer(state.legal_moves(), observer)
  } else { MoveList::new() };
  \```
  Both observers see reveals when awaiting; otherwise the existing side-of-move gate stands.

Tests in `crates/chess-core/tests/banqi_first_flip_color.rs`:
- Keep the existing "RED flips, locks side assignment" path but parameterise the rule set with `HouseRules::PREASSIGN_COLORS` so it documents the legacy mode.
- Add `default_rules_let_black_seat_flip_first`: build state, force `side_to_move = Side::BLACK`, apply `Move::Reveal`, assert `side_assignment` resolves with BLACK as the flipper.
- Add `view_pre_first_flip_exposes_legal_moves_to_both_observers`: `PlayerView::project` from both RED and BLACK sees the 32 reveals; after the first flip only the new `side_to_move` does.
- Add `view_with_preassign_colors_only_lets_red_see_legal_moves`: same setup with `PREASSIGN_COLORS` set, opposite assertion.

### Phase 2 — Deployment: server-side guard relax

Goal: let either seat make the first banqi reveal. No protocol message changes; only relax the existing "not your turn" guard.

`crates/chess-net/src/room.rs::process_move` (line 435):

\```rust
let is_first_banqi_reveal = matches!(mv, Move::Reveal { .. })
    && self.state.banqi_awaiting_first_flip();
if is_first_banqi_reveal {
    self.state.side_to_move = seat;          // attribute flip to the actual clicker
} else if self.state.side_to_move != seat {
    out.push(Outbound { peer: from,
        msg: ServerMsg::Error { message: "not your turn".into() } });
    return;
}
// existing make_move / broadcast_update continues
\```

Same logic for `clients/chess-web/src/host_room.rs`'s peer-message router (HostRoom wraps the same `Room::apply` path, so once `room.rs` is patched both deployments inherit the fix).

Add a chess-net integration test: two peers join a banqi room with default rules; the second (BLACK seat) sends `Reveal` first; assert it succeeds, `Update` broadcasts to both, and the new `current_color` reflects BLACK-seat-plays-revealed-colour. Add the negative test: same setup with `?preassign=1` URL param (introduced in Phase 3); BLACK's first reveal returns `Error { "not your turn" }`.

### Phase 3 — Per-room config: protocol surface

A new `RoomConfig` struct carries host presets through both deployments. Fields:

\```rust
pub struct RoomConfig {
    pub rules: RuleSet,                          // variant + house bits
    pub host_color: Option<Side>,                // None = system default (RED)
    pub first_flipper: FirstFlipper,             // Either (default) / Host / Joiner
}

pub enum FirstFlipper { Either, Host, Joiner }
\```

`FirstFlipper` only applies to banqi when `PREASSIGN_COLORS` is OFF. Otherwise the colour is fixed and the engine's existing "Red moves first" rule decides.

#### Wire formats

**chess-net WS URL params** (extending the existing `?password=` precedent):
- `?host_color=red|black|random`
- `?first_flipper=either|host|joiner`
- `?preassign=1`

Parsed inside `room.rs::join_player` only when the room is first created (subsequent joiners' params are ignored — the room's config is locked). Persisted on `RoomState` for the lobby `Rooms` snapshot.

**chess-web `/lan/host` → `/lan/join` offer envelope** (`transport/webrtc.rs::encode_sdp` line 431):

Extend the JSON envelope to carry an optional config payload:
\```rust
pub fn encode_sdp_v2(kind: &str, sdp: &str, config: Option<&RoomConfig>) -> String { … }
\```
Old envelopes deserialise via `serde(default)` and yield `config = None` → falls back to the engine's default rules (back-compat with any in-flight invitations from the previous version).

#### chess-net seat assignment

`room.rs::next_seat` currently always returns RED first, BLACK second. Update to:
\```rust
pub fn next_seat(&self) -> Option<Side> {
    match self.seats.len() {
        0 => Some(self.config.host_color.unwrap_or(Side::RED)),
        1 => Some(self.seats[0].0.opposite()),
        _ => None,
    }
}
\```
`host_color = Random` is resolved to a concrete `Side` once at room creation (server uses `ChaCha8Rng` for determinism with `?random_seed=...`, or `thread_rng` otherwise).

#### Initial side_to_move (banqi only)

After `build_banqi` runs (`side_to_move = Side::RED` by default), the room layer overrides:
\```rust
match config.first_flipper {
    FirstFlipper::Either => { /* leave side_to_move untouched; banqi_awaiting_first_flip() == true allows either */ }
    FirstFlipper::Host   => state.side_to_move = config.host_color.unwrap_or(Side::RED),
    FirstFlipper::Joiner => state.side_to_move = config.host_color.unwrap_or(Side::RED).opposite(),
}
\```

### Phase 4 — chess-web `/lan/host` picker UI

`clients/chess-web/src/pages/lan.rs::LanHostPage`:

- After the existing variant radio + house-rule checkboxes, render new fields:
  - **Xiangqi** (and banqi when `PREASSIGN_COLORS` checked): "I play as: Red / Black / Random" radio. Default Red.
  - **Banqi** (when `PREASSIGN_COLORS` unchecked): "First flipper: Either / Host / Joiner" radio. Default Either.
- Bind to a new signal `chosen_config: RwSignal<RoomConfig>`; replace the existing `chosen_rules` commit path with one that builds the full `RoomConfig`.
- Pass `Some(&config)` into the new `encode_sdp_v2` envelope so the joiner sees the host's choices before accepting.
- `HostRoom::new` signature extends to `new(config: RoomConfig, password: Option<String>, hints: bool)`; the line-171 seat assignment uses `config.host_color`.

`clients/chess-web/src/pages/lan.rs::LanJoinPage`:

- Parse the offer envelope; show the host's chosen variant + rules + colour preferences as a read-only summary above the "Accept invite" button. ("Host plays Black — you will be Red and move first." style copy.)

### Phase 5 — chess-tui room-creation flow

`clients/chess-tui/src/`:

- The lobby's "create room" prompt (`c` key in lobby) currently takes only a room name. Extend it to a multi-line form:
  - Variant (radio)
  - Strict / casual (xiangqi)
  - House rules (banqi)
  - Host colour (xiangqi + banqi-preassign)
  - First flipper (banqi-no-preassign)
- The resulting `RoomConfig` is encoded into the URL query string when connecting: `ws://host/ws/room?host_color=black&first_flipper=joiner&preassign=1`.
- Direct `--connect` invocation: add CLI flags `--host-color`, `--first-flipper`, `--preassign` that emit the same query string.

### Phase 6 — Pre-first-flip sidebar text

`clients/chess-tui/src/ui.rs` (sidebar lines 1876–1893) and `clients/chess-web/src/pages/play.rs` (lines 608–637) plus `pages/local.rs`:

- When `view.banqi_awaiting_first_flip` is true, replace "Red to move" / "Your turn:" / "Opponent:" with a single neutral string. Same code path serves Local and Net.
- **Copy**: `Awaiting first flip — either side may flip` (English); `未翻牌 — 任一方皆可先翻` (CJK glyph fallback). One helper per client, kept locally — no new shared crate per the existing `orient.rs` / `glyph.rs` duplication policy.
- Web click guard at `pages/play.rs:464-467`: when `v.banqi_awaiting_first_flip`, allow the click regardless of `v.observer == v.side_to_move`. Server is still authoritative.

### Phase 7 — Documentation

- `docs/rules/banqi.md`: new section **First-flip & colour assignment** describing the default ("either side may flip; revealed colour determines mapping per Taiwan rule") and the legacy `PREASSIGN_COLORS` mode.
- `docs/rules/banqi-house.md`: add a `PREASSIGN_COLORS` row (`1 << 6`, default OFF, banqi-only).
- `docs/adr/`: new ADR-000N **Per-room host presets via URL params + offer envelope** capturing why we chose URL params over a `ClientMsg::CreateRoom` message (back-compat, simpler, no protocol-version bump on the existing message types).

## Phasing & shippability

Each phase is mergeable on its own:

- **P1 alone**: engine gains `PREASSIGN_COLORS` bit + helper + view field. Tests pass. No user-visible change because the deployment layer still always uses `side_to_move = RED`.
- **P1 + P2**: net+host-room let either seat flip first in banqi default rules. UI still says "Red to move" pre-flip (cosmetic). New TUI/web sidebar copy can land in P6.
- **P3 alone (on top of P2)**: protocol surface for host config — server seat assignment honours `host_color`; banqi first-flipper preference flows through.
- **P4/P5**: UI to drive P3.

If sequencing pressure exists, P6 (sidebar text) can land with P2 since it doesn't depend on P3+.

## Critical files

| `crates/chess-core/src/rules/house.rs` | add `PREASSIGN_COLORS = 1 << 6` |

## Reused primitives

- `state.banqi_side_assignment(flipper, revealed)` (`state/mod.rs:319-332`) already encodes the Taiwan rule — once the deployment layer sets `side_to_move = clicker_seat`, mapping is correct.
- `routes.rs::parse_local_rules` (URL token round-trip) extends by one token.
- `ChaCha8Rng` (already imported for `banqi_with_seed`) handles the deterministic side-coin-flip for `host_color = Random`.

## Approved Plan:
# Banqi defers colour until first flip + host-side game-setup presets

## Context

Today's game-room behaviour is hard-coded:

- **Banqi** — `setup.rs::build_banqi` always seeds `side_to_move = Side::RED` and `side_assignment = None`. The first room-seat (RED) is forced to make the first reveal; only after that flip does `state.side_assignment` lock in who plays which colour (Taiwan rule: flipper plays the colour they reveal, see `state/mod.rs::banqi_side_assignment`).
- **Xiangqi** — the first joiner is always seated as RED (`chess-net/src/room.rs::next_seat` line 198, and `chess-web/src/host_room.rs:171` for the WebRTC variant).
- **chess-net WS server** — rooms inherit a single CLI-baked `RuleSet`. No per-room customisation.
- **chess-web `/lan/host`** — host picks variant + `HouseRules`, but cannot pick which colour they play nor (for banqi) who flips first.

The user wants two related changes:

1. **Banqi defaults to "either seat flips first"** — no colour pre-commitment until a real flip. Pre-assigning colours becomes an opt-in `HouseRules` flag.
2. **Host-side game-setup presets** — when creating a room (either via WebRTC `/lan/host` or chess-net WS), the host can pre-configure:
   - **Banqi first-flipper**: `Either` (default) / `Host` / `Joiner`
   - **Xiangqi host colour**: `Red` (default) / `Black` / `Random`

Both must work on both deployment paths: WebRTC peer-to-peer LAN play and the central chess-net WS server.

## Approach

The work splits into 7 phases. Phases 1–2 are the core "either flips first" behaviour and are independently shippable. Phases 3–5 add the host-preset UI/protocol surface across both deployments. Phases 6–7 are display tweaks and docs.

### Phase 1 — Engine: banqi defers colour until first flip

`crates/chess-core/src/`:

- `rules/house.rs`: add bit `PREASSIGN_COLORS = 1 << 6`. Banqi-only semantics. Default OFF.
- `state/mod.rs`: helper
  ```rust
  pub fn banqi_awaiting_first_flip(&self) -> bool {
      matches!(self.rules.variant, Variant::Banqi)
          && self.side_assignment.is_none()
          && !self.rules.house.contains(HouseRules::PREASSIGN_COLORS)
  }
  ```
  No change to `make_move` — `banqi_side_assignment(flipper, revealed)` already reads `state.side_to_move` as the flipper, so the deployment layer just sets `side_to_move = <clicker's seat>` before calling `make_move` on the first reveal.
- `view.rs::PlayerView`: add `#[serde(default)] pub banqi_awaiting_first_flip: bool` (no `PROTOCOL_VERSION` bump — older payloads default to `false`). In `project`:
  ```rust
  let awaiting = state.banqi_awaiting_first_flip();
  let legal_moves = if observer == state.side_to_move || awaiting {
      sanitize_for_observer(state.legal_moves(), observer)
  } else { MoveList::new() };
  ```
  Both observers see reveals when awaiting; otherwise the existing side-of-move gate stands.

Tests in `crates/chess-core/tests/banqi_first_flip_color.rs`:
- Keep the existing "RED flips, locks side assignment" path but parameterise the rule set with `HouseRules::PREASSIGN_COLORS` so it documents the legacy mode.
- Add `default_rules_let_black_seat_flip_first`: build state, force `side_to_move = Side::BLACK`, apply `Move::Reveal`, assert `side_assignment` resolves with BLACK as the flipper.
- Add `view_pre_first_flip_exposes_legal_moves_to_both_observers`: `PlayerView::project` from both RED and BLACK sees the 32 reveals; after the first flip only the new `side_to_move` does.
- Add `view_with_preassign_colors_only_lets_red_see_legal_moves`: same setup with `PREASSIGN_COLORS` set, opposite assertion.

### Phase 2 — Deployment: server-side guard relax

Goal: let either seat make the first banqi reveal. No protocol message changes; only relax the existing "not your turn" guard.

`crates/chess-net/src/room.rs::process_move` (line 435):

```rust
let is_first_banqi_reveal = matches!(mv, Move::Reveal { .. })
    && self.state.banqi_awaiting_first_flip();
if is_first_banqi_reveal {
    self.state.side_to_move = seat;          // attribute flip to the actual clicker
} else if self.state.side_to_move != seat {
    out.push(Outbound { peer: from,
        msg: ServerMsg::Error { message: "not your turn".into() } });
    return;
}
// existing make_move / broadcast_update continues
```

Same logic for `clients/chess-web/src/host_room.rs`'s peer-message router (HostRoom wraps the same `Room::apply` path, so once `room.rs` is patched both deployments inherit the fix).

Add a chess-net integration test: two peers join a banqi room with default rules; the second (BLACK seat) sends `Reveal` first; assert it succeeds, `Update` broadcasts to both, and the new `current_color` reflects BLACK-seat-plays-revealed-colour. Add the negative test: same setup with `?preassign=1` URL param (introduced in Phase 3); BLACK's first reveal returns `Error { "not your turn" }`.

### Phase 3 — Per-room config: protocol surface

A new `RoomConfig` struct carries host presets through both deployments. Fields:

```rust
pub struct RoomConfig {
    pub rules: RuleSet,                          // variant + house bits
    pub host_color: Option<Side>,                // None = system default (RED)
    pub first_flipper: FirstFlipper,             // Either (default) / Host / Joiner
}

pub enum FirstFlipper { Either, Host, Joiner }
```

`FirstFlipper` only applies to banqi when `PREASSIGN_COLORS` is OFF. Otherwise the colour is fixed and the engine's existing "Red moves first" rule decides.

#### Wire formats

**chess-net WS URL params** (extending the existing `?password=` precedent):
- `?host_color=red|black|random`
- `?first_flipper=either|host|joiner`
- `?preassign=1`

Parsed inside `room.rs::join_player` only when the room is first created (subsequent joiners' params are ignored — the room's config is locked). Persisted on `RoomState` for the lobby `Rooms` snapshot.

**chess-web `/lan/host` → `/lan/join` offer envelope** (`transport/webrtc.rs::encode_sdp` line 431):

Extend the JSON envelope to carry an optional config payload:
```rust
pub fn encode_sdp_v2(kind: &str, sdp: &str, config: Option<&RoomConfig>) -> String { … }
```
Old envelopes deserialise via `serde(default)` and yield `config = None` → falls back to the engine's default rules (back-compat with any in-flight invitations from the previous version).

#### chess-net seat assignment

`room.rs::next_seat` currently always returns RED first, BLACK second. Update to:
```rust
pub fn next_seat(&self) -> Option<Side> {
    match self.seats.len() {
        0 => Some(self.config.host_color.unwrap_or(Side::RED)),
        1 => Some(self.seats[0].0.opposite()),
        _ => None,
    }
}
```
`host_color = Random` is resolved to a concrete `Side` once at room creation (server uses `ChaCha8Rng` for determinism with `?random_seed=...`, or `thread_rng` otherwise).

#### Initial side_to_move (banqi only)

After `build_banqi` runs (`side_to_move = Side::RED` by default), the room layer overrides:
```rust
match config.first_flipper {
    FirstFlipper::Either => { /* leave side_to_move untouched; banqi_awaiting_first_flip() == true allows either */ }
    FirstFlipper::Host   => state.side_to_move = config.host_color.unwrap_or(Side::RED),
    FirstFlipper::Joiner => state.side_to_move = config.host_color.unwrap_or(Side::RED).opposite(),
}
```

### Phase 4 — chess-web `/lan/host` picker UI

`clients/chess-web/src/pages/lan.rs::LanHostPage`:

- After the existing variant radio + house-rule checkboxes, render new fields:
  - **Xiangqi** (and banqi when `PREASSIGN_COLORS` checked): "I play as: Red / Black / Random" radio. Default Red.
  - **Banqi** (when `PREASSIGN_COLORS` unchecked): "First flipper: Either / Host / Joiner" radio. Default Either.
- Bind to a new signal `chosen_config: RwSignal<RoomConfig>`; replace the existing `chosen_rules` commit path with one that builds the full `RoomConfig`.
- Pass `Some(&config)` into the new `encode_sdp_v2` envelope so the joiner sees the host's choices before accepting.
- `HostRoom::new` signature extends to `new(config: RoomConfig, password: Option<String>, hints: bool)`; the line-171 seat assignment uses `config.host_color`.

`clients/chess-web/src/pages/lan.rs::LanJoinPage`:

- Parse the offer envelope; show the host's chosen variant + rules + colour preferences as a read-only summary above the "Accept invite" button. ("Host plays Black — you will be Red and move first." style copy.)

### Phase 5 — chess-tui room-creation flow

`clients/chess-tui/src/`:

- The lobby's "create room" prompt (`c` key in lobby) currently takes only a room name. Extend it to a multi-line form:
  - Variant (radio)
  - Strict / casual (xiangqi)
  - House rules (banqi)
  - Host colour (xiangqi + banqi-preassign)
  - First flipper (banqi-no-preassign)
- The resulting `RoomConfig` is encoded into the URL query string when connecting: `ws://host/ws/room?host_color=black&first_flipper=joiner&preassign=1`.
- Direct `--connect` invocation: add CLI flags `--host-color`, `--first-flipper`, `--preassign` that emit the same query string.

### Phase 6 — Pre-first-flip sidebar text

`clients/chess-tui/src/ui.rs` (sidebar lines 1876–1893) and `clients/chess-web/src/pages/play.rs` (lines 608–637) plus `pages/local.rs`:

- When `view.banqi_awaiting_first_flip` is true, replace "Red to move" / "Your turn:" / "Opponent:" with a single neutral string. Same code path serves Local and Net.
- **Copy**: `Awaiting first flip — either side may flip` (English); `未翻牌 — 任一方皆可先翻` (CJK glyph fallback). One helper per client, kept locally — no new shared crate per the existing `orient.rs` / `glyph.rs` duplication policy.
- Web click guard at `pages/play.rs:464-467`: when `v.banqi_awaiting_first_flip`, allow the click regardless of `v.observer == v.side_to_move`. Server is still authoritative.

### Phase 7 — Documentation

- `docs/rules/banqi.md`: new section **First-flip & colour assignment** describing the default ("either side may flip; revealed colour determines mapping per Taiwan rule") and the legacy `PREASSIGN_COLORS` mode.
- `docs/rules/banqi-house.md`: add a `PREASSIGN_COLORS` row (`1 << 6`, default OFF, banqi-only).
- `docs/adr/`: new ADR-000N **Per-room host presets via URL params + offer envelope** capturing why we chose URL params over a `ClientMsg::CreateRoom` message (back-compat, simpler, no protocol-version bump on the existing message types).

## Phasing & shippability

Each phase is mergeable on its own:

- **P1 alone**: engine gains `PREASSIGN_COLORS` bit + helper + view field. Tests pass. No user-visible change because the deployment layer still always uses `side_to_move = RED`.
- **P1 + P2**: net+host-room let either seat flip first in banqi default rules. UI still says "Red to move" pre-flip (cosmetic). New TUI/web sidebar copy can land in P6.
- **P3 alone (on top of P2)**: protocol surface for host config — server seat assignment honours `host_color`; banqi first-flipper preference flows through.
- **P4/P5**: UI to drive P3.

If sequencing pressure exists, P6 (sidebar text) can land with P2 since it doesn't depend on P3+.

```text
1	//! Banqi house rules.
2	//!
3	//! Five independent toggles + three named presets. See `docs/rules/banqi-house.md`.
4	
5	use bitflags::bitflags;
6	
7	bitflags! {
8	    /// Toggleable banqi house rules. Combine with `|`.
9	    #[derive(Copy, Clone, Eq, PartialEq, Debug, Default)]
10	    #[derive(serde::Serialize, serde::Deserialize)]
11	    pub struct HouseRules: u32 {
12	        /// 連吃 — chain captures along the same line.
13	        const CHAIN_CAPTURE       = 1 << 0;
14	        /// 暗吃 — atomic reveal+capture of a face-down piece (probe variant:
15	        /// rank-fail leaves both pieces in place with target now revealed).
16	        const DARK_CAPTURE        = 1 << 1;
17	        /// 車衝 — chariot moves multi-square in a line.
18	        const CHARIOT_RUSH        = 1 << 2;
19	        /// 馬斜 — horse adds 4 diagonal one-step moves; diagonal captures
20	        /// ignore rank (any piece).
21	        const HORSE_DIAGONAL      = 1 << 3;
22	        /// 炮快移 — cannon non-capturing move slides any distance.
23	        const CANNON_FAST_MOVE    = 1 << 4;
24	        /// 暗吃 trade variant — on rank-fail the small attacker dies (is
25	        /// removed), target stays revealed. Implies DARK_CAPTURE.
26	        const DARK_CAPTURE_TRADE  = 1 << 5;
27	    }
28	}
29	
30	/// Classic banqi, no house rules.
31	pub const PRESET_PURIST: HouseRules = HouseRules::empty();
32	
33	/// Common Taiwanese house ruleset: chain captures + chariot rush.
34	pub const PRESET_TAIWAN: HouseRules = HouseRules::CHAIN_CAPTURE.union(HouseRules::CHARIOT_RUSH);
35	
36	/// Aggressive ruleset enabling chain, dark-capture, chariot rush, horse
37	/// diagonal.
38	pub const PRESET_AGGRESSIVE: HouseRules = HouseRules::CHAIN_CAPTURE
39	    .union(HouseRules::DARK_CAPTURE)
40	    .union(HouseRules::CHARIOT_RUSH)
41	    .union(HouseRules::HORSE_DIAGONAL);
42	
43	/// `DARK_CAPTURE_TRADE` implies `DARK_CAPTURE`. Returns the canonical form.
44	pub fn normalize(mut rules: HouseRules) -> HouseRules {
45	    if rules.contains(HouseRules::DARK_CAPTURE_TRADE) {
46	        rules.insert(HouseRules::DARK_CAPTURE);
47	    }
48	    rules
49	}
50	
51	#[cfg(test)]
52	mod tests {
53	    use super::*;
54	
55	    #[test]
56	    fn presets_are_distinct() {
57	        assert_ne!(PRESET_PURIST, PRESET_TAIWAN);
58	        assert_ne!(PRESET_TAIWAN, PRESET_AGGRESSIVE);
59	        assert_ne!(PRESET_PURIST, PRESET_AGGRESSIVE);
60	    }
61	
62	    #[test]
63	    fn taiwan_preset_has_chain_and_rush() {
64	        assert!(PRESET_TAIWAN.contains(HouseRules::CHAIN_CAPTURE));
65	        assert!(PRESET_TAIWAN.contains(HouseRules::CHARIOT_RUSH));
66	        assert!(!PRESET_TAIWAN.contains(HouseRules::DARK_CAPTURE));
67	    }
68	
69	    #[test]
70	    fn dark_capture_trade_normalizes_to_include_dark_capture() {
71	        let r = normalize(HouseRules::DARK_CAPTURE_TRADE);
72	        assert!(r.contains(HouseRules::DARK_CAPTURE));
73	        assert!(r.contains(HouseRules::DARK_CAPTURE_TRADE));
74	    }
75	}
76
```
</details></tool-use>

68	    /// Banqi 連吃 chain-mode lock. `Some(sq)` means the moving piece at

70	    /// — the turn does NOT auto-advance until the player issues

72	    /// capture which keeps the lock active). `#[serde(default)]` so
73	    /// pre-chain-mode snapshots load.
74	    #[serde(default)]
75	    pub chain_lock: Option<crate::coord::Square>,

80	    /// `#[serde(default)]` for back-compat: deserialized snapshots get

88	        crate::setup::build_initial_state(rules)

92	    /// board. Call after constructing a `GameState` from a struct

94	    /// mutation of `board` / `side_to_move` that bypasses the

97	        self.position_hash = zobrist::compute_position_hash(self);

103	    fn xor_piece(&mut self, piece: Piece, sq: crate::coord::Square) {
104	        self.position_hash ^= zobrist::zobrist_piece(piece.side, piece.kind, sq.0 as usize);
105	    }

113	        let mut out = MoveList::new();
114	        crate::rules::generate_moves(self, &mut out);

116	            out.retain(|m| match m {

118	                | Move::CannonJump { from, .. }
119	                | Move::DarkCapture { from, .. }
120	                | Move::ChainCapture { from, .. } => *from == lock,
121	                _ => false,
122	            });
123	            out.push(Move::EndChain { at: lock });
124	        }
125	        out
126	    }

146	            chain_lock_before,
147	        });

317	    fn apply_inner(&mut self, m: &Move) -> Result<(), CoreError> {
318	        match m {
319	            Move::Reveal { at, revealed } => {
320	                let mut pos = self.board.get(*at).ok_or(CoreError::Illegal("Reveal: empty"))?;
321	                if pos.revealed {
322	                    return Err(CoreError::Illegal("Reveal: already revealed"));
323	                }
324	                pos.revealed = true;
325	                self.board.set(*at, Some(pos));
326	                // First flip in banqi locks side assignment.
327	                if self.side_assignment.is_none() && self.rules.variant == Variant::Banqi {
328	                    if let Some(p) = revealed {
329	                        self.side_assignment =
330	                            Some(banqi_side_assignment(self.side_to_move, p.side));
331	                    }
332	                }
333	                // Reveal does NOT change piece location → no Zobrist delta.
334	                // (See `state::zobrist` module docs: hidden vs revealed
335	                // are deliberately treated identically.)
336	            }
337	            Move::Step { from, to } => {
338	                let p = self.board.get(*from).ok_or(CoreError::Illegal("Step: empty from"))?;
339	                if self.board.get(*to).is_some() {
340	                    return Err(CoreError::Illegal("Step: occupied to"));
341	                }
342	                self.board.set(*from, None);
343	                self.board.set(*to, Some(p));
344	                // Hash: piece left `from`, arrived at `to`.
345	                self.xor_piece(p.piece, *from);
346	                self.xor_piece(p.piece, *to);
347	            }
348	            Move::Capture { from, to, captured } => {
349	                let p = self.board.get(*from).ok_or(CoreError::Illegal("Capture: empty from"))?;
350	                self.board.set(*from, None);
351	                self.board.set(*to, Some(p));
352	                // Hash: attacker moved from→to, defender removed at `to`.
353	                self.xor_piece(p.piece, *from);
354	                self.xor_piece(*captured, *to);
355	                self.xor_piece(p.piece, *to);
356	            }
357	            Move::CannonJump { from, to, screen: _, captured } => {
358	                let p =
359	                    self.board.get(*from).ok_or(CoreError::Illegal("CannonJump: empty from"))?;
360	                self.board.set(*from, None);
361	                self.board.set(*to, Some(p));
362	                self.xor_piece(p.piece, *from);
363	                self.xor_piece(*captured, *to);
364	                self.xor_piece(p.piece, *to);
365	            }
366	            Move::ChainCapture { from, path } => {
367	                let p = self.board.get(*from).ok_or(CoreError::Illegal("Chain: empty from"))?;
368	                if path.is_empty() {
369	                    return Err(CoreError::Illegal("Chain: empty path"));
370	                }
371	                self.board.set(*from, None);
372	                for hop in &path[..path.len() - 1] {
373	                    self.board.set(hop.to, None);
374	                }
375	                let last = *path.last().unwrap();
376	                self.board.set(last.to, Some(p));
377	                // Hash: attacker moved from→last_hop.to; every captured
378	                // piece along the path is removed.
379	                self.xor_piece(p.piece, *from);
380	                for hop in path {
381	                    self.xor_piece(hop.captured, hop.to);
382	                }
383	                self.xor_piece(p.piece, last.to);
384	            }
385	            Move::EndChain { at } => {
386	                // No board change — just verifies that a chain-locked piece
387	                // exists at `at`. The turn-advance machinery in
388	                // `make_move` clears `chain_lock` based on
389	                // `compute_chain_lock_after`.
390	                if self.chain_lock != Some(*at) {
391	                    return Err(CoreError::Illegal("EndChain: no active chain lock"));
392	                }
393	            }
394	            Move::DarkCapture { from, to, revealed, attacker } => {
395	                let revealed_piece =
396	                    revealed.ok_or(CoreError::Illegal("DarkCapture: missing revealed"))?;
397	                let attacker_piece =
398	                    attacker.ok_or(CoreError::Illegal("DarkCapture: missing attacker"))?;
399	                // Reveal the target.
400	                let mut target_pos =
401	                    self.board.get(*to).ok_or(CoreError::Illegal("DarkCapture: empty to"))?;
402	                if target_pos.revealed {
403	                    return Err(CoreError::Illegal("DarkCapture: target already revealed"));
404	                }
405	                target_pos.revealed = true;
406	                self.board.set(*to, Some(target_pos));
407	
408	                // First flip in banqi locks side assignment (mirrors Reveal).
409	                if self.side_assignment.is_none() && self.rules.variant == Variant::Banqi {
410	                    self.side_assignment =
411	                        Some(banqi_side_assignment(self.side_to_move, revealed_piece.side));
412	                }
413	
414	                // Resolve outcome based on rank + DARK_CAPTURE_TRADE flag.
415	                let attacker_pos =
416	                    self.board.get(*from).ok_or(CoreError::Illegal("DarkCapture: empty from"))?;
417	                let outcome = dark_capture_outcome(
418	                    attacker_piece,
419	                    revealed_piece,
420	                    self.rules.house,
421	                    *from,
422	                    *to,
423	                    &self.board,
424	                );
425	                match outcome {
426	                    DarkCaptureOutcome::Capture => {
427	                        self.board.set(*from, None);
428	                        self.board.set(*to, Some(attacker_pos));
429	                        // Hash: attacker from→to, defender removed at to.
430	                        self.xor_piece(attacker_piece, *from);
431	                        self.xor_piece(revealed_piece, *to);
432	                        self.xor_piece(attacker_piece, *to);
433	                    }
434	                    DarkCaptureOutcome::Trade => {
435	                        // Attacker dies; target stays revealed at `to`.
436	                        self.board.set(*from, None);
437	                        // Hash: attacker removed at from, defender removed at to.
438	                        // (Defender was hidden but contributed to hash.)
439	                        self.xor_piece(attacker_piece, *from);

699	                    self.side_to_move

1	//! Turn order: round-robin over 2 or 3 seats.
2	
3	use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
4	use smallvec::SmallVec;
5	
6	use crate::piece::Side;
7	
8	#[derive(Clone, Eq, PartialEq, Debug, Serialize, Deserialize)]
9	pub struct TurnOrder {
10	    pub seats: SmallVec<[Side; 3]>,
11	    /// Index into `seats`.
12	    pub current: u8,
13	}
14	
15	impl TurnOrder {
16	    pub fn two_player() -> Self {
17	        let mut seats = SmallVec::new();
18	        seats.push(Side::RED);
19	        seats.push(Side::BLACK);
20	        Self { seats, current: 0 }
21	    }
22	
23	    pub fn three_player() -> Self {
24	        let mut seats = SmallVec::new();
25	        seats.push(Side(0));
26	        seats.push(Side(1));
27	        seats.push(Side(2));
28	        Self { seats, current: 0 }
29	    }
30	
31	    #[inline]
32	    pub fn current_side(&self) -> Side {
33	        self.seats[self.current as usize]
34	    }
35	
36	    pub fn advance(&mut self) {
37	        self.current = (self.current + 1) % self.seats.len() as u8;
38	    }
39	
40	    /// Advance to the next non-eliminated seat. Used when a faction is wiped
41	    /// out in 三國暗棋. If all opponents are eliminated this is a no-op.
42	    pub fn advance_skipping(&mut self, eliminated: &[Side]) {
43	        for _ in 0..self.seats.len() {
44	            self.advance();
45	            if !eliminated.contains(&self.current_side()) {
46	                return;
47	            }
48	        }
49	    }
50	}
51	
52	#[cfg(test)]
53	mod tests {
54	    use super::*;
55	
56	    #[test]
57	    fn two_player_alternates() {
58	        let mut t = TurnOrder::two_player();
59	        assert_eq!(t.current_side(), Side::RED);
60	        t.advance();
61	        assert_eq!(t.current_side(), Side::BLACK);
62	        t.advance();
63	        assert_eq!(t.current_side(), Side::RED);
64	    }
65	
66	    #[test]
67	    fn three_player_cycles() {
68	        let mut t = TurnOrder::three_player();
69	        let order: Vec<_> = (0..6)
70	            .map(|_| {
71	                let s = t.current_side();
72	                t.advance();
73	                s
74	            })
75	            .collect();
76	        assert_eq!(order, vec![Side(0), Side(1), Side(2), Side(0), Side(1), Side(2)]);
77	    }
78	
79	    #[test]
80	    fn advance_skipping_skips_eliminated() {
81	        let mut t = TurnOrder::three_player();
82	        // Side 0 just moved; eliminated = [Side(1)] means skip to Side(2)
83	        t.advance_skipping(&[Side(1)]);
84	        assert_eq!(t.current_side(), Side(2));
85	        // Continue: skip Side(1) again
86	        t.advance_skipping(&[Side(1)]);
87	        assert_eq!(t.current_side(), Side(0));
88	    }
89	}

1	//! Edge cases around the create / leave / rejoin / GC lifecycle. Locks
2	//! in the current "no host, room is just a name + state" model so future
3	//! refactors don't accidentally leak old room state across GC, drop the
4	//! wrong rematch flag, or forget to notify the opponent on a mid-game
5	//! disconnect.
6	//!
7	//! Pairs with `server_smoke.rs` (basic happy paths) and
8	//! `spectator_chat.rs` (v3 protocol surface). The split keeps each file
9	//! readable; some overlap with existing tests is intentional — these are
10	//! the lifecycle-edge fixtures.
11	
12	use std::time::Duration;
13	
14	use anyhow::Result;
15	use chess_core::piece::Side;
16	use chess_core::rules::RuleSet;
17	use chess_net::{ChatLine, ClientMsg, RoomStatus, RoomSummary, ServerMsg};
18	use tungstenite::Message;
19	
20	fn read_json<S: std::io::Read + std::io::Write>(
21	    ws: &mut tungstenite::WebSocket<S>,
22	) -> Result<ServerMsg> {
23	    let m = ws.read()?;
24	    let text = m.to_text()?;
25	    Ok(serde_json::from_str(text)?)
26	}
27	
28	fn send_json<S: std::io::Read + std::io::Write>(
29	    ws: &mut tungstenite::WebSocket<S>,
30	    msg: &ClientMsg,
31	) -> Result<()> {
32	    ws.send(Message::Text(serde_json::to_string(msg)?))?;
33	    Ok(())
34	}
35	
36	/// Drain `Hello` (or `Spectating`) + `ChatHistory` so subsequent reads
37	/// see live traffic. Returns the chat-history lines for tests that care.
38	fn drain_welcome<S: std::io::Read + std::io::Write>(
39	    ws: &mut tungstenite::WebSocket<S>,
40	) -> Result<Vec<ChatLine>> {
41	    let _welcome = read_json(ws)?;
42	    match read_json(ws)? {
43	        ServerMsg::ChatHistory { lines } => Ok(lines),
44	        other => anyhow::bail!("expected ChatHistory, got {other:?}"),
45	    }
46	}
47	
48	/// Drain `Rooms` pushes from a lobby socket until `pred` matches. Mirrors
49	/// `server_smoke::read_rooms_until` — the lobby push is eventually
50	/// consistent and several intermediate snapshots can land back-to-back
51	/// during fast churn.
52	fn read_rooms_until<S, F>(
53	    ws: &mut tungstenite::WebSocket<S>,
54	    mut pred: F,
55	) -> Result<Vec<RoomSummary>>
56	where
57	    S: std::io::Read + std::io::Write,
58	    F: FnMut(&[RoomSummary]) -> bool,
59	{
60	    for _ in 0..32 {
61	        match read_json(ws)? {
62	            ServerMsg::Rooms { rooms } => {
63	                if pred(&rooms) {
64	                    return Ok(rooms);
65	                }
66	            }
67	            ServerMsg::Error { message } => {
68	                anyhow::bail!("unexpected error on lobby socket: {message}")
69	            }
70	            other => anyhow::bail!("expected Rooms on lobby socket, got {other:?}"),
71	        }
72	    }
73	    anyhow::bail!("predicate never matched within 32 lobby pushes")
74	}
75	
76	/// Read `ServerMsg`s from a player socket until `pred` matches one. The
77	/// server can interleave its own `Update` / `Error` frames with the one
78	/// the test cares about; this helper drains until the predicate fires
79	/// (or we hit a sane budget).
80	fn read_msgs_until<S, F>(ws: &mut tungstenite::WebSocket<S>, mut pred: F) -> Result<ServerMsg>
81	where
82	    S: std::io::Read + std::io::Write,
83	    F: FnMut(&ServerMsg) -> bool,
84	{
85	    for _ in 0..16 {
86	        let msg = read_json(ws)?;
87	        if pred(&msg) {
88	            return Ok(msg);
89	        }
90	    }
91	    anyhow::bail!("predicate never matched within 16 frames")
92	}
93

99	    // where the old room's Arc somehow survived the rooms-map remove.
100	    let listener = tokio::net::TcpListener::bind("127.0.0.1:0").await?;

```rs
//! Banqi default rules let EITHER seat make the first reveal — locked
//! in here so future refactors of the room layer don't accidentally
//! re-impose the "RED-flips-first" guard.
//!
//! Pairs with `crates/chess-core/tests/banqi_first_flip_color.rs` which
//! tests the engine-half of the same contract. This file proves the
//! deployment layer (chess-net's `process_move`) correctly attributes
//! the flip to whichever seat clicked first.

use std::time::Duration;

use anyhow::Result;
use chess_core::moves::Move;
use chess_core::piece::Side;
use chess_core::rules::{HouseRules, RuleSet};
use chess_net::{ClientMsg, ServerMsg};
use tungstenite::Message;

fn read_json<S: std::io::Read + std::io::Write>(
    ws: &mut tungstenite::WebSocket<S>,
) -> Result<ServerMsg> {
    let m = ws.read()?;
    let text = m.to_text()?;
    Ok(serde_json::from_str(text)?)
}

fn drain_welcome<S: std::io::Read + std::io::Write>(
    ws: &mut tungstenite::WebSocket<S>,
) -> Result<ServerMsg> {
    let welcome = read_json(ws)?;
    // ChatHistory follows Hello/Spectating.
    let _ = read_json(ws)?;
    Ok(welcome)
}

#[tokio::test(flavor = "multi_thread")]
async fn black_seat_can_flip_first_under_default_banqi_rules() -> Result<()> {
    let listener = tokio::net::TcpListener::bind("127.0.0.1:0").await?;
    let addr = listener.local_addr()?;
    let server = tokio::spawn(chess_net::serve(
        listener,
        RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(HouseRules::empty(), 42),
    ));
    tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(50)).await;

    let url = format!("ws://{addr}/ws");

    tokio::task::spawn_blocking(move || -> Result<()> {
        let (mut red, _) = tungstenite::connect(&url)?;
        let red_hello = drain_welcome(&mut red)?;
        let red_view = match &red_hello {
            ServerMsg::Hello { observer, view, .. } => {
                assert_eq!(*observer, Side::RED, "first connect must be RED");
                assert!(
                    view.banqi_awaiting_first_flip,
                    "default banqi must surface the pre-first-flip sentinel"
                );
                assert!(
                    !view.legal_moves.is_empty(),
                    "RED observer must see reveal moves pre-first-flip"
                );
                view.clone()
            }
            other => panic!("expected Hello, got {other:?}"),
        };

        let (mut black, _) = tungstenite::connect(&url)?;
        let black_hello = drain_welcome(&mut black)?;
        let black_view = match &black_hello {
            ServerMsg::Hello { observer, view, .. } => {
                assert_eq!(*observer, Side::BLACK, "second connect must be BLACK");
                assert!(
                    view.banqi_awaiting_first_flip,
                    "BLACK observer must also see the pre-first-flip sentinel"
                );
                assert!(
                    !view.legal_moves.is_empty(),
                    "BLACK observer must ALSO see reveal moves (the relaxed gate)"
                );
                view.clone()
            }
            other => panic!("expected Hello, got {other:?}"),
        };
        // Both views expose the same reveal-move count — the projection
        // doesn't gate on observer-vs-side-to-move in this state.
        assert_eq!(red_view.legal_moves.len(), black_view.legal_moves.len());

        // BLACK sends the first reveal. The server attributes the flip
        // to BLACK seat via `state.set_active_seat`.
        let first_reveal = black_view.legal_moves[0].clone();
        assert!(matches!(first_reveal, Move::Reveal { .. }));
        black.send(Message::Text(serde_json::to_string(&ClientMsg::Move {
            mv: first_reveal,
        })?))?;

        // Both seats receive an Update broadcast; pre-flip sentinel is now
        // cleared on the projected views.
        let red_update = read_json(&mut red)?;
        let black_update = read_json(&mut black)?;
        match red_update {
            ServerMsg::Update { view } => {
                assert!(!view.banqi_awaiting_first_flip, "sentinel must clear after first flip");
                assert!(
                    view.side_assignment_locked(),
                    "side assignment must lock after first flip"
                );
            }
            other => panic!("expected Update for RED, got {other:?}"),
        }
        match black_update {
            ServerMsg::Update { view } => {
                assert!(!view.banqi_awaiting_first_flip);
            }
            other => panic!("expected Update for BLACK, got {other:?}"),
        }
        Ok(())
    })
    .await??;

    server.abort();
    Ok(())
}

#[tokio::test(flavor = "multi_thread")]
async fn preassign_colors_rejects_blacks_first_reveal_attempt() -> Result<()> {
    // Legacy mode: HouseRules::PREASSIGN_COLORS keeps RED as the forced
    // first mover. BLACK attempting the first reveal must get the
    // standard "not your turn" error.
    let listener = tokio::net::TcpListener::bind("127.0.0.1:0").await?;
    let addr = listener.local_addr()?;
    let server = tokio::spawn(chess_net::serve(
        listener,
        RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(HouseRules::PREASSIGN_COLORS, 42),
    ));
    tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(50)).await;

    let url = format!("ws://{addr}/ws");

    tokio::task::spawn_blocking(move || -> Result<()> {
        let (mut red, _) = tungstenite::connect(&url)?;
        let red_hello = drain_welcome(&mut red)?;
        let red_view = match &red_hello {
            ServerMsg::Hello { observer, view, .. } => {
                assert_eq!(*observer, Side::RED);
                assert!(
                    !view.banqi_awaiting_first_flip,
                    "PREASSIGN_COLORS suppresses the sentinel"
                );
                view.clone()
            }
            other => panic!("expected Hello, got {other:?}"),
        };

        let (mut black, _) = tungstenite::connect(&url)?;
        let black_hello = drain_welcome(&mut black)?;
        match &black_hello {
            ServerMsg::Hello { observer, view, .. } => {
                assert_eq!(*observer, Side::BLACK);
                assert!(
                    view.legal_moves.is_empty(),
                    "BLACK observer must not see legal moves under PREASSIGN_COLORS"
                );
            }
            other => panic!("expected Hello, got {other:?}"),
        }

        // BLACK tries to flip — must be rejected.
        let any_reveal = red_view.legal_moves[0].clone();
        black.send(Message::Text(serde_json::to_string(&ClientMsg::Move {
            mv: any_reveal,
        })?))?;
        match read_json(&mut black)? {
            ServerMsg::Error { message } => {
                assert!(message.contains("not your turn"), "got: {message}");
            }
            other => panic!("expected Error, got {other:?}"),
        }
        Ok(())
    })
    .await??;

    server.abort();
    Ok(())
}

/// Shim for the assertion above: a Hello-time view doesn't carry the
/// `side_assignment` field directly, so we approximate "the assignment
/// locked" by `current_color` matching a side AND the view no longer
/// claiming the awaiting sentinel. Lives in the test file rather than
/// the view crate to avoid leaking test helpers.
trait SideAssignmentLockedExt {
    fn side_assignment_locked(&self) -> bool;
}

impl SideAssignmentLockedExt for chess_core::view::PlayerView {
    fn side_assignment_locked(&self) -> bool {
        !self.banqi_awaiting_first_flip
    }
}

```

235	    // Subtitle clarifies whose POV the analysis is from. For hint
236	    // content this matches side-to-move (hint pump only runs on
237	    // human's turn). For debug content it's the AI's last move POV —
238	    // which equals the *previous* side-to-move; shown as "Last AI:
239	    // Red 紅" to be unambiguous.
240	    let panel_subtitle = Signal::derive(move || {
241	        if hints_enabled && hint_view_active.get() {
242	            let stm = state.with(|s| s.side_to_move);
243	            match stm {
244	                Side::RED => "Red 紅 to move".to_string(),
245	                Side::BLACK => "Black 黑 to move".to_string(),
246	                _ => "Green 綠 to move".to_string(),
247	            }
248	        } else if let Some(cfg) = ai {
249	            // Debug view shows the AI's own POV.
250	            match cfg.ai_side {
251	                Side::RED => "AI POV: Red 紅".to_string(),
252	                Side::BLACK => "AI POV: Black 黑".to_string(),
253	                _ => "AI POV: Green 綠".to_string(),
254	            }
255	        } else {
256	            String::new()
257	        }
258	    });
259	    // The signal the panel actually reads. Mirrors `panel_title` /
260	    // `panel_subtitle` logic so all three stay in sync.
261	    let analysis_signal: Signal<Option<AiAnalysis>> = Signal::derive(move || {
262	        if hints_enabled && hint_view_active.get() {
263	            hint_analysis.get()
264	        } else {
265	            debug_analysis.get()
266	        }
267	    });
268	
269	    // Render from the human player's POV when in vs-AI mode so the player's
270	    // pieces sit on the bottom; in pass-and-play the board is fixed Red-side.
271	    let observer = match ai {
272	        Some(cfg) => cfg.ai_side.opposite(),
273	        None => Side::RED,
274	    };
275	    let shape = state.with_untracked(|s| s.board.shape());
276	
277	    let view = create_memo(move |_| state.with(|s| PlayerView::project(s, s.side_to_move)));
278	
279	    // Clear any held selection when the seat-to-move flips. Catches the
280	    // chain-mode tail where the last hop ends the chain and switches
281	    // sides — the previous player's `selected` is meaningless for the
282	    // next one. Also a safety net for any future click path that
283	    // forgets its own `selected.set(None)`.
284	    let side_signal = Signal::derive(move || view.with(|v| v.side_to_move));
```

6	## Board
7	
8	- 4 files × 8 ranks (half a xiangqi board, oriented sideways)
9	- All 32 standard xiangqi pieces are placed face-down at random in the 32 cells
10	
11	## Setup
12	
13	1. Shuffle all 32 pieces (16 red + 16 black) face-down across the 4×8 grid.
14	2. The starting player flips any piece on their turn — the **color** they reveal determines their faction for the rest of the game (`SideAssignment` is locked here).
15	3. From this point onward, players control their assigned color.
16	
17	The `chess-core` setup uses a seedable `ChaCha8Rng` so games are reproducible from a `RuleSet::banqi_seed`.
18	
19	## Movement
20	
21	- Face-down pieces cannot move (only flipped). On your turn, `Move::Reveal { at }` flips any face-down tile.
22	- Face-up pieces move **one orthogonal step** to an empty square or to capture an enemy. Diagonal moves are NOT part of base banqi (the `HORSE_DIAGONAL` house rule adds diagonal *captures* only — see [`banqi-house.md`](banqi-house.md)).
23	- **Cannon**: captures by jumping over **exactly one piece** (the screen) to land on an enemy. The screen may itself be face-down. A cannon's non-capturing move is also one orthogonal step.
24	
25	## Capture (rank-based)
26	
27	| Rank | Piece |
28	|---|---|
29	| 6 | General (將) |
30	| 5 | Advisor (士) |
31	| 4 | Elephant (象) |
32	| 3 | Chariot (車) |
33	| 2 | Horse (馬) |
34	| 1 | Cannon (炮) |
35	| 0 | Soldier (卒) |
36	
37	A piece may capture an enemy piece of equal or lower rank, **with two exceptions**:
38	
39	- **Soldier beats General** (卒剋將). A soldier captures the general; the general cannot capture the soldier.
40	- **Cannon captures by jumping**, ignoring rank entirely (炮 can take any enemy via a one-piece screen).
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
1	# Banqi House Rules (家規)
2	
3	> **Status**: skeleton — formal spec to be filled in alongside implementation.
4	
5	Six independent toggles. Combine freely. Three named presets bundle common combos.
6	
7	## Toggles
8	
9	### `CHAIN_CAPTURE` — 連吃
10	
11	After a capture, if the capturing piece can immediately capture another enemy piece **in any direction the piece is allowed to attack** (orthogonal for most; cannon-jump for cannons; +diagonal for horses with `HORSE_DIAGONAL`), it **must** continue OR explicitly end the chain — the engine refuses to advance the turn while a chain is live.
12	
13	Engine state machine (`GameState.chain_lock`):
14	
15	1. After a chain-eligible capture lands the attacker on a new square `sq`, the engine sets `chain_lock = Some(sq)` and does NOT advance the turn.
16	2. While locked, `legal_moves` is filtered to captures originating at `sq`, plus a single `Move::EndChain { at: sq }` terminator. The terminator is the explicit "I'm done — pass the turn" move.
17	3. The chain naturally ends when the attacker has no further captures from its current square (`chain_lock` clears automatically and the turn advances).
18	
19	Player-facing UX: each hop is a separate click / `Move::Capture` (or `Move::DarkCapture`) — single-step, NOT an atomic multi-hop move. To end early: click the locked piece (TUI/web) or press `Esc` (TUI). Engine still ships an atomic `Move::ChainCapture { from, path }` variant in `Move`'s enum for back-compat with older snapshots and for the same-direction extension generator, but that path isn't user-facing in the current clients.
20	
21	Chains-with-dark-hops (atomic chain captures that pass through hidden tiles) is a Phase 2 follow-up — see `TODO.md`.
22	
23	### `DARK_CAPTURE` — 暗吃
24	
25	Atomically reveal a face-down piece and resolve a capture in one move. The player commits to the action without prior knowledge of the target's identity. Three outcomes (resolved at apply-time from the revealed piece's rank vs the attacker's):
26	
27	- **Capture** — attacker outranks (or is one of the rank-bypass cases below): attacker takes the target's square, defender removed.
28	- **Probe (default)** — attacker is outranked: target stays revealed in place, attacker stays put, turn ends. Information cost only.
29	- **Trade** — attacker is outranked AND `DARK_CAPTURE_TRADE` is set: attacker is removed instead of staying put.
30	
31	**Rank bypasses** (the dark-capture *always* resolves to Capture regardless of the revealed piece's rank):
32	
33	- **Cannon attacker via jump-over-screen.** Standard banqi cannons capture any piece via jump; the dark-capture path mirrors that. (Cannons do NOT emit a 1-step adjacent dark-capture — only the jump variant.)
34	- **Horse attacker on a diagonal hit under `HORSE_DIAGONAL`.** 馬斜's "any piece" diagonal capture extends to the dark-capture path. Orthogonal horse dark-capture still obeys rank.
35	
36	Emission paths in the move generator:
37	- 1-step orthogonal onto a hidden tile (any non-Cannon piece).
38	- Chariot rush (`CHARIOT_RUSH`) onto a hidden blocker past a gap.
39	- Cannon jump-over-screen onto a hidden tile.
40	- Horse diagonal onto a hidden tile (`HORSE_DIAGONAL`).
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
52	### `HORSE_DIAGONAL` — 馬斜
53	
54	Adds **diagonal one-step *captures only*** to the horse — the horse may NOT slide diagonally onto an empty square. Orthogonal moves (steps + captures) are unchanged. Diagonal captures **ignore rank** (any piece, including General); diagonal dark-captures (under `DARK_CAPTURE`) likewise resolve to Capture regardless of the revealed piece's rank. Orthogonal captures still follow the standard rank rules — the bypass is diagonal-only.
55	
56	### `CANNON_FAST_MOVE` — 炮快移
57	
58	Cannon may move any number of empty squares in a line for non-capturing moves (capture still requires the one-piece jump). Speeds up cannon repositioning without changing capture geometry. *(Currently accepted but not wired into move-gen — see TODO.md.)*
59	
60	## Presets
61	
62	```rust
63	HouseRules::PRESET_PURIST     // empty: classic banqi
64	HouseRules::PRESET_TAIWAN     // CHAIN_CAPTURE | CHARIOT_RUSH
65	HouseRules::PRESET_AGGRESSIVE // CHAIN_CAPTURE | DARK_CAPTURE | CHARIOT_RUSH | HORSE_DIAGONAL
66	```
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
1	//! Wire protocol between `chess-net-server` and clients.
2	//!
3	//! Frames are JSON text frames, one message per frame. Tagged enums via
4	//! `#[serde(tag = "type")]` keep the schema obvious in `wscat`/`jq`.
5	//!
6	//! All payload types come straight from `chess-core` and already derive
7	//! `Serialize/Deserialize`, including the proptest-validated no-leak
8	//! projection in `PlayerView` (hidden banqi pieces stay opaque on the wire).
9	
10	use chess_core::moves::Move;
11	use chess_core::piece::Side;
12	use chess_core::rules::{RuleSet, Variant};
13	use chess_core::view::PlayerView;
14	use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
15	
16	/// Bumped whenever the wire schema changes incompatibly.
17	///
18	/// v1 → v2: added `ServerMsg::Rooms` and `ClientMsg::ListRooms` for the
19	/// multi-room lobby. Old (v1) clients still work against a v2 server in
20	/// the default room "main" — the new message variants are additive.
21	///
22	/// v2 → v3: added `ServerMsg::{Spectating, ChatHistory, Chat}` and
23	/// `ClientMsg::Chat` for read-only spectators + in-room chat. v2 clients
24	/// keep working as players (they never request spectator role; the new
25	/// chat variants are additive). `RoomSummary` gains a `spectators` field
26	/// with `#[serde(default)]` so v2 lobby clients deserializing a v3 server
27	/// snapshot ignore the new column.
28	///
29	/// v3 → v4: added `PlayerView.in_check: bool` so clients can render a
30	/// "將軍 / CHECK" warning without having to recompute it from the board.
31	/// Wire-compatible — `#[serde(default)]` on the new field means a v3
32	/// client deserializing a v4 message reads `in_check = false` and a v4
33	/// client deserializing a v3 message gets the same default. The bump is
34	/// for documentation, not enforcement; the handshake still uses lenient
35	/// equality. See ADR-0007.
36	///
37	/// v4 → v5: added `hints_allowed: bool` to `RoomSummary`, `Hello`, and
38	/// `Spectating` so clients can decide whether to mount the AI debug /
39	/// hint panel for a given room. Set at room creation time from the
40	/// first joiner's `?hints=1` query parameter, frozen for the room's
41	/// lifetime (same pattern as `password`). Closes the previous
42	/// client-only-`?debug=1` cheat hole — net-mode clients now refuse to
43	/// mount the panel unless the server says hints are allowed in this
44	/// room. Wire-compatible: `#[serde(default)]` on every new field means
45	/// v4 clients deserializing v5 messages see `hints_allowed = false`,
46	/// and v5 clients deserializing v4 messages also see `false`. Local
47	/// (offline / GitHub Pages) `?debug=1` and `?hints=1` are unaffected
48	/// — they bypass the server entirely.
49	pub const PROTOCOL_VERSION: u16 = 5;
50	
51	/// Server → client.
52	#[derive(Clone, Debug, Serialize, Deserialize)]
53	#[serde(tag = "type")]
54	pub enum ServerMsg {
55	    /// Sent once per client right after side assignment. Carries the variant
56	    /// (so the client knows what to render) plus the initial `PlayerView`.
57	    /// `hints_allowed` (v5+) reflects whether the room was created with
58	    /// `?hints=1`; clients consult this to decide whether to mount the
59	    /// AI debug / hint panel.
60	    Hello {
61	        protocol: u16,
62	        observer: Side,
63	        rules: RuleSet,
64	        view: PlayerView,
65	        #[serde(default)]
66	        hints_allowed: bool,
67	    },
68	    /// Sent to every seated client after each committed move.
69	    Update { view: PlayerView },
70	    /// Illegal move, room full, malformed message, opponent disconnect, etc.
71	    /// Non-fatal by default — clients should display and keep the connection.
72	    Error { message: String },
73	    /// Pushed on the lobby socket only — initial snapshot on connect, then
74	    /// again whenever any room's state changes (seat insertion / removal /
75	    /// game finish / GC). Game sockets never receive this variant.
76	    Rooms { rooms: Vec<RoomSummary> },
77	    /// Spectator-side counterpart to `Hello` — no `observer` because
78	    /// spectators are not seated. Carries the same `rules` + initial `view`
79	    /// (projected from `Side::RED`'s perspective so banqi hidden tiles stay
80	    /// hidden). Sent immediately after a `?role=spectator` connection
81	    /// upgrades successfully. `hints_allowed` (v5+) — see [`Hello`].
82	    Spectating {
83	        protocol: u16,
84	        rules: RuleSet,
85	        view: PlayerView,
86	        #[serde(default)]
87	        hints_allowed: bool,
88	    },
89	    /// Pushed once right after `Hello` / `Spectating` with the room's chat
90	    /// ring buffer (≤50 lines). Empty `lines` for a fresh room.
91	    ChatHistory { lines: Vec<ChatLine> },
92	    /// Pushed live to every recipient (seats + spectators) on each new chat
93	    /// line. `from` is currently always a player's `Side`; system messages
94	    /// (player joined / left) are deferred — see
95	    /// `backlog/chess-net-system-messages.md`.
96	    Chat { line: ChatLine },
97	}
98	
99	/// Client → server.
100	#[derive(Clone, Debug, Serialize, Deserialize)]
101	#[serde(tag = "type")]
102	pub enum ClientMsg {
103	    /// Submit a move. For `Move::Reveal`, send `revealed: None` — the server
104	    /// fills in the piece identity locally; the wire form stays `None`.
105	    Move { mv: Move },
106	    /// Resign — ends the game with `WinReason::Resignation` for the opponent.
107	    Resign,
108	    /// Request a rematch. Only valid after the current game has ended
109	    /// (Won / Drawn). Server resets the game when both sides have requested
110	    /// and re-sends `Hello` to each. Same observer assignment, same rules,
111	    /// fresh `GameState`.
112	    Rematch,
113	    /// Lobby-only: forces a fresh `Rooms` snapshot to the requester. The
114	    /// server pushes `Rooms` automatically on state changes; this is the
115	    /// manual-refresh button (`r` in the TUI lobby).
116	    ListRooms,
117	    /// Send a chat line. Server enforces players-only (spectators get
118	    /// `Error{"spectators cannot chat"}`), trims to ≤256 chars after stripping
119	    /// control chars, stamps `ts_ms` server-side, and broadcasts the result
120	    /// to every seat + spectator as `ServerMsg::Chat`.
121	    Chat { text: String },
122	}
123	
124	/// One chat line as it appears on the wire and in the per-room ring buffer.
125	/// `ts_ms` is unix milliseconds set server-side so clients don't have to
126	/// agree on a clock.
127	#[derive(Clone, Debug, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq, Eq)]
128	pub struct ChatLine {
129	    pub from: Side,
130	    pub text: String,
```
</details></tool-use>

134	/// One row in the lobby browser. Mirrors what's interesting to a player
135	/// deciding whether to join: variant for setting expectations, seat count
136	/// for "is this room joinable?", `has_password` for the lock icon, and
137	/// `status` so finished rooms stand out from active games.
138	#[derive(Clone, Debug, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq, Eq)]
139	pub struct RoomSummary {
140	    pub id: String,
141	    pub variant: String,
142	    pub seats: u8,
143	    /// Watching-only clients connected via `?role=spectator`. v2 lobby
144	    /// snapshots omit this field entirely; the `serde(default)` keeps v2
145	    /// clients deserializing a v3 `RoomSummary` cleanly (they just see 0).
146	    #[serde(default)]
147	    pub spectators: u16,
148	    pub has_password: bool,
149	    pub status: RoomStatus,
150	    /// Whether this room was created with `?hints=1` — i.e., AI hints
151	    /// (the in-game debug panel + PV overlay) are sanctioned by the
152	    /// room and visible to BOTH players + spectators. Frozen for the
153	    /// room's lifetime. v4 lobby snapshots omit this field; the
154	    /// `serde(default)` keeps v4 clients reading v5 summaries cleanly
155	    /// (treating the column as `false`).
156	    #[serde(default)]
157	    pub hints_allowed: bool,
158	}
159	
160	#[derive(Copy, Clone, Debug, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq, Eq)]
161	#[serde(rename_all = "lowercase")]
162	pub enum RoomStatus {
163	    /// Game has not begun (≤1 seat occupied or ongoing with both seats).
164	    Lobby,
165	    /// Both seats occupied and the game is `Ongoing`.
166	    Playing,
167	    /// `GameStatus::Won` or `GameStatus::Drawn`. The room still exists but
168	    /// requires a rematch (or for both clients to disconnect → GC) to be
169	    /// reusable.
170	    Finished,
171	}
172	
173	/// Stable variant string for `RoomSummary.variant`. Uses kebab-case so the
174	/// JSON looks the same as the chess-tui `--style ascii` / `--style cjk`
175	/// arguments and the `--house` token list.
176	pub fn variant_label(rules: &RuleSet) -> &'static str {
177	    match rules.variant {
178	        Variant::Xiangqi => {
179	            if rules.xiangqi_allow_self_check {
180	                "xiangqi"
181	            } else {
182	                "xiangqi-strict"
183	            }
184	        }
185	        Variant::Banqi => "banqi",
186	        Variant::ThreeKingdomBanqi => "three-kingdom",
187	    }
188	}

1	[package]
2	name        = "chess-net"
3	description = "Wire protocol + axum websocket server for chess-core. Multi-room. Default features include the server; `default-features = false` exposes the protocol types only (e.g. for WASM clients)."
4	version.workspace      = true
5	edition.workspace      = true
6	rust-version.workspace = true
7	license.workspace      = true
8	repository.workspace   = true
9	authors.workspace      = true
10	
11	[lib]
12	name = "chess_net"
13	path = "src/lib.rs"
14	
15	[[bin]]
16	name = "chess-net-server"
17	path = "src/bin/server.rs"
18	required-features = ["server"]
19	
20	[features]
21	default = ["server", "static-serve"]
22	# Enables axum/tokio/clap and the `server` module. Off by default for
23	# wasm32 consumers (chess-web) that only need the protocol types.
24	server = ["dep:tokio", "dep:axum", "dep:futures-util", "dep:clap", "dep:anyhow"]
25	# Enables `--static-dir` static-file serving (tower-http ServeDir). When the
26	# binary is started with `--static-dir <path>`, chess-net serves that
27	# directory as the route fallback. Useful for serving the chess-web `dist/`
28	# build alongside the WS endpoints in production.
29	static-serve = ["server", "dep:tower", "dep:tower-http"]
30	
31	[dependencies]
32	chess-core   = { path = "../chess-core" }
33	serde        = { workspace = true }
34	serde_json   = { workspace = true }
35	anyhow       = { workspace = true, optional = true }
36	tokio        = { workspace = true, optional = true }

354	async fn handle_room_socket(

359	    role: JoinRole,

365	    // === Pre-flight validation ===

367	        let _ = send_close_with(

133	// SECTION: HostRoom::new + Session bridge
