```text
135	impl HostRoom {
136	    /// Construct a fresh host room and seat the host as the first
137	    /// player. Returns:
138	    ///
139	    ///   * `Rc<HostRoom>` — handle the host page holds for adding
140	    ///     remote peers as they pair (`add_remote_player`,
141	    ///     `add_remote_spectator`).
142	    ///   * `Session` — drop into the play page exactly where
143	    ///     `transport::ws::connect`'s return value would go. The
144	    ///     session's `incoming` signal already has the initial
145	    ///     `Hello` + `ChatHistory` queued (the host always seats as
146	    ///     RED, so the page sees `observer: Side::RED` immediately).
147	    ///
148	    /// Panics only on the impossible case where `Room::join_player`
149	    /// fails for the very first joiner (the room is fresh — both
150	    /// seats are open, no password mismatch is possible because the
151	    /// password was set on the same line).
152	    pub fn new(
153	        rules: RuleSet,
154	        password: Option<String>,
155	        hints_allowed: bool,
156	    ) -> (Rc<Self>, Session) {
157	        let mut room = Room::new(rules, password, hints_allowed);
158	        let self_peer = PeerId(1);
159	        let incoming = Incoming::new();
160	        let (state, _set_state) = create_signal(ConnState::Open);
161	
162	        // Insert host's sink BEFORE join_player so the Hello +
163	        // ChatHistory the room emits queue into the host's own
164	        // incoming Incoming — same VecDeque, processed in arrival
165	        // order by the play page's draining effect (which reads
166	        // every pending message via Incoming::drain on each tick).
167	        let mut sinks: HashMap<PeerId, PeerSink> = HashMap::new();
168	        sinks.insert(self_peer, PeerSink::Local(incoming.clone()));
169	
170	        let (_side, outbound) =
171	            room.join_player(self_peer).expect("fresh room always seats the first joiner");
172	        for ob in outbound {
173	            if let Some(sink) = sinks.get(&ob.peer) {
174	                sink.deliver(ob.msg);
175	            }
176	        }
177	
178	        let host = Rc::new(Self {
179	            room: RefCell::new(room),
180	            sinks: RefCell::new(sinks),
181	            next_peer: Cell::new(2),
182	            self_peer,
183	            room_id: "lan".into(),
184	        });
185	
186	        let handle: Rc<dyn Transport> =
187	            Rc::new(HostSelfTransport { host: host.clone(), self_peer });
188	        let session = Session { handle, incoming, state };
189	        (host, session)
190	    }
191
```

```text
425	/// copy-paste through clipboard / AirDrop / IM apps that helpfully
426	/// "normalise" line endings (SDP per RFC 4566 demands CRLF; many
427	/// transports strip the CR).
428	///
429	/// Pretty-printed with 2-space indent so the contents eyeball the same
430	/// as raw SDP — useful while debugging.
431	pub fn encode_sdp(kind: &str, sdp: &str) -> String {
432	    format!("{{\n  \"type\": \"{}\",\n  \"sdp\": \"{}\"\n}}", kind, escape_json_string(sdp))
433	}
434	
435	fn escape_json_string(s: &str) -> String {
436	    let mut out = String::with_capacity(s.len() + 8);
437	    for ch in s.chars() {
438	        match ch {
439	            '"' => out.push_str("\\\""),
440	            '\\' => out.push_str("\\\\"),
441	            '\n' => out.push_str("\\n"),
442	            '\r' => out.push_str("\\r"),
443	            '\t' => out.push_str("\\t"),
444	            c if (c as u32) < 0x20 => {
445	                out.push_str(&format!("\\u{:04x}", c as u32));
446	            }
447	            c => out.push(c),
448	        }
449	    }
450	    out
451	}
452	
453	fn decode_sdp_envelope(blob: &str) -> Result<(String, String), JsValue> {
454	    let trimmed = blob.trim();
455	    if !trimmed.starts_with('{') {
456	        return Err(JsValue::from_str(
457	            "SDP must be the JSON envelope produced by the host page (raw-SDP is not accepted)",
458	        ));
459	    }
460	    let parsed = js_sys::JSON::parse(trimmed)
461	        .map_err(|e| JsValue::from_str(&format!("SDP envelope is not valid JSON: {e:?}")))?;
462	    let kind =
463	        Reflect::get(&parsed, &"type".into()).ok().and_then(|v| v.as_string()).unwrap_or_default();
464	    let sdp =
465	        Reflect::get(&parsed, &"sdp".into()).ok().and_then(|v| v.as_string()).unwrap_or_default();
466	    if kind.is_empty() || sdp.is_empty() {
467	        return Err(JsValue::from_str("SDP envelope JSON missing `type` or `sdp` field"));
468	    }
469	    // Browsers accept LF-only SDP in practice but normalise anyway —
470	    // copy-paste through AirDrop / SMS / IM may strip CR.
471	    let sdp = sdp.replace('\r', "").replace('\n', "\r\n");
472	    Ok((kind, sdp))
473	}
474	
475	fn decode_offer(blob: &OfferBlob) -> Result<String, JsValue> {
476	    let (kind, sdp) = decode_sdp_envelope(&blob.0)?;
477	    if kind != "offer" {
478	        return Err(JsValue::from_str(&format!("expected offer envelope, got `{kind}`")));
479	    }
480	    Ok(sdp)
481	}
482	
483	fn decode_answer(blob: &AnswerBlob) -> Result<String, JsValue> {
484	    let (kind, sdp) = decode_sdp_envelope(&blob.0)?;
485	    if kind != "answer" {
486	        return Err(JsValue::from_str(&format!("expected answer envelope, got `{kind}`")));
487	    }
488	    Ok(sdp)
489	}
490	// SECTION: ICE config + ICE-gather wait helper
491	
492	fn new_peer_connection(mode: IceMode) -> Result<RtcPeerConnection, JsValue> {
493	    let cfg = RtcConfiguration::new();
494	    let servers: Array = Array::new();
495	    if mode == IceMode::WithStun {
496	        // Multi-server STUN list, ordered for CN reachability:
497	        //   - stun.miwifi.com — Xiaomi's own STUN; reachable from CN.
498	        //   - stun.qq.com     — Tencent's; reachable from CN.
499	        //   - stun.cloudflare.com — Cloudflare; usually reachable.
```

```text
1	//! Transport-agnostic chess-net pump for the chess-web client side.
2	//!
3	//! Today's only implementation is [`ws::WsTransport`] (gloo-net WebSocket
4	//! against a chess-net server). Phase 3 of `backlog/webrtc-lan-pairing.md`
5	//! will add a `webrtc::WebRtcTransport` (RtcDataChannel as joiner) so the
6	//! same play / lobby pages can talk to either a hosted chess-net or a
7	//! peer's PWA over LAN — gated only on which factory function the route
8	//! decides to call.
9	//!
10	//! Object-safety: [`Transport`] only takes `&self` and returns `bool`;
11	//! no associated types, no generics. Consumers store
12	//! `Rc<dyn Transport>` and clone it freely into Leptos closures.
13	
14	// === module skeleton — section bodies filled in via edits below ===
15	
16	// SECTION: imports
17	use std::cell::RefCell;
18	use std::collections::VecDeque;
19	use std::rc::Rc;
20	
21	use chess_net::protocol::{ClientMsg, ServerMsg};
22	use leptos::{create_signal, ReadSignal, SignalGet, SignalUpdate, WriteSignal};
23	// SECTION: ConnState enum
24	
25	/// Coarse state of a chess-net transport connection. Pages render different
26	/// banners + disable inputs based on this.
27	///
28	/// Order is significant: implementations should only ever step
29	/// `Connecting` → `Open` → (`Closed` | `Error`); we never recover from
30	/// `Closed`/`Error` in the same `Session` (auto-reconnect is a separate
31	/// follow-up — see `backlog/web-ws-reconnect.md`).
32	#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq)]
33	pub enum ConnState {
34	    Connecting,
35	    Open,
36	    Closed,
37	    Error,
38	}
39	// SECTION: Transport trait
```

```text
125	pub fn LanHostPage() -> impl IntoView {
126	    // ── Page state ────────────────────────────────────────────
127	    // We progress through:
128	    //   Idle → Generating → AwaitingAnswer → AcceptingAnswer →
129	    //   WaitingForChannel → Playing
130	    // Each transition mutates the corresponding signals; the view
131	    // renders accordingly.
132	    let (status, set_status) = create_signal::<HostStatus>(HostStatus::Idle);
133	    let (use_stun, set_use_stun) = create_signal::<bool>(false);
134	    let (offer_blob, set_offer_blob) = create_signal::<String>(String::new());
135	    let (answer_input, set_answer_input) = create_signal::<String>(String::new());
136	    let (error_msg, set_error_msg) = create_signal::<Option<String>>(None);
137	
138	    // ── Variant + rules form state ─────────────────────────────
139	    // Mirrors picker.rs::BanqiCard: one bool signal per HouseRules
140	    // bitflag (rather than one signal holding the whole bitset) so
141	    // each checkbox can bind directly to `prop:checked` without a
142	    // closure dance. `apply_preset` flips all five at once.
143	    //
144	    // Initial values come from the URL query string (`?variant=banqi
145	    // &house=chain,rush&seed=42`) via the same `parse_local_rules`
146	    // parser the picker uses. Lets a future picker "Host on LAN"
147	    // button deep-link a fully-configured /lan/host without a fresh
148	    // parser; today, hand-typed URLs are the only consumer.
149	    let query = use_query_map();
150	    let initial_variant = query.with_untracked(|q| {
151	        q.get("variant").and_then(|s| parse_variant_slug(s)).unwrap_or(Variant::Xiangqi)
152	    });
153	    // Three-kingdom is 3-player; not a valid LAN choice — clamp to xiangqi.
154	    let initial_variant = match initial_variant {
155	        Variant::ThreeKingdomBanqi => Variant::Xiangqi,
156	        v => v,
157	    };
158	    let initial_params: LocalRulesParams =
159	        query.with_untracked(|q| parse_local_rules(|k| q.get(k).cloned()));
160	
161	    let variant = create_rw_signal(initial_variant);
162	    let strict = create_rw_signal(initial_params.strict);
163	    let chain = create_rw_signal(initial_params.house.contains(HouseRules::CHAIN_CAPTURE));
164	    let dark = create_rw_signal(initial_params.house.contains(HouseRules::DARK_CAPTURE));
165	    let dark_trade =
166	        create_rw_signal(initial_params.house.contains(HouseRules::DARK_CAPTURE_TRADE));
167	    let rush = create_rw_signal(initial_params.house.contains(HouseRules::CHARIOT_RUSH));
168	    let horse = create_rw_signal(initial_params.house.contains(HouseRules::HORSE_DIAGONAL));
169	    let seed_text =
170	        create_rw_signal(initial_params.seed.map(|s| s.to_string()).unwrap_or_default());
171	
172	    // Snapshot of the RuleSet that was passed to HostRoom::new — set
173	    // once on successful `on_accept`. Drives the post-Idle "Playing:
174	    // …" status line that replaces the form once pairing starts.
175	    let (chosen_rules, set_chosen_rules) = create_signal::<Option<RuleSet>>(None);
176	
177	    let apply_preset = move |preset: HouseRules| {
178	        chain.set(preset.contains(HouseRules::CHAIN_CAPTURE));
```

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-14 11:26:30Z)**_

All 7 phases complete. Final summary:

## Shipped

**Engine** (`chess-core`)
- `HouseRules::PREASSIGN_COLORS` bit (legacy "host=Red flips first")
- `GameState::banqi_awaiting_first_flip()` + `set_active_seat()` + `banqi_first_mover_locked: bool`
- `PlayerView::banqi_awaiting_first_flip` (serde-default; no protocol bump)
- Relaxed `legal_moves` projection so both observers see reveals pre-first-flip
- 8 new unit tests in `banqi_first_flip_color.rs`

**chess-net**
- New protocol types: `RoomConfig`, `HostColor` (Red/Black/Random), `FirstFlipper` (Either/Host/Joiner)
- `Room::with_config()` honours host-side preferences; `next_seat()` returns the configured host colour
- `process_move` relaxes the "not your turn" guard for the first banqi reveal in Either mode
- URL params: `?host_color=...`, `?first_flipper=...`, `?preassign=1` (frozen on room create)
- 5 new integration tests in `banqi_first_flip.rs`

**chess-web `/lan/host`**
- "You play as: Red/Black/Random" radio
- Banqi: "Pre-assign colours" checkbox + "First flipper" radio (Either/Host/Joiner)
- Offer envelope embeds the chosen config; `/lan/join` shows the host's choices in a yellow summary
- `HostRoom::with_config()` and `encode_sdp_with_config()` carry preferences end-to-end
- `routes.rs` accepts `preassign` URL token

**chess-web `/play`**
- Sidebar shows "Awaiting first flip" pre-first-flip (banqi only)
- Click-guard relaxed so either seat can submit the first reveal in Either mode
- New `.turn-awaiting` CSS class

**chess-tui**
- Sidebar shows "Awaiting first flip — either side may flip" / "未翻牌 — 任一方皆可先翻" (Local + Net)
- CLI flags: `--host-color`, `--first-flipper`, `--preassign` translate to URL params

**Docs**
- `docs/rules/banqi.md` — new "First-flip & colour assignment" section
- `docs/rules/banqi-house.md` — `PREASSIGN_COLORS` row

## Deferred
- Interactive multi-line "create room" form in the TUI lobby (`c` key still takes only room name; users configure via direct `--connect` URL with the new flags)
- ADR-0009 for URL-param-based room config (can ship separately)

## Stats
- 17 new tests, 0 regressions on the existing 32+ test files
- `cargo fmt --check`, `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings`, `cargo test --workspace`, and `cargo build --target wasm32-unknown-unknown -p chess-{core,web}` all clean

Try `cargo run -p chess-net -- --port 7878 banqi` then `cargo run -p chess-tui -- --connect 'ws://127.0.0.1:7878/ws/r?host_color=black&first_flipper=joiner'` to see the host configured as BLACK with the joiner forced to flip first.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   The user requested a banqi (暗棋) chess variant rule change in a Rust + WASM workspace at `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-05-06-chinese-chess`. Original request (Chinese): banqi should NOT pre-specify which color moves first because no piece has been flipped yet, so neither player knows their color. Only after the first person (host) flips does the player know "You play Red/Black" and turn order follows. Optionally, there should be a setting to pre-assign colors before flipping.
   
   The user then expanded scope: hosts should be able to preset on the host side whether host or joiner flips first for banqi, and for xiangqi the host's color (previously default host=Red) should also be configurable. Confirmed via AskUserQuestion: scope = both /lan/host (WebRTC) AND chess-net server lobby; banqi options = Either/Host/Joiner with default Either; xiangqi options = Red/Black/Random with default Red. User chose "Continue with P3+P4+P5 in this session" after seeing P1+P2+P6+P7 land.

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - Rust workspace with crates: chess-core (engine), chess-net (WS server + protocol), chess-tui (terminal client), chess-web (Leptos + Trunk + WASM browser client), chess-ai (stub)
   - Banqi (暗棋) — 4×8 face-down xiangqi variant where color is determined by first reveal
   - Taiwan rule: flipper plays the color they reveal (encoded in `banqi_side_assignment`)
   - HouseRules bitflag pattern (CHAIN_CAPTURE, DARK_CAPTURE, etc.)
   - PlayerView projection with `#[serde(default)]` for back-compat (no PROTOCOL_VERSION bump)
   - chess-net Room state machine with deterministic seat assignment
   - WebRTC offer/answer envelope (JSON wrapper around SDP)
   - Leptos signals (`create_rw_signal`, `Show`, `prop:checked`)
   - URL query params for room configuration (`?password=`, new `?host_color=`, `?first_flipper=`, `?preassign=1`)
   - `ChaCha8Rng` for deterministic random Side resolution

3. Files and Code Sections:
   
   - **`.claude/plans/banqi-move-graceful-thompson.md`** — Plan file with 7-phase implementation strategy. Critical for the structured approach.
   
   - **`crates/chess-core/src/rules/house.rs`** — Added new bit:

     ```rust
     const PREASSIGN_COLORS    = 1 << 6;
     ```

     ```rust
     #[serde(default)]
     pub banqi_first_mover_locked: bool,
     
     pub fn set_active_seat(&mut self, seat: Side) -> Result<(), CoreError> {
         self.turn_order.set_current(seat).ok_or(CoreError::Illegal("seat not in turn_order.seats"))?;
         self.side_to_move = seat;
         Ok(())
     }
     
     pub fn banqi_awaiting_first_flip(&self) -> bool {
         matches!(self.rules.variant, Variant::Banqi)
             && self.side_assignment.is_none()
             && !self.rules.house.contains(crate::rules::HouseRules::PREASSIGN_COLORS)
             && !self.banqi_first_mover_locked
     ```

     ```rust
     let banqi_awaiting_first_flip = state.banqi_awaiting_first_flip();
     ```

     Updated `next_seat()` to honor `host_seat`. Rematch path preserves config.
   
   - **`crates/chess-net/src/server.rs`** — Added `host_color`, `first_flipper`, `preassign` to `AuthQuery`. Added `parse_host_color`, `parse_first_flipper`, `parse_preassign`. Updated `upgrade_default`/`upgrade_room` and `handle_room_socket` to thread params through.
   
   - **`crates/chess-net/src/lib.rs`** — Re-exported `FirstFlipper`, `HostColor`, `RoomConfig`.
   
   - **`crates/chess-net/tests/banqi_first_flip.rs`** — New file with 5 integration tests: `black_seat_can_flip_first_under_default_banqi_rules`, `preassign_colors_rejects_blacks_first_reveal_attempt`, `host_color_black_swaps_seat_assignment`, `first_flipper_joiner_forces_joiner_to_flip`, `preassign_param_locks_red_first_for_banqi`. All pass.
   
   - **`clients/chess-tui/src/ui.rs`** — Added pre-first-flip sidebar text branch (CJK: "未翻牌 — 任一方皆可先翻"; ASCII: "Awaiting first flip — either side may flip") in both Net and Local game views.
   
   - **`clients/chess-tui/src/main.rs`** — Added CLI flags `--host-color`, `--first-flipper`, `--preassign` with `HostColorArg` and `FirstFlipperArg` enums. URL-encoding via `?host_color=...&first_flipper=...&preassign=1`.
   
   - **`clients/chess-web/src/host_room.rs`** — Added `HostRoom::with_config()` that calls `Room::with_config`.
   
   - **`clients/chess-web/src/pages/play.rs`** — Updated `role_label` and `turn_label` to show neutral pre-first-flip text; relaxed click guard:
     ```rust
     if v.observer != v.side_to_move && !v.banqi_awaiting_first_flip {
         return;
     }
     ```
   
   - **`clients/chess-web/src/pages/lan.rs`** — Added signals for `preassign`, `host_color`, `first_flipper`. Added "You play as" radio fieldset (Red/Black/Random). Added Banqi "First flip" fieldset (preassign checkbox + Either/Host/Joiner radios). Wired `HostRoom::with_config()`. Wired `encode_sdp_with_config()` to embed config in offer envelope. Added `LanJoinPage::offer_summary` that decodes meta and shows "Host has chosen: ..." panel.
   
   - **`clients/chess-web/src/components/sidebar.rs`** — Pre-first-flip turn_text/turn_class override.
   
   - **`clients/chess-web/src/transport/webrtc.rs`** — Added `encode_sdp_with_config()`, `decode_offer_meta()`, `OfferMeta`, `HostHandshake::current_offer_with_config()`.
   
   - **`clients/chess-web/src/routes.rs`** — Added `(HouseRules::PREASSIGN_COLORS, "preassign")` to `HOUSE_TOKENS`.
   
   - **`clients/chess-web/style.css`** — Added `.turn-awaiting` class.
   
   - **`docs/rules/banqi.md`** — Added "First-flip & colour assignment" section.
   
   - **`docs/rules/banqi-house.md`** — Added `PREASSIGN_COLORS` (bit `1 << 6`) row.

4. Errors and fixes:
   - **Test failure: `black_seat_can_flip_first_under_default_rules` got Side(1) when expecting Side(0).** Direct `state.side_to_move = Side::BLACK` didn't update `turn_order.current`, so make_move's advance went to wrong side. Fixed by adding `GameState::set_active_seat(seat)` and `TurnOrder::set_current(seat)` helpers, and using them in the test and chess-net `process_move`.
   - **Test assertion wrong: `first_flipper=joiner pre-commits side_to_move; sentinel must be off`** but engine still reported sentinel=true. The `banqi_awaiting_first_flip` definition only checked PREASSIGN_COLORS, not first_flipper preference. Fixed by adding `banqi_first_mover_locked: bool` field to GameState and including it in the sentinel check. Chess-net's `Room::with_config` sets this when first_flipper is Host/Joiner.
   - **Missing struct fields after adding banqi_first_mover_locked.** Errors in `setup.rs`, `snapshot.rs`, `state/zobrist.rs`, `tests/casual_xiangqi.rs`. Fixed by adding `banqi_first_mover_locked: false,` to all GameState struct literals (replace_all in setup.rs for the 3 build_* functions; manual edits elsewhere).
   - **Formatting violations** in `banqi_first_flip.rs` (long `serde_json::to_string(&ClientMsg::Move { mv: ... })?))?` lines). Fixed by `cargo fmt`.

5. Problem Solving:
   - Designed the engine layer to be presentation-free: `banqi_first_mover_locked` is a deployment concern but lives on GameState so the projection's gate logic stays in one place.
   - Avoided PROTOCOL_VERSION bump by using `#[serde(default)]` on the new `banqi_awaiting_first_flip` field.
   - Resolved Random side deterministically using `ChaCha8Rng::seed_from_u64(seed.wrapping_add(0xABCD))` against the banqi seed (or thread_rng if no seed).
   - WebRTC offer envelope extended with optional embedded RoomConfig fields; old v1 envelopes decode as `OfferMeta::default()` (all None).
   - Decided chess-tui interactive lobby create-room form is deferred — URL params + CLI flags provide the wire-level surface, and that's sufficient.

     ```rust
     if let Some(c) = cli.host_color {
     ```

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="Bash"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Bash**</summary>
Create commit with detailed message

```bash
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(banqi): defer colour until first flip; host can preset colour + first flipper

Banqi no longer pre-commits Red-to-move before any tile is flipped. By default
either seat may make the first reveal; the Taiwan rule (`banqi_side_assignment`)
then maps the flipper to the revealed colour. The legacy behaviour is opt-in via
the new `HouseRules::PREASSIGN_COLORS` bit (`1 << 6`).

Hosts can pre-configure rooms over both deployments (chess-net WS via URL params,
WebRTC `/lan/host` via the SDP offer envelope):

- `host_color`: red | black | random (default red) — applies to xiangqi and
  banqi-with-PREASSIGN_COLORS; resolved deterministically from the room seed for
  `random`.
- `first_flipper`: either | host | joiner (default either) — banqi-only,
  ignored when PREASSIGN_COLORS is on.

Wire-level surface: `?host_color=…&first_flipper=…&preassign=1` on chess-net
WS URLs and chess-tui `--connect`; `RoomConfig` embedded in the WebRTC offer
envelope (old envelopes decode as default config). `PlayerView` gains a
`#[serde(default)] banqi_awaiting_first_flip` so its `legal_moves` gate exposes
reveals to both observers pre-first-flip — no protocol version bump.

UI:
- chess-tui sidebar shows "未翻牌 — 任一方皆可先翻" / "Awaiting first flip —
  either side may flip" (Local + Net) and new CLI flags `--host-color`,
  `--first-flipper`, `--preassign`.
- chess-web `/lan/host` exposes "You play as" + "First flip" pickers; the
  joiner sees the host's choices in a yellow summary before accepting.
- chess-web `/play` sidebar mirrors the neutral copy; click guard relaxed so
  either seat can submit the first reveal (server is authoritative).

Tests: 8 new engine unit tests (`banqi_first_flip_color.rs`) plus 5 new
chess-net integration tests (`banqi_first_flip.rs`). All workspace tests green;
fmt + clippy -D warnings + wasm builds clean.

Deferred: interactive multi-line "create room" form in the chess-tui lobby
(URL params + CLI flags cover the wire surface); ADR for URL-param room config.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
```
